title: Acelgas o Espinacas (Chard or Spinach)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chard or spinach, cooked
- butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- two tablespoons of sugar
- pepper
- salt
- milk
- lemon juice
- bread slices, fried in butter

## Directions

1. After the chard has been cooked, grind it well and fry it in a little butter with one tablespoon of flour, two tablespoons of sugar, a little pepper and salt
2. Add milk so that it boils and becomes a thick sauce
3. When it is cooked, add a little lemon juice
4. On top place some slices of bread fried in butter
5. For spinach, do not add sugar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Acelgas o Espinacas (Chard or Spinach)
body:
## Ingredients

- chard or spinach
- butter
- flour
- sugar
- pepper
- salt
- milk
- lemon acid
- bread fried in butter

## Directions

1. After cooking the chard, grind well and fry in a little butter with a spoonful of flour, two of sugar, a little pepper and salt.
2. Add milk so it boils and forms a thick sauce.
3. When cooked, add a little lemon juice.
4. Top with slices of bread fried in butter.
5. For spinach, do not add sugar.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ajo (Garlic Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- garlic
- butter
- white wine
- water
- bread crumbs
- lemon juice
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. After roasting and removing from shells, put in a pot with a piece of butter, white wine and water, bread crumbs, lemon juice and a little nutmeg
2. Bring to boil
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Alcachofas a la Pebre (Artichokes with Pepper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- small artichokes
- vinegar
- oil
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Choose the smallest artichokes
2. Cut them into quarters
3. Remove the outer leaves and cut the upper part
4. Cook them in cold water with a sauce of vinegar, oil, salt and pepper
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Alcachofas a la Salsa Blanca (Artichokes with White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- water
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and cook the artichokes
2. Place them in cold water
3. Reheat them at serving time, placing them in boiling water after removing the outer part and interior
4. Place them on a plate, pouring white sauce into the hollow of the heart
5. The sauce can also be served separately in a sauce boat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Alcachofas Guisadas (Braised Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- ground meat
- sausages
- shallots
- celery
- parsley
- aromatics
- onion
- breadcrumbs
- oil

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves and cut off the top with a knife
2. Round them well and remove the heart from the middle
3. Prepare a filling composed of ground meat, sausages, shallots, celery, parsley, all kinds of aromatics, onion and breadcrumbs, all finely chopped
4. Fill the artichokes, arrange them in a dish, cover each one with a little oil and the remaining filling
5. Blanch them before filling
6. Cook them covered in steam
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Alcachofas Rellenas (Stuffed Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salt
- lard
- parsley
- oil
- breadcrumbs
- ground meat
- egg yolks
- hazelnut sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves and boil them once in salted water
2. Place them on a board to drain well
3. Have lard in a pot, and after filling the artichokes with a mixture of chopped parsley, oil and grated breadcrumbs or very finely ground meat, place them in it on low heat
4. Cover the pot with an iron lid with coals until they brown
5. If desired with sauce, pass them through a beaten mixture of egg yolks and add a hazelnut sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Alhoja (Herbal Sorbet)
body:
## Ingredients

- Culen wood shavings
- Guindo wood shavings
- A little quinoa
- Sugar
- Allspice
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Toasted corn kernels

## Directions

1. Scrape some culen wood sticks and others of guindo and with a little quinoa put them to boil.
2. Remove from the fire, add as much sugar as desired, clarify it, give it a short boil and strain.
3. When it is warm, pour it into a calabash with allspice, cloves and cinnamon and some toasted corn kernels.
4. Quinoa can be replaced with corn.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Almendrado (Almond Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of almonds
- milk
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- sugar
- one large tablespoon of ground and sifted rice
- four egg yolks

## Directions

1. Reduce half pound of almonds to paste, place in a napkin, pour boiling milk on top and squeeze to make the milk come out
2. Put to boil with some lemon peel, cinnamon, sugar and one large tablespoon well full of ground and sifted rice, not stopping to stir so the rice does not settle
3. When it has boiled well and is thick, beat four egg yolks a little, add to the milk and let it boil
4. A little later, pour into a serving dish and sprinkle cinnamon on top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Anguila Mechada (Larded Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- thin bacon

## Directions

1. Lard with thin bacon throughout the length of its back
2. Tie it with string or twine
3. Arrange it in a circle shape to place in the brazier
4. Serve afterwards with all kinds of sauces
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Anguilas a la Tarta (Eels in Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- eels
- salt
- water
- breadcrumbs
- egg yolks
- butter

## Directions

1. After well prepared, open and cut into pieces
2. Cook in little water with a bit of salt
3. When cold, remove all the water
4. Coat in grated breadcrumbs
5. Then in two beaten egg yolks mixed with melted butter
6. Coat again in grated breadcrumbs
7. Let them take a little color on the grill and serve with a sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Anguilas en Salsa (Eels in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- eels
- oil
- paper
- butter
- fine herbs
- breadcrumbs
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Place on the grill wrapped in paper coated with oil
2. Must be divided along the back at the time of serving
3. Can be filled with a paste made of butter, fine herbs and breadcrumbs
4. Can also be made into a white sauce stew, served in a sauce boat separately
5. The eels in another plate, so each person can season to their taste
6. Lemon juice is very acceptable for this dish
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Anguilas Guisadas (Stewed Eels)
body:
## Ingredients

- eels
- parsley
- pepper
- clove
- saffron
- cinnamon
- water
- salt
- pine nuts or hazelnuts
- bread

## Directions

1. Cut into pieces; after washing, put in a pot
2. Add chopped parsley, pepper, clove, saffron and cinnamon
3. Cook with water and salt
4. As they cook, make a sauce of pine nuts or hazelnuts with soaked breadcrumbs
5. Let this sauce boil a couple of times, stirring everything well so it does not burn
6. Season in proportion
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Apio Entero en Guisado (Whole Celery in Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- salt
- butter
- flour
- puchero broth

## Directions

1. When peeling the celery root, cut it equal in thickness and length
2. Wash with great care and blanch in lightly salted water
3. Refresh and drain well
4. Make a light paste of butter and flour, which dissolve in puchero broth, stirring continuously
5. When it is boiling add the celery without breaking it and cook on high heat for about half an hour
6. Serve covered with sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Arroz a la Valenciana (Valencian Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- oil or butter
- garlic
- rice
- salt
- pepper
- saffron powder
- water or broth

## Directions

1. Heat oil or butter and add a clove of garlic, remove it when it begins to burn
2. Add rice and toast it
3. Mix in the salt, pepper and saffron powder for coloring
4. When the oil has been absorbed by the rice and the grains begin to toast, pour water or broth over it, which immediately begins to boil
5. After the rice grains have puffed up and acquired their full size, consuming the broth in which they were boiling, remove from heat and let rest for half an hour, placing hot coals on top of the pot lid which brown the upper part of the rice
6. The merit of rice à la Valenciana consists in the grains being soft but dry at the same time and separated from each other
7. To the rice can be added all kinds of meat, poultry and fish which should be added moments before adding the broth, after the rice has been browned, taking advantage of leftovers of poultry, ham and meats from the previous day
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Arroz con Pimientos (Rice with Peppers)
body:
## Ingredients

- oil
- onion
- rice
- whole peppers
- water
- salt
- pepper
- saffron

## Directions

1. Fry a little oil and onion, and when well sautéed add the rice
2. Sauté also a little and then place the whole peppers
3. Fill the pot with water, and season with salt, a little pepper and saffron
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Arroz en Tortilla Soplada (Rice in Puffed Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of rice, clean and washed
- milk
- sugar
- salt
- six egg yolks
- orange blossom water
- lemon zest
- butter, about half an ounce
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook the rice with the appropriate amount of milk, sugar and a little salt
2. After well cooked, pass it through a metal strainer
3. Put the puree in a pot with six egg yolks, a little orange blossom water, lemon zest and a little butter, about half an ounce
4. Beat the egg whites well and add to the rice
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Arvejas (Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- salt
- flour
- butter
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook peas with salt
2. Make a light sauce of flour and butter
3. Add the peas and if desired add a bit of sugar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Arvejas en Mantequilla (Peas in Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- salt
- egg yolks
- butter

## Directions

1. After cooking them with salt, beat them with some egg yolks
2. Add a little butter and add the peas
3. Cook on low heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Aves Arrolladas (Rolled Poultry)
body:
## Ingredients

- poultry
- garlic
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- oregano
- thin strips of bacon
- lamb or beef meat
- cloth
- string

## Directions

1. Freshly killed birds, pluck without scalding in hot water so the skin does not soften
2. Begin to remove the skin with a fine knife, starting from the tip of the backbone near the neck and making a cut from this part to the rump
3. Remove the crop from inside to avoid tearing the front
4. Remove the bones so the legs remain boneless without breaking them; the wings are not deboned as they give shape to the birds
5. Place the bird meat, thin strips of bacon and lamb or beef meat in a marinade of garlic, vinegar, salt, pepper and oregano to increase the filling
6. Leave there the necessary time for it to marinate well
7. Then spread the bird skin on a table and arrange the meat and bacon on it
8. After completing this operation, pound it so it unites
9. Wrap, starting from the rump, then roll with fine string, wrap in cloth and cook in boiling water seasoned with salt and vinegar from the same in which the bird has been, being careful to turn it when it is boiling
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bacalao a la Francesa (Cod French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- onion
- parsley
- lemon
- pepper
- oil
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. After the cod is cooked, take the serving dish, which should be somewhat deep
2. Put in it a bit of onion, parsley, lemon slices without skin or seed
3. Peppercorns, two spoonfuls of oil and a piece of butter, if beef better than pork, the size of an egg
4. Place the cod on top and cover with a layer of the same mixture, sprinkle with breadcrumbs
5. Then place the dish over low heat so it boils little by little
6. Add a bit of lemon juice and pepper on top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bacalao con Papas a lo Canario (Cod with Potatoes Canary Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- potatoes
- oil
- water
- parsley
- pepper
- saffron
- bread

## Directions

1. Clean and desalt the cod
2. Fry in oil until golden on both sides
3. Remove it, and in the same oil fry the peeled and cut potatoes
4. Add the cod with the potatoes and a pitcher of water, bringing it to a boil
5. When everything is cooked, take a bit of parsley, pepper, a few strands of saffron and a soaked breadcrumb
6. Dissolve all this in the cod broth and add to the stew
7. Season, and when it boils once, remove from heat and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bacalao con Papas de Otro Modo (Cod with Potatoes Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- potatoes
- parsley
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Fry the cod and then sauté in potatoes
2. Add water, a bit of parsley and vinegar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bacalao en Albóndigas (Cod in Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- bread
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- eggs
- fish broth
- oil
- saffron

## Directions

1. Chop the cod after cooking and mix with breadcrumbs, salt, pepper and parsley, all ground and beaten eggs in proportion so it takes a regular consistency
2. After well mixed, make balls, wrap in breadcrumb and fry
3. Can afterwards be stewed with fish broth and oil, a piece of bread mixed with parsley, pepper and a bit of saffron, scattered over the meatballs
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bacalao en Blanco (Cod in White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- butter
- nutmeg
- flour
- pepper
- milk

## Directions

1. After well cleaned, cook it; when about to boil, skim
2. Remove from fire, cover well, leaving it a quarter hour, then remove from water and drain
3. Put in a pot a bit of butter, nutmeg, flour and pepper
4. Dissolve everything in a bit of milk and add the cod afterwards so it takes on the taste of this stew and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bavarois de Almendra (Almond Bavarois)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces almonds
- 2 glasses milk
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass
- 1 plate cream

## Directions

1. Peel 4 ounces almonds in hot water and place in cold water to blanch
2. Dry in a napkin and grind in a mortar, adding a little water to prevent them from turning to oil
3. Add 2 glasses of milk that has been boiled with 1/2 pound sugar
4. Let infuse until cool and pass through a napkin, pressing well to extract all the almond juice
5. Add 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass and when it begins to thicken, add 1 plate of cream and freeze as with the others
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bavarois de Café (Coffee Bavarois)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces whole roasted coffee
- 2 glasses boiling milk
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass
- 1 plate whipped cream

## Directions

1. Take 4 ounces of whole roasted coffee and pour 2 glasses of boiling milk over it
2. Cover the infusion and when cold, pass through a napkin
3. Add 1/2 pound sugar and 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass
4. Pass a second time through a napkin
5. Let thicken and add 1 plate of whipped cream
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bavarois de Damasco (Apricot Bavarois)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen ripe apricots
- 1/2 pound powdered sugar
- milk
- 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass
- 1 plate whipped cream

## Directions

1. Take 2 dozen of the best ripe apricots and remove the pits
2. Place in a bowl with 1/2 pound powdered sugar
3. After two hours, pass through a sieve
4. Add a little milk and 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass
5. When it begins to curdle, add 1 plate of whipped cream
6. Finish as with the previous recipes
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bavarois de Frutillas (Strawberry Bavarois)
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 strawberries
- 1/2 pound powdered sugar
- 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Remove hulls from strawberries and pass through a sieve
2. Add 1/2 pound powdered sugar and 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass
3. Bring to a boil and when the preparation begins to thicken, let cool and pour into a mold
4. Place mold in snow for two hours
5. Beat 6 egg whites as for meringues and add to the mixture before molding
6. To serve, place mold in a pan with warm water that can be held comfortably by hand, then quickly turn out onto the serving plate
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bavarois de Grosella (Currant Bavarois)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound currants
- some raspberries
- 10 ounces powdered sugar
- 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass
- 1 plate whipped cream

## Directions

1. Take 1 pound currants and some raspberries
2. Place in a bowl and break apart with a wooden spoon
3. Add 10 ounces powdered sugar
4. Let sit for a couple of hours and pass everything through a sieve
5. Add 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass and when it begins to thicken, add 1 plate of whipped cream
6. Finish as with the previous recipes
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bavarois de Melón (Melon Bavarois)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ripe yellow sweet melon
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass
- 1 plate cream

## Directions

1. Choose a ripe yellow sweet melon
2. Cut into thin slices and remove seeds and rind
3. Cut into small pieces and place in a pan with 1/2 pound sugar
4. Boil until it becomes a jam and pass through a sieve
5. Add 1/2 ounce clarified isinglass and mix well
6. When it begins to take consistency, add 1 plate of cream
7. Pour into mold and place in snow
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Beef Meatballs (Albóndigas)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ground meat from cooked beef
- Breadcrumbs
- Milk
- Spices
- Flour or breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. To make more delicate meatballs, soak breadcrumbs in hot milk
2. Mix with the ground meat until forming a homogeneous paste
3. Taste to check if it has sufficient spices
4. Form balls, roll them in flour or breadcrumbs
5. Fry them
6. These meatballs can be kept several days and eaten on grills or reheated with a white sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Beefsteak (Bifteck)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef steak
- Oil
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the meat the day before eating it
2. It should be clean and without nerves, beaten until it takes a square or round shape
3. Two hours before grilling, coat well with a mixture of raw oil and a little salt
4. Place on the grill over strong fire, turning it twice
5. Collect carefully the juice that comes from the meat and with a little melted butter dissolved in it, baste each portion when serving
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Beefsteak with Onions (Bifteck con Cebollas)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beefsteak
- Grease
- Salt
- Onion

## Directions

1. After preparing the meat, place it in a pan with burned grease, salt and finely chopped onion
2. Let it brown
3. Add the sauce and let it reduce somewhat on the fire
4. Remove and serve on a plate
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Besitos (Little Kisses)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of flour
- Four ounces of sugar
- Four ounces of butter
- A little milk

## Directions

1. Knead the dough for half an hour
2. Bake in a gentle oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bifteck Apañado (Prepared Beefsteak)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- melted butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the loin into slices
2. Pass through melted butter
3. Wrap well in breadcrumbs
4. Roast on the grill
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bifteck con Salsa de Perejil (Beefsteak with Parsley Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- beefsteak
- handful of parsley
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Roast the beefsteak on a grill
2. When it is done on one side, turn it
3. Grind a handful of parsley until it becomes a paste
4. Mix it with a piece of butter and the juice of a lemon
5. When placing the beefsteak on a platter, spread the sauce on the other side
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bifteck Rebozado (Breaded Beefsteak)
body:
## Ingredients

- good slices of loin
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- fat

## Directions

1. Cut good slices of loin and pass them through beaten egg
2. Roll them in breadcrumbs
3. Fry in very hot fat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Birutas (Meringue Candies)
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg white
- Lemon zest and juice
- Ground sugar as needed

## Directions

1. Beat the egg white
2. Add the lemon zest and juice
3. Add ground sugar until forming a thick dough
4. Place on wafers, cut into pieces and dry with gentle heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bizcochitos de Huevos (Egg Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty egg yolks, well beaten
- Four ounces of sugar
- Four ounces of unsalted butter
- Flour as needed

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks well
2. Add the sugar and butter
3. Add flour as necessary to form a soft dough
4. Knead well
5. Cut the biscuits and bake
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bizcochos (Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve egg yolks
- Six egg whites
- Half a pound of sugar
- Half a pound of beaten butter
- Half a cup of milk
- Flour as needed

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks, whites, and sugar well
2. Add the beaten butter and milk
3. Mix all together with sufficient flour
4. Knead well
5. Cut into squares and bake
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bizcochos de Leche (Milk Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of flour
- Four ounces of washed butter
- Four ounces of sugar
- Milk

## Directions

1. Mix all together using milk instead of water
2. Knead for about half an hour
3. Cut the biscuits and bake
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bizcochos Hors-Achos (Sponge Biscuits with Wine Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- One dozen eggs
- Nine ounces of ground sugar
- One pound of flour
- One and a half pounds of sugar for syrup
- One large glass of sweet wine
- Ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with nine ounces of ground sugar
2. Add one pound of flour and fill large boxes made of paper
3. Bake in a mild oven
4. Make a syrup from one and a half pounds of sugar
5. When ready, add a large glass of sweet wine and bring to caramel point
6. When the biscuits are removed, let them cool a little, cut into pieces and with two forks soak in the wine syrup until well soaked
7. Sprinkle a little ground cinnamon and air dry in wicker baskets
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bizcochos para Enfermos (Biscuits for the Sick)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of flour
- Six ounces of burned fat
- Two ounces of sugar dissolved in more than tepid water
- A little salt

## Directions

1. Mix all together and knead well
2. Cut and bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bizcochuelo de Leche (Milk Sponge Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- About two bottles of milk
- One quarter of a half of burned fat
- Flour as needed
- Fourteen eggs

## Directions

1. Mix the milk with the quarter part of a half of burned fat
2. Bring to a boil and add handfuls of flour until forming a not very hard dough
3. Boil and stir constantly until it separates from the pot or pan
4. Empty into a dish or bowl and when lukewarm, add fourteen eggs that will be incorporated with the dough
5. The fat for frying should not be too inert; when adding to fry it is good to wet the fingers with cold water so the batter does not stick
6. Strike them so they split and when removed already fried, place a little fire underneath so they do not dampen
7. Pass through syrup or honey raised to point to make the figure desired
8. When already lukewarm, knead into small pieces and like little rolls, have very fine powdered sugar on hand to take in the hands each figure made so it does not stick to the fingers, and afterwards pick with very fine tongs
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bollos (Rolls with Almonds)
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve ounces of flour
- Half a pound of powdered sugar
- About a large nut of butter
- A little salt
- Lemon zest
- Eight whole eggs
- Blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Mix all together; if not enough to moisten, add one more white
2. The dough should be hard
3. Beat with the wooden spoon to soften
4. Cut the rolls long, placing some split almonds that are flattened with the wooden spoon
5. Butter the baking sheets and bake
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bollos (Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of flour
- Half a pound of refined powdered sugar
- Four ounces of butter
- Seven egg yolks
- Half a cup of milk
- Three pinches of salt
- Three pinches of anise
- A little cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks as for sponge cake
2. Mix all ingredients until in state to knead
3. If too soft, add more flour
4. Knead well, cut and bake in not very hot oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Bonitos (Delights)
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg yolks with five egg whites
- half pound of sugar
- four ounces of butter
- a little anise water broken
- a little orange blossom water
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Beat six egg yolks with five egg whites like sponge cake, half pound of sugar, four ounces of butter, a little anise water, a little orange blossom water and enough flour to make a very soft dough
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Breaded Beefsteak (Bifteck Apañado)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sirloin
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the sirloin into slices
2. Pass them through melted butter
3. Wrap well in breadcrumbs
4. Grill them
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Breaded Beefsteak (Bifteck Rebozado)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sirloin slices
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Grease

## Directions

1. Cut good slices of sirloin
2. Pass them through beaten egg
3. Roll them in breadcrumbs
4. Fry them in very hot grease
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Breaded Lamb Feet (Patitas Rebozadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb feet
- Eggs
- Sugar
- Cream of milk
- Broth
- Onion
- Hard-boiled egg

## Directions

1. Cook the lamb feet
2. Roll them in beaten egg and fry them
3. Make a light cream with three egg yolks and a little sugar
4. When serving, unite the lamb feet with the sauce
5. Alternatively, serve with broth with chopped onion and hard-boiled egg
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Breaded Leg of Lamb (Pierna Guisada)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb leg
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- potatoes
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the leg as for a stew
2. After cooking, cut into large pieces
3. Roll in melted butter, then in salt, ground pepper and bread crumbs
4. Arrange in a dish
5. Add plenty of the sauce in which it cooked
6. Add cooked potatoes cut into slices and hard-boiled eggs also cut
7. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and remaining butter
8. Place in the oven to brown
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Brochetas (Kidney and Bacon Skewers)
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of bacon
- raw kidneys
- melted butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut thin slices of bacon and raw kidneys
2. Thread alternating slices of kidney and bacon onto a stick
3. Coat thoroughly with melted butter and roll in breadcrumbs
4. Place on a grill in the oven to roast
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Almendras (Almond Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups of thick orgeat
- Cinnamon
- Lemon zest
- A piece of butter
- Six egg yolks
- Two egg whites

## Directions

1. Make two cups of thick orgeat and sweeten to taste
2. Add a little cinnamon, lemon zest, and a piece of butter
3. Thicken the orgeat with six yolks and two whites
4. Mix everything together and pour into a mold lined with butter and bread crumbs
5. Place in the oven
6. Serve with a wine or foam sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Arroz (Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked rice
- milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- raisins
- cognac
- six egg yolks
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook rice in milk and season with sugar, cinnamon, raisins, cognac and six yolks
2. Pour into a mold lined with butter and breadcrumbs
3. Place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Arroz Cocido (Cooked Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Three tablespoons of rice
- Milk
- Four egg whites
- Two egg yolks
- Sugar to taste
- Flavorings as desired
- Butter or caramel

## Directions

1. Cook three tablespoons of rice in milk over low heat
2. Add four whites and two yolks of eggs, sugar to taste and desired flavorings
3. Pour into a mold prepared with caramel or butter
4. Place the mold in a pan of boiling water, cover with fire on top, and let boil for one hour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Camote (Sweet Potato Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two well-cooked sweet potatoes
- A little milk
- Three or four tablespoons of flour
- Twelve eggs, yolks and whites separated
- Cinnamon
- Lemon zest
- Sugar to taste
- Butter
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Strain the cooked sweet potatoes with a little milk
2. Add three or four tablespoons of flour
3. Beat twelve eggs, yolks and whites separately, and mix together with the sweet potato
4. Add a little cinnamon, lemon zest, and sugar to taste
5. Prepare the pudding mold with butter and bread crumbs
6. Place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Chocolate (Chocolate Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of peeled almonds
- Half a pound of sugar
- Water for syrup
- Lemon zest
- One egg
- Four egg whites
- Five egg yolks
- Three ounces of ground chocolate
- Two tablespoons of cinnamon
- Butter without salt
- Flour

## Directions

1. Peel half a pound of almonds and dry well with a cloth
2. Make a light syrup from half a pound of sugar with necessary water, stirring always in one direction
3. Pour the syrup into a cup and cool slightly
4. Add the almonds and lemon zest
5. Add one egg and beat well
6. Continue until four whites and five yolks are incorporated and beaten until thickened
7. Add three ounces of ground chocolate and two tablespoons of cinnamon
8. Line the mold with unsalted butter and dust with flour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Chocolate Segundo (Second Chocolate Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Six ounces of powdered sugar
- Eight eggs
- Four ounces of peeled and finely chopped almonds
- Four ounces of shredded chocolate
- Two ounces of fine flour
- Butter
- Cream sauce

## Directions

1. Beat eight yolks with sugar as for sponge cake
2. When the mixture is well thickened, beat eight whites separately as for meringue
3. Unite the two mixtures and quickly add the flour, almonds, and chocolate
4. Stir and immediately place in a mold lined with butter
5. Bake in a not very hot oven
6. Make a cream sauce to serve hot
7. It is inadvisable for the oven to be hot before beginning the beating
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Chuno (Potato Starch Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- thick chuño made in milk
- sugar
- a small piece of butter
- lemon zest
- three eggs with egg whites
- spices

## Directions

1. Make a thick chuño in milk and pour it into a cup to cool
2. Add sugar, a small piece of butter, and lemon zest
3. When cold, beat three eggs with egg whites
4. Mix with the chuño, adding some spices
5. Place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Espuma (Foam Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four ounces of butter
- Half a bottle of milk
- Eight ounces of flour
- Ten egg yolks, beaten
- Cinnamon
- Sugar
- Lemon zest
- Egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. Boil all ingredients together, stirring always in the same direction, until it detaches from the pan
2. Let cool for a moment
3. Add ten beaten egg yolks, cinnamon, sugar, and lemon zest, all to taste
4. At the time of pouring into the pudding mold, add the beaten egg whites
5. Place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Eva (Eve's Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one teacup of cold bread crumbs
- three finely chopped apples
- four ounces of very finely chopped fat
- sugar to taste
- four eggs, well beaten
- half a nutmeg

## Directions

1. Fill one teacup with cold bread crumbs
2. Chop finely three apples and four ounces of fat
3. Add sugar to taste and four well-beaten eggs
4. Add half a nutmeg and mix everything
5. Place in a buttered cup
6. Moisten a cloth with hot water, place the cup in it, wring out, tie, and boil upside down for several hours
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Harina (Flour Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Seven eggs
- Four ounces of butter
- Four ounces of sugar
- Four ounces of flour
- One and a half bottles of milk
- Cinnamon
- Lemon zest

## Directions

1. Boil the milk with the butter and gradually add the flour, stirring constantly until the mixture detaches well from the pan
2. When the mixture is cool, add the seven beaten yolks, four ounces of sugar, lemon zest, and a little cinnamon
3. Mix well and add the beaten egg whites
4. Stir again and pour into a buttered pudding mold lined with bread crumbs
5. Place in the oven
6. Can also be made with semolina or rice flour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Limón (Lemon Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lemon
- four ounces of sponge cake, grated
- four ounces of liquid butter
- juice of one lemon
- four eggs
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Peel one lemon, removing the skin as thinly as possible and chop it well
2. Grate four ounces of sponge cake and add four ounces of liquid butter
3. Add the juice of one lemon, four eggs, and sugar to taste
4. Mix everything well and place in a dish in the oven or in pastries
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Manzanas (Apple Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Clove
- Twelve egg yolks
- Twelve egg whites
- Butter

## Directions

1. Peel apples and cook with sugar, a little cinnamon, and clove
2. After cooking, let cool
3. Beat twelve yolks lightly
4. Separately, beat twelve whites as for meringue
5. Incorporate everything together
6. Line the pudding mold with butter
7. Pour in the beaten mixture and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Pan (Bread Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little bread without crust
- cold milk
- a little butter
- three eggs with egg whites
- sugar
- spices

## Directions

1. Soak a little bread without crust in cold milk
2. When well soaked, mix it with a spoon and add a little butter
3. Beat three eggs with egg whites and add sugar and spices
4. Mix everything well and place in a buttered dish
5. Bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Pan de Cerveza (Beer Bread Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- beer bread
- milk
- eight egg yolks
- five egg whites
- four ounces powdered sugar
- four ounces ground almond
- lemon zest
- cinnamon
- raisins
- half ounce butter

## Directions

1. Soak a beer bread in milk for two hours and pass it through a sieve
2. Beat eight yolks with five whites and four ounces powdered sugar
3. Add four ounces ground almond, lemon zest, cinnamon, raisins, and half ounce butter
4. Mix everything together with the bread and pour into a mold prepared with butter and breadcrumbs
5. Place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Papas (Potato Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of potatoes with skin
- Half a pound of melted butter
- One glass of cream
- Six whole eggs
- Half a pound of ground sugar
- One glass of white wine
- A little salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook two pounds of potatoes with skins
2. Mash in half a pound of melted butter, mixing well
3. Add one glass of cream
4. Beat six whole eggs and add
5. Add half a pound of ground sugar, one glass of white wine, a little salt, nutmeg, and other seasonings
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Papas Segundo (Potato Pudding, Second)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of potatoes
- four ounces of butter
- six ounces of powdered sugar
- lemon zest and juice
- four eggs

## Directions

1. Mash half a pound of potatoes
2. Melt four ounces of butter
3. Add six ounces of powdered sugar and lemon zest and juice
4. Mix everything well with four eggs
5. Butter the dish and place it in the oven for half an hour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Papas Tercero (Potato Pudding, Third)
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- six ounces of sugar
- one large tablespoon of flour
- three large tablespoons of ground potatoes
- some raisins

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar
2. Add the potatoes and flour
3. Last, fold in the beaten egg whites
4. Place everything in a dish coated with butter
5. Bake in the oven and serve with wine sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Pascua (Easter Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of cold bread crumbs, well broken up
- six ounces of finely chopped fat
- two pounds of small potatoes and one of large potatoes
- eight eggs
- one small cup of cognac
- candied lemon and orange peel
- four finely chopped apples
- a little corn
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Take one cup of cold bread crumbs, well broken up
2. Add six ounces of finely chopped fat
3. Add two pounds of small potatoes and one large potato
4. Add eight eggs, one small cup of cognac, candied lemon and orange peel, four finely chopped apples, a little corn, cinnamon, nutmeg, and sugar to taste
5. Place in cloth and boil for six hours
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Tapioca (Tapioca Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Five tablespoons of tapioca
- Milk
- Five whole eggs
- Sugar to taste
- Nutmeg
- Cinnamon
- Lemon zest
- A small glass of white wine
- A small teaspoon of butter

## Directions

1. Soak five tablespoons of tapioca in milk
2. Cook until thick
3. Beat five whole eggs separately with sugar to taste, nutmeg, cinnamon, lemon zest, a small glass of white wine, and a small teaspoon of butter
4. Prepare the mold and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín de Tostadas (Toast Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beer bread
- Butter
- Preserved fruit in syrup, preferably quince
- Five egg yolks
- Two egg whites
- Sugar
- Milk

## Directions

1. Cut beer bread into slices and butter half of them
2. Arrange in a pudding mold prepared like the previous ones
3. Alternate layers of buttered toast with preserved fruit in syrup
4. Beat five yolks and two whites, add necessary sugar and enough milk to well cover the toast
5. Pour the beaten mixture over the toast and let soak for two hours
6. Place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín en Fuente (Pudding in Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve eggs, half with whites
- Two pounds of sugar
- Cinnamon
- Two reales of milk

## Directions

1. Beat twelve eggs, half with whites and two pounds of sugar, until thick
2. Add a little cinnamon
3. Make cream with two reales of milk and mix with the beaten mixture
4. Place in the oven, sprinkling sugar on top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín Frito de Vainilla (Fried Vanilla Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bottle milk
- five ounces sugar
- vanilla
- lemon zest
- cinnamon
- seven ounces cornstarch
- water
- seven egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put milk, sugar, vanilla, lemon zest and cinnamon on the fire and cook slowly until it boils
2. Strain and return to the fire, adding seven ounces cornstarch dissolved in water little by little
3. Stir until it boils for five minutes
4. Let cool and add seven well-beaten yolks
5. Place on the fire for a moment without stopping to stir and pour into a pudding mold to cool
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Budín Sencillo (Simple Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of raisins
- Half a pound of flour
- Half a pound of sugar
- Half a pound of suet, trimmed and finely chopped
- Four whole eggs
- One glass of white wine
- A little cognac
- Lemon peel
- Various spices
- Sufficient milk to moisten

## Directions

1. Combine raisins, flour, sugar, suet, whole eggs, white wine, cognac, lemon peel, and spices
2. Add sufficient milk to moisten
3. Wrap in a napkin
4. Place in boiling water for six hours
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Buñuelos (Fried Pastry Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a half bottles of water
- Four ounces of butter
- Two ounces of sugar
- Zest of one lemon
- A little cinnamon
- Twelve ounces of flour
- Eight eggs
- Four egg yolks

## Directions

1. Bring water to boil in a pot with butter, sugar, lemon zest, and cinnamon
2. When boiling, add flour little by little and stir constantly until the dough separates from the pot
3. Pour into a spread dish to cool
4. When lukewarm, pass to a cup or bowl to incorporate eight eggs and four yolks
5. When well incorporated, fry the puffs in very hot lard and place in a dish that has heat from below so they do not dampen
6. These puffs can be passed through syrup at point, or if you wish to serve with ground sugar, add five ounces of sugar instead of two to the water
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Caballeros Pobres (Poor Knights)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread (beer bread)
- milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- four eggs
- a little flour
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Cut beer bread into slices and soak overnight in milk seasoned with sugar and cinnamon
2. When ready to fry, make a batter with four eggs and a little flour
3. Pass the bread slices through the batter
4. Fry in butter or lard
5. Top with sugar or honey
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Caballeros Pobres (Poor Nobles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beer bread
- Milk
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Eggs
- Flour
- Butter
- Fat
- Sugar or honey

## Directions

1. Cut a loaf of beer bread into slices and soak them from the night before in milk seasoned with sugar and cinnamon
2. At the time of frying, make a beaten mixture of four eggs with a little flour
3. Pass the slices through it and fry in butter or fat
4. Sprinkle with sugar or bee honey on top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Cabezas de Cordero o Cabrito (Lamb or Kid Heads)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb or kid heads
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolk
- bacon
- lemon juice
- butter
- twine
- paper

## Directions

1. Split heads in half and stuff with a filling prepared as follows: chop some parsley and add salt, pepper, an egg yolk and bacon, sauté the mixture in a pan with butter
2. Once the heads are filled, coat the outside with lemon juice and butter, then tie with twine and wrap in paper
3. Place on the spit and leave until tender
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Cajitas de Sesos (Brain Cases)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brains
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook the brains, cut them into slices and arrange them in paper cases with a piece of butter, salt, pepper and plenty of bread crumbs
2. Place them in the oven to brown
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Caldo de Gallina (Hen Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- One hen
- Two and a half liters of water

## Directions

1. Scald the hen's feet after plucking.
2. Cook in two and a half liters of water.
3. Let cook over low heat until reduced to two liters.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Caldo de Pollo (Chicken Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a chicken without fat
- One liter of water
- Lettuce leaves
- Chervil
- Salt to taste

## Directions

1. Place half a chicken without fat to boil in one liter of water for one hour.
2. Add lettuce leaves and chervil.
3. Season with salt.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Caldo de Sustancia (Meat Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a veal leg
- A piece of beef
- Some poultry
- Stock from puchero
- Whole cloves

## Directions

1. Place half a veal leg, a piece of beef and some poultry to cook with stock from puchero.
2. Stir and skim carefully, adding more stock and whole cloves.
3. Cook over low heat for five hours.
4. Pass through a sieve.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Caldo Hecho en una Hora (Broth Made in One Hour)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of beef or veal, cut into small pieces
- Onion
- Bacon
- Half a glass of water
- Salt to taste
- Boiling water

## Directions

1. Cut one pound of beef or veal into small pieces.
2. Brown in a pot with onion, bacon, and half a glass of water until it begins to stick.
3. Add boiling water and season with salt.
4. Cook for three-quarters of an hour.
5. Strain through a sieve.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Capón con Ostras (Capon with Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- One capon
- Water and salt for cooking
- A good piece of butter
- One tablespoon of flour
- Whole pepper
- Oyster broth
- Oysters
- Three eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the capon in water and salt
2. In a saucepan put a good piece of butter, one tablespoon of flour and whole pepper
3. Add the oyster broth
4. At meal time add the oysters and three eggs and bring to a light boil
5. Be careful to stir frequently so it does not stick
6. The sauce should be of regular thickness; if it is too thick, thin it with the broth in which the capon was cooked
7. Place the capon on a platter and pour the sauce over it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Carbonada in Broth
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- water
- salt
- burnt fat
- potatoes
- squash
- green beans
- peas
- cabbage
- seasonings
- egg

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into small pieces and put it to cook in a regular amount of water with salt
2. When it boils, skim it
3. Remove the meat and fry it in burnt fat
4. Dice potatoes, squash, green beans and peas if available, or cabbage, fry all this with the meat
5. Add the same broth and cook again
6. Add all the seasonings desired and let it rest for one or more hours
7. At the time of serving, season with egg
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Cardo Guisado (Braised Cardoon)
body:
## Ingredients

- cardoon
- salt
- water
- pine nuts
- hazelnuts
- oil
- broth

## Directions

1. Clean and remove the strings and membranes
2. Cut it, placing the pieces in cold water so they do not blacken
3. Card them and cook with salt
4. Then drain them and add a sauce of pine nuts and hazelnuts, drizzling oil over the broth
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Cardo Guisado (Stewed Cardoon)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cardoon
- Salt
- Pine nuts
- Hazelnuts
- Raw oil
- Water

## Directions

1. Clean the cardoon and remove the strings and membranes
2. Cut into pieces and place in cold water so they do not blacken
3. Blanch and cook with salt
4. Drain and add a sauce of pine nuts and hazelnuts
5. Drizzle raw oil over the broth and bring to a boil a couple of times
6. Alternatively, serve the cardoon dressed after it has been cooked
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Carlota Rusa (Russian Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 to 1.5 dozen egg yolks
- 1 pound sugar
- 1 ounce isinglass
- 1 large bottle of milk
- egg whites beaten like meringue
- vanilla or orange blossom water
- sponge cake crumbs

## Directions

1. Put everything (egg yolks, sugar, isinglass, and milk) in a saucepan and place over regular heat
2. Stir continuously with a wooden spoon
3. When it begins to stick to the spoon, remove from heat and cool a little
4. Pour from one pan to another so it does not curdle
5. Mix the beaten egg whites like for meringues with the milk when already almost cold
6. Add vanilla or orange blossom water
7. Place some sponge cake crumbs on the bottom and sides of the mold, then pour the mixture
8. When solid, remove from the mold by first immersing it in cold water
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Carlota Rusa (Russian Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a mold of milk
- 4 ounces sugar
- a piece of vanilla
- 1 ounce isinglass
- 6 egg yolks
- egg whites well beaten
- sponge cake toasts for lining

## Directions

1. Measure half a mold of milk and bring to a boil with 4 ounces sugar
2. When boiling, add a piece of vanilla
3. When tepid, add 1 ounce of isinglass and stir until it dissolves
4. Add 6 egg yolks beaten slightly and return to heat until it boils
5. Pour this over the well-beaten egg whites and mix everything well
6. Pour into the mold, which should be prepared with sponge cake toasts arranged around it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Carne de Ternera en Jalea Frita (Veal in Fried Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- butter
- wine
- onion
- cloves
- pepper
- bay leaves
- veal feet

## Directions

1. Lard the meat very finely and leave it with a bottle of wine overnight
2. The next day add a piece of butter to a saucepan until it begins to brown
3. Then add the meat with onion, cloves, pepper, bay leaves, veal feet and the wine in which the meat has been soaked
4. Cover the saucepan and place over low heat, being careful that it does not burn
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Carnero Guisado (Stewed Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon
- onion
- leg meat
- salt
- pepper
- warm water
- parsley
- mint
- lettuce heart
- soaked pine nuts
- spices
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Fry bacon cut in dice-like pieces with some finely chopped onion
2. Cut the meat into pieces and place everything in a pot with salt and pepper
3. Reheat over gentle heat and then add warm water
4. When cooked, add parsley, a mint leaf, a lettuce heart, soaked pine nuts, and spices, all finely chopped
5. When serving, add a little lemon juice
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Cartuchos Rellenos (Filled Cornucopias)
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty-four egg yolks
- Two egg whites
- One pound of powdered sugar
- Half a bottle of must
- Twenty-two ounces of flour
- One quarter ounce of ground cinnamon
- Wax
- Paper strips
- Wafer paste
- Three pounds of powdered sugar for filling
- Fifteen egg whites for filling

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks with two whites and one pound of powdered sugar
2. When beaten, it takes a white color; add half a bottle of must and twenty-two ounces of flour and one quarter ounce of ground cinnamon
3. Continue beating until all is well incorporated
4. To make the cartuchos, use an iron mold for making wafers
5. Heat the iron on moderate fire so they should not be toasted
6. Coat the iron with a little wax and wipe with a cloth
7. Put a small spoonful of dough on each end of the mold and press to form two wafers
8. Place on fire for a moment, separate the wafer with a knife, and on the same iron wrap the cartucho around the mold
9. Before filling, have cut narrow strips of paper glued with wafers, form rings to place on the cartuchos so they do not break apart with the moisture of the filling
10. To fill them, beat three pounds of powdered sugar with fifteen egg whites until the beaten mixture takes consistency
11. Fill the cartuchos with this beaten mixture and place on baking sheets in a gentle oven
12. To glaze them, make syrup from one pound of sugar; when at point, take each cartucho by the middle and glaze
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Cazuela of Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- water
- burnt fat
- potatoes
- rice
- green beans
- peas
- 2 heads of onions
- 1 head of garlic
- egg
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Pluck the chicken well, gut it, wash it and cut it into pieces
2. Put it to cook in a regular amount of water
3. When it is boiling, skim well so the broth remains clean and clear
4. Remove the pieces and fry them in burnt fat
5. Add seasonings and diced potatoes, a little rice, green beans and peas if available, 2 heads of onions and 1 head of garlic
6. After this operation, add the broth in which the chicken has boiled and cook over low heat until dinner time
7. Before serving, season with beaten egg and vegetables
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Cazuela of Lamb
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb meat
- water
- garlic
- cumin
- pepper
- potatoes
- rice
- green beans
- peas
- egg

## Directions

1. Wash the meat and put it to cook
2. When well skimmed, remove the broth and fry the meat in color with ground garlic, cumin and a few grains of pepper
3. Add the broth to the pot, add potatoes, rice, green beans and peas
4. Before serving at table, season with beaten egg
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Cebollas Asadas (Roasted Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- Onions
- Oil
- Sugar
- Ground pepper
- Cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Cut both ends off the onions and remove the outer layers
2. Place in a pan with oil, a little sugar, a grain of ground pepper, and a little cinnamon powder
3. Roast on low heat
4. Cover the pan with an iron lid and place some coals on top until they are well browned
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Cebollas Rellenas (Stuffed Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ground lean meat
- Fat bacon
- Parsley
- Fried onion
- Breadcrumbs
- Salt
- Pine nuts
- Spices
- Grated cheese

## Directions

1. Make a mixture of ground lean meat, fat bacon, parsley, fried onion, breadcrumbs, salt, pine nuts, spices, and grated cheese
2. Remove the inner layers of the onions with a knife and stuff them
3. Place in a pot without pressing them down
4. Cook and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Champús de Leche (Corn and Milk Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quarter of corn kernels (mote maiz)
- 2 reales of milk
- 2 pounds sugar
- 1 pound ground rice
- a little rice cooked with very fine cinnamon
- half a pound of peeled almonds, cut very finely
- cinnamon and almonds or whole corn for topping

## Directions

1. Take the corn kernels and boil until they begin to fall apart
2. Drain the water in which they were cooked
3. Without letting it cool, add to the milk with the sugar
4. Once the milk has boiled, add the ground rice so that it becomes as smooth as possible
5. Once it comes to its first boil, place on low heat so it boils slowly until it is heated through
6. Add a little rice cooked with very fine cinnamon
7. Shortly before removing from heat, add the finely cut peeled almonds
8. Pour into a serving dish and top with cinnamon and almonds or whole corn
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chanchito Asado (Roasted Suckling Pig)
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- water
- oil
- flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Place in a pot of water slightly warmer than lukewarm
2. After breaking the tusks, rub with the hand to remove the bristles, putting it in and taking it out of the water several times until all the hair disappears
3. Remove the hooves
4. Remove the belly being careful not to remove the kidneys
5. Make some cuts in the neck and other larger ones on the back
6. Introduce the tail into the skin and flesh
7. Gather the legs and shoulders by means of two iron skewers passed one through the thigh and the other through the chest
8. Place immediately for twenty-four hours in cold water
9. Hang and let it dry
10. Cover the belly with flour mixed with lard and put on the spit
11. While on the fire, brush with good oil using feathers
12. After roasting, make other incisions in the neck
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chanchito en Salsa (Suckling Pig in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- lard
- water or broth
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- egg yolks
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut into larger pieces
2. After roasting, pass through lard and moisten with water or broth
3. Add salt, pepper and a bay leaf
4. Make a liaison of egg yolks at the time of serving and add lemon juice
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chanchito Relleno en Asador (Stuffed Suckling Pig on Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- liver
- bacon
- truffles
- mushrooms
- capers
- anchovies
- fine herbs
- pepper
- salt
- bread crumbs
- butter
- cream or milk
- bread crumbs
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. After scalding and preparing like roasted suckling pig, fill with its own liver chopped, removing the bile, bacon, truffles, mushrooms, capers, anchovies, fine herbs seasoned with pepper and salt, bread crumbs toasted on the fire in cream or milk, a bit of butter, bread crumbs crushed and dissolved in three egg yolks
2. Fill the belly of the suckling pig with this mixture
3. Tie it, put on the spit and do the same as with the aforementioned
4. Serve accompanied with pebre sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chanfaina (Lamb Offal Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb liver
- lamb lungs
- cooked lamb blood
- butter
- onions
- spices
- parsley, chopped
- paprika
- water
- flour
- eggs, beaten (optional)

## Directions

1. Take pieces of liver, lungs and cooked blood
2. Sauté in butter
3. Add onions, spices, chopped parsley and paprika
4. After everything is well sautéed, add water and cook
5. Mix flour dissolved in water when it is boiling and add to the chanfaina to thicken the broth
6. If desired, a beaten egg mixture can be added
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chaucho en Salsa (Pork in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork pieces
- lard
- water or broth
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- egg yolks
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the pork into larger pieces
2. After roasting, pass it through lard
3. Moisten with water or broth and add salt, pepper, and a bay leaf
4. Make a liaison of egg yolks and add lemon juice at the time of serving
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Cheesecake de Manzanas (Apple Cheesecake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces apples
- 3 ounces butter
- 4 ounces ground sugar
- juice and peel of half a lemon
- 6 to 8 chopped bitter almonds
- 4 eggs, removing the white from 2 of them

## Directions

1. Chop the apples very finely
2. Combine all ingredients well
3. Place in small pastries
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chicos Kuchen (Small German Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ounces unpeeled almonds, ground not very finely
- 12 ounces ground sugar
- half an ounce of fine cinnamon
- the peel of a candied lemon
- 4 ounces butter
- half an ounce of nutmeg
- 5 beaten egg whites
- a little clove

## Directions

1. Mix everything together
2. Spread on wafers
3. Place in a pastry oven for half an hour with heat from below, but not very strong
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chicos Kuchen de Harina (Small Flour Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- half a tablespoon of cinnamon
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Make a thick dough with the butter, egg yolks, sugar, cinnamon and precise flour
2. Form small round rolls
3. Place on paper greased with butter and grated sponge cake
4. Top the rolls with beaten egg whites
5. Bake in a pastry oven with heat from below
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Choros con Salsa (Mussels with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- parsley
- salt
- bread
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. After washing well, cook in water with parsley and salt
2. Remove from shells and place each mussel on a very thin slice of bread
3. Season the mussel broth with beaten egg yolks and plenty of chopped parsley until it becomes a little thick
4. Place the mussels on a serving dish and pour the sauce over them
5. Serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Choros de Oro (Golden Threads)
body:
## Ingredients

- egg white
- one tablespoon of flour per egg white
- one tablespoon of powdered sugar per egg white
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. For each egg white put one tablespoon of flour and one tablespoon of powdered sugar
2. Mix these ingredients well
3. When the butter for frying is hot, put one tablespoon of this mixture in a funnel that has three tubes the thickness of a grain of wheat, and let it fall into the butter, turning the funnel to form circles the size of a tea cup opening
4. When golden, remove with a skimmer and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chuletas Apañadas (Breaded Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- chops
- oil or melted butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare the chops and soak in oil or melted butter
2. Roll in breadcrumbs and place on the grill
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chuletas de Carnero con Salsa de Tomates (Mutton Chops with Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- bread
- oil
- tomatoes

## Directions

1. After preparing and roasting as described previously, place on a platter
2. Place fried bread toasts in each interval
3. Sprinkle with a sauce made from tomatoes passed through a sieve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chuletas de Carnero en Legumbres (Mutton Chops with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- butter
- parsley
- cloves
- white wine
- ham
- legumes

## Directions

1. Reheat chops in butter with parsley and cloves
2. Add white wine and a small piece of ham
3. After cooking, remove the chops; let the sauce thicken
4. Use the sauce to dress the vegetables that have been prepared beforehand, and arrange the chops in a crown shape on top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chuletas de Chancho en Parrilla (Pork Chops on Grill)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork chops
- salt
- piquant sauce with gherkins

## Directions

1. Cut the chops like veal chops, leaving some fat
2. Flatten them giving them a good shape
3. Sprinkle with salt and place on the grill
4. Serve with a piquant sauce with gherkins
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chuletas de Chancho en Parrilla (Pork Chops on the Grill)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork chops
- salt
- peppery sauce with pickles

## Directions

1. Cut the chops like veal chops, leaving some fat
2. Flatten them and give them a good shape
3. Sprinkle with salt and place on the grill
4. Serve with a peppery sauce with pickles
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chuletas de Cordero con Pan Rayado y en Parrilla (Lamb Chops with Grated Bread and on Grill)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops
- hot butter
- egg yolks
- clear sauce
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pass through very hot butter without allowing them to brown
2. Remove and let the butter cool slightly, then mix with egg yolks
3. Soak the chops in this sauce, then place on the grill and roast over gentle heat
4. Serve with clear sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chuletas Trasparentes (Transparent Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- rib meat
- grated bread
- parsley
- pepper
- 2 hard eggs
- egg white
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Remove meat from the ribs and arrange in the shape of a chop
2. Prepare some grated bread, parsley, pepper and 2 hard eggs according to the amount of meat
3. Wrap the chops in this preparation and then pass through a lightly beaten egg white
4. Fry in butter or not very hot fat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chupe de Lima (Lima Chowder)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- onion
- garlic
- oregano
- potatoes
- curd
- raw shrimp
- eggs

## Directions

1. Put a pot on the fire with a little butter, chopped onion, a little garlic and a little oregano, fry a little
2. Then add raw potatoes cut in half, fry a little more
3. Add a large quantity of water and let the potatoes boil until well cooked
4. Add a little dissolved curd
5. Add raw shrimp to cook with the potatoes
6. When everything is well cooked, add two beaten eggs
7. When these are cooked, add six or eight whole eggs on top of the stew
8. Can be made with fresh silverside fish, oysters or fresh lobsters
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Chupe de Lima (Lima Chupe Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- onion
- garlic
- oregano
- potatoes
- water
- cheese curds
- shrimp

## Directions

1. Put a pot on the fire with a bit of butter, chopped onion, a little garlic and a bit of oregano
2. Fry a bit and then add raw potatoes halved
3. Fry a bit more, add a large amount of water
4. Let the potatoes boil until well cooked
5. Then add a bit of cheese curds dissolved
6. Add raw shrimp so they cook with the potatoes
7. When everything is very well cooked
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Cocido (Boiled Meat and Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- chickpeas
- beef or mutton
- ham
- bacon
- blood sausage
- salt
- chicken
- meat scraps

## Directions

1. In a pot proportionate to the food to be cooked, put water and when hot add chickpeas and well-washed, well-skinned meat, adding for each pound of beef or mutton half a pound of chickpeas
2. When it begins to boil, skim, being careful not to overdo it so as not to deprive the cocido of its substance
3. After two hours, a little ham, bacon and small blood sausage can be added
4. Let everything boil over low heat, seasoning with salt and adding warm water from time to time
5. If half a chicken, meat scraps, etc. are added to this mixture, with such a simple and easy method the best that exists in ordinary cocido will be achieved
6. The broth serves to improve all stews in which a substance is needed without resorting to water
7. The vegetables are cooked separately with bacon or chorizo
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Colas de Carnero (Mutton Tails)
body:
## Ingredients

- laurel leaves
- bacon
- onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- white wine
- broth
- lettuce
- chicory

## Directions

1. Prepare a pot with laurel leaves, bacon, onion, parsley, salt, pepper, a glass of white wine and the same amount of broth
2. Add the mutton tail and let it cook over gentle heat
3. When done, remove and let it drain
4. Serve with lettuce and chicory
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Coles Rebozadas (Breaded Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- water
- salt
- batter for coating
- parsley sauce

## Directions

1. Cook a cabbage for about a quarter of an hour
2. Place it afterwards in cold water
3. Remove the leaves one by one and bread them
4. Then replace as if it were whole, fastening it with a cord or thread
5. Cook it, and at serving time squeeze it, placing it on a plate, and on top pour a parsley sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Coliflor a la Peltre (Cauliflower with Bacon and Flour)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Bacon
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pass the cauliflower with bacon, butter, and a little flour
2. Add a little broth, salt, pepper, and nutmeg
3. Place on low heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Coliflor a la Peltre (Cauliflower with Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- bacon
- butter
- flour
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Fry it with bacon and butter and a little flour
2. Add a little broth, salt, pepper and nutmeg to this
3. Cook on low heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Compota de Cerezas o Frutillas (Cherry or Strawberry Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherries or strawberries
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the cherries
2. Cook with water and sugar in proportion to the quantity being made
3. Once cooked, place in a compote dish
4. Pour the syrup over them and serve cold
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Compota de Ciruelas (Plum Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of plums
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of plums with a little water and sugar until they soften when pressed with a finger
2. Place in a compote dish
3. If the syrup is not the right consistency, boil until it thickens
4. Pour the syrup over the plums
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Compota de Damasco (Apricot Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots
- Sugar for syrup
- Water

## Directions

1. Make a syrup
2. Before it thickens, add peeled apricots, whole or halved
3. Let them cook and remove the foam
4. Remove from heat and arrange in a compote dish
5. Bring the syrup to the proper consistency and pour over the fruit
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Compota de Manzanas o Duraznos (Apple or Peach Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples or peaches
- Cold water
- Lemon peel
- Cinnamon sticks
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the fruits and remove the core without damaging them
2. Place in cold water to prevent browning
3. Cook with a glass of cold water, lemon peel, cinnamon sticks, and sugar
4. When the fruit is cooked, remove and place in a compote dish
5. Return the syrup to the heat and reduce it to a jelly
6. Pour into a plate and let cool
7. Once cold, dip the plate in boiling water to loosen the jelly
8. Cover the fruit with this jelly and serve cold
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Compota de Membrillo (Quince Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- Cold water
- Sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Cook the quinces until half done
2. Remove and place in cold water
3. Cut into four pieces and peel carefully, removing the core
4. Make a syrup, removing all foam that forms
5. Add the quinces to finish cooking
6. Serve cold with little syrup
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Conejo en Fricasé (Rabbit Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- beef lard
- bacon
- flour

## Directions

1. Skin, gut and clean the rabbit, then cut into pieces.
2. Heat a good piece of fresh beef lard in a pan, through which pass square pieces of bacon.
3. Remove immediately, put two spoonfuls of flour in the lard and knead.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Conejo en Papel (Rabbit in Paper)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- lard
- fine herbs
- rabbit liver
- egg yolks
- bacon
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit into pieces and cook in lard.
2. Place in paper packets with a filling containing fine herbs, the liver of the same rabbit and a couple of egg yolks for consistency.
3. Cover the intervals in the packet with this filling, then cover all with strips of bacon and paper spread with butter.
4. Prepared this way, place in the oven and serve with an appropriate sauce.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Conejo en Parrilla (Grilled Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- butter
- fine herbs or anchovy butter

## Directions

1. Open lengthwise after gutting and flatten with the flat of the knife.
2. Place on the grill, surrounded by a sheet of paper spread with butter.
3. When done, remove the paper and serve mixed with fine herbs or with anchovy butter if available.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Conejo en Yerbas Finas (Rabbit with Fine Herbs)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- lard
- mushrooms
- parsley
- bay leaf
- flour
- wine
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- rabbit liver

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit meat into pieces and sauté in lard with mushrooms, parsley and chopped bay leaf.
2. Add a spoonful of flour, wine and broth in equal parts.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. When serving, add the liver which has also been cooked, and mix with the sauce.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Conejo Frito (Fried Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- white wine
- lemon juice
- thyme
- bay leaf
- salt
- pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut into pieces and place in a marinade of white wine.
2. Add lemon juice, thyme, bay leaf, salt and pepper.
3. After two hours, drain and coat with flour.
4. Should be served with an appropriate sauce.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Conejo Guisado (Stewed Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- raw oil
- parsley
- pepper
- hot water
- bay leaf
- clove
- cinnamon powder
- capers or lemon slices

## Directions

1. After skinning and cleaning without wetting it, cut into pieces.
2. Place in a pot or pan with raw oil, parsley and pepper to stew over gentle heat.
3. Add hot water and when half cooked, add bay leaf, clove and cinnamon powder.
4. Very good with capers or lemon slices.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Congrio con Aceite o Vinagre (Conger with Oil or Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- conger
- water
- salt
- garlic
- pepper
- flour
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook in salted water and let the broth reduce
2. Prepare a sauce of roasted garlic and a bit of pepper thickened with flour
3. Add to the conger and at the same time of serving sprinkle with vinegar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Consuelos del Cura (Priest's Comforts)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 whole egg
- 4 ounces powdered sugar
- a little lemon zest
- 3 ounces melted butter
- 1 glass of rum
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Beat the whole egg with powdered sugar and lemon zest
2. Add the melted butter and rum
3. Add sufficient flour to make a white dough
4. Knead and extend with a rolling pin until the thickness of a finger
5. Cut the dough into small squares with a rounded cutter
6. Fry in not very hot lard
7. Serve dusted with powdered sugar and may be placed in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Corderas en Salsa de Perejil (Lamb in Parsley Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb loin
- parsley
- potatoes or bread
- color
- broth

## Directions

1. Cook the lamb loin and make the sauce in this broth
2. Grind plenty of parsley with potatoes or soaked bread, fry in a little color and then combine with the broth and meat pieces
3. Leave to make a delicate sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Corvina Cocida (Cooked Corvina)
body:
## Ingredients

- corvina
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the whole corvina with salt and some aromatics
2. When cooked, remove the bones and place on a fish platter where it will be adorned with some sauce or salad
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Corvina o Congrio Cocido (Cooked Corvina or Conger)
body:
## Ingredients

- corvina or conger
- onion
- tomatoes
- corn
- aromatics
- broth
- oil

## Directions

1. Put in an earthenware dish, covered, chopped onion, tomatoes and corn and all kinds of aromatics
2. Then add the fish divided into pieces
3. Cover with another layer of the same mixture, cover well and cook
4. If it dries too much, add broth until it finishes cooking
5. At the time of serving, if desired, add oil
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Corvina Rellena (Stuffed Corvina)
body:
## Ingredients

- corvina
- onion
- tomato
- hard boiled egg
- celery
- parsley
- water
- egg

## Directions

1. Chop onion and tomato very finely, fry well in color
2. Add chopped hard boiled egg, a bit of celery and parsley, well seasoned
3. Layer this with raw corvina in a dish
4. Add a bit of water; when ready to bring to table, add a beaten egg
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Costeletas (Lamb Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb ribs meat
- mashed potatoes
- 3 eggs
- parsley
- celery
- pepper
- rib bone

## Directions

1. Scrape the meat from lamb ribs and fry it
2. Pound it and mix with as much mashed potato as meat
3. Add 3 eggs, parsley, celery and pepper, mix well
4. Place the cleaned small bone in the middle giving it the shape of a cutlet
5. Roll in egg, breadcrumbs and fry
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Costillas Asadas (Roasted Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork ribs
- lard
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- bread crumbs
- flour
- white wine
- pickles

## Directions

1. Cut, prepare and flatten the ribs and place in the roaster with a little lard
2. While roasting, cover them with breadcrumbs mixed with salt, pepper and fine herbs
3. Remove when done, adding grated bread, a little flour and a glass of white wine
4. Let it reduce a little and add pickles cut into slices
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Costillas de Chancho (Pork Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork ribs
- broth
- thyme
- mint
- parsley
- aromatic herbs
- salt
- pepper
- lard
- mushrooms
- flour
- white wine
- onions
- cloves

## Directions

1. Cook the ribs with broth and a bouquet of thyme, mint, parsley and other aromatic herbs, salt and pepper
2. Place lard, mushrooms and flour in a pot
3. Brown with broth, white wine, parsley, onions and cloves
4. After cooking, pour the sauce over the ribs and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Costillas de Cordero o Ternera (Lamb or Veal Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- ribs
- 2 tablespoons fat
- chopped onion
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- rice
- oil
- bacon
- onion head
- saffron
- broth

## Directions

1. Place well-washed ribs in a pot with 2 tablespoons fat, some chopped onion, parsley, pepper and salt
2. Cover the pot and place over heat
3. Separately, fry some rice with oil; add some bacon, an onion head cut in pieces, a little saffron, pepper, parsley and broth added gradually so it does not dry out
4. When serving, combine everything together
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Costillas de Ternera (Veal Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal ribs
- butter
- cheese
- grated bread
- parsley
- pepper
- onion head

## Directions

1. Cut the ribs into pieces and place in a pot
2. Add a little butter, cheese and grated bread, parsley, pepper and an onion head cut in pieces
3. Place over gentle heat
