source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Costritas (Small Crusts)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- 2 egg whites
- 4 ounces sugar
- 4 ounces burnt fat
- 1 glass of white wine
- cinnamon
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the sugar
2. When well thickened, gradually add the hot fat little by little
3. Add cinnamon, wine and sufficient flour to make a white dough
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Crema a la Vainilla (Vanilla Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- One vanilla bean
- Four glasses of boiled milk
- Half a pound of sugar
- A grain of salt
- Eight egg yolks
- Half an ounce of clarified isinglass

## Directions

1. Cut a vanilla bean into small pieces
2. Add to four glasses of boiled milk and leave on the fire for a quarter hour
3. Add half a pound of sugar and a grain of salt
4. Cover the milk so it does not lose its perfume and when lukewarm, strain through a cloth
5. Pour the milk over eight egg yolks and mix well with a spoon
6. Cook over gentle heat, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon
7. When the cream is thick and begins to stick to the spoon, pass through a strainer
8. When cooled, mix with half an ounce of clarified isinglass
9. When the cream is cold, pour into a mold like gelatin
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Crema Asada de Trimag (Baked Trimag Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Just over one bottle of milk
- Six eggs
- A little nutmeg
- Lemon zest
- A little cognac
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Beat six eggs and add to just over one bottle of milk
2. Add a little nutmeg, lemon zest, a little cognac, and sugar to taste
3. Mix everything and pour into a mold
4. Cook with very gentle heat and also with heat from above
5. Ensure it does not boil or it will curdle
6. As soon as it is set, remove from the fire and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Crema de Almendras (Almond Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four ounces of almonds
- Two glasses of milk
- Half a pound of sugar
- Isinglass

## Directions

1. Place four ounces of almonds in boiling water until the skin can be removed
2. Place in cold water so they do not become oily and grind with a little sugar
3. Add to two glasses of milk that have been boiled with half a pound of sugar
4. Let the infusion cool, strain through a cloth and add isinglass as in the previous recipes
5. When it begins to thicken, add snow
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Crema de Avellana al Baño de María (Hazelnut Cream Bain-Marie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four ounces of hazelnuts
- Four glasses of milk
- Half a pound of sugar
- A grain of salt
- Eight egg yolks

## Directions

1. Toast four ounces of hazelnuts until golden
2. Grind them in a mortar
3. Heat four glasses of milk with half a pound of sugar and a grain of salt
4. Add the ground hazelnuts to the hot milk
5. When the infusion is cold, strain through a cloth
6. Mix with eight beaten egg yolks
7. Pass everything through a strainer and pour into a mold as in the previous recipe
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Crema de Café al Baño de María (Coffee Cream Bain-Marie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four ounces of roasted coffee
- Four glasses of milk
- Eight egg yolks
- Half a pound of sugar
- A grain of salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Add four ounces of roasted coffee to four glasses of boiling milk
2. Cover the infusion and when cold, strain through a cloth
3. Beat eight egg yolks and mix with a spoon
4. Add half a pound of sugar and a grain of salt
5. Mix everything well and pass through a strainer
6. Pour the cream into a mold greased with butter
7. Place the mold in a pan and cook over very gentle heat, ensuring the water does not boil
8. It is done when it is firm to the touch
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Crema de Frutillas (Strawberry Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four glasses of milk
- One tablespoon of cochineal grains
- Ten ounces of sugar
- A grain of salt
- Eight egg yolks
- One pound of ripe red strawberries

## Directions

1. Boil four glasses of milk with one tablespoon of cochineal grains, ten ounces of sugar, and a grain of salt
2. Pour over eight egg yolks and mix well with a spoon
3. Cook the cream, stirring continuously until it begins to thicken, then strain through a colander
4. Pass one pound of ripe red strawberries through a sieve
5. When the cream is cold, mix with the strawberries and pour into the mold
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Crema de Marrasquino de Italia (Italian Maraschino Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- four glasses of milk
- half a pound of sugar
- a grain of salt
- eight egg yolks
- half an ounce of clarified isinglass
- half a glass of Italian maraschino

## Directions

1. Bring four glasses of milk to a boil
2. Add half a pound of sugar and a grain of salt
3. Dissolve eight egg yolks with a spoon and add them to the milk, stirring to mix well
4. Heat until it begins to thicken and pass through a strainer
5. Let cool
6. Add half an ounce of clarified isinglass that is only lukewarm at the moment of pouring into the mold, which should be when it is cold
7. Add half a glass of Italian maraschino
8. Place in ice and remove as customary
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Crema de Marrasquino de Italia (Italian Maraschino Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four glasses of milk
- Half a pound of sugar
- A grain of salt
- Eight egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil four glasses of milk
2. Add half a pound of sugar and a grain of salt
3. Beat eight egg yolks with a spoon and add the milk, stirring to mix well
4. Cook and when it begins to
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Crema de Piña a la Flombière (Pineapple Cream à la Flombière)
body:
## Ingredients

- One small pineapple
- Syrup
- Six egg yolks
- One tablespoon of rice flour
- One glass of milk
- Two ounces of sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the pineapple and cut into small pieces
2. Add to the syrup and boil until it becomes a jam
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Mix six egg yolks with one tablespoon of rice flour, one glass of milk, and two ounces of sugar
5. Cook over gentle heat, stirring continuously until it begins to thicken
6. Remove from fire and beat well with a spoon
7. Return to heat for a few minutes, then transfer to another pan
8. Allow to cool and place in the snow
9. When ready, place the jam on a plate of whipped cream and serve in small cups placed again in the snow
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Crema de Té al Baño de María (Tea Cream Bain-Marie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four tablespoons of tea
- Four glasses of milk
- Two ounces of sugar
- A little boiling water
- Eight egg yolks
- Half a pound of powdered sugar
- A grain of salt

## Directions

1. Add four tablespoons of tea to four glasses of boiling milk
2. Cover the infusion so it does not lose its perfume and when cold, strain through a cloth
3. Make a caramel with two ounces of sugar and a little boiling water
4. Add the caramel to the infusion
5. Mix with eight egg yolks that have been beaten with half a pound of powdered sugar and a grain of salt
6. Mix everything and pass through a strainer
7. Pour into a mold and place in a bain-marie
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Crema Helada de Limón (Frozen Lemon Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar
- Lemon zest
- Four glasses of milk
- Half a pound of sugar
- Twelve bitter almonds, ground
- Eight egg yolks
- Two ounces of butter
- Salt and saltpeter

## Directions

1. Scrape the zest of a fresh lemon onto a piece of sugar
2. Boil four glasses of milk and add the sugar with lemon zest and half a pound more sugar
3. Add twelve ground bitter almonds
4. Cover the infusion and when lukewarm, pour over eight beaten egg yolks
5. Mix well and cook over gentle heat, stirring constantly when it begins to thicken
6. As soon as it boils, remove from fire and add two ounces of butter
7. Strain through a colander
8. When cold, place in a freezer set in snow with salt and a little saltpeter
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Croquetas de Gallina u Otra Carne Cocida (Chicken or Other Cooked Meat Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked meat, fried
- A little butter
- One tablespoon of flour
- One glass of milk
- Aromatics (herbs)
- Beaten egg
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Chop the cooked meat well, which should be fried
2. Put it in the pan with a little butter
3. When somewhat fried, add one tablespoon of flour, stir constantly so it does not burn
4. Add one glass of milk for it to cook, add aromatics
5. When everything is well cooked and buttery, let it cool
6. Then form little balls the size of an egg, roll them in beaten egg and then in bread crumbs
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Despojos o Menudillos de Cordero (Lamb Offal)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb head
- liver
- lungs
- feet
- heart
- salt
- spicy sauce or preferred sauce
- 2 egg yolks
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the jaws and snout from the head
2. Cut the offal into slices, drain of blood and wash well with hot water
3. Place in a pot and cook with salt
4. Just before serving, drain the offal and, except for the head, add a spicy sauce or preferred sauce with 2 beaten egg yolks, a little butter and lemon juice
5. Place the dish in a platter with the head in the middle
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dry Carbonada
body:
## Ingredients

- raw beef
- potatoes
- squash
- corn
- green beans
- wine
- vinegar
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Mince raw beef and fry it a little
2. Once fried, add diced potatoes, squash, corn and green beans
3. Calculate equal amounts of meat and vegetables
4. Add 2 spoonfuls of wine, 1 of vinegar and all kinds of seasonings
5. Leave to cook over low heat until dinner time
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Acallóte (Candied Acallóte)
body:
## Ingredients

- Acallóte
- 1 pound 4 ounces sugar per 1 pound acallóte
- Chopped almond

## Directions

1. Cook the acallóte in water and strain it in a basket.
2. Weigh 1 pound 4 ounces of sugar for every 1 pound of acallóte.
3. Make the syrup with the sugar and add the acallóte before it reaches point; stir constantly so it does not stick and add a little bit of chopped almond.
4. Remove when it reaches point.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Camote (Candied Sweet Potato)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet potato
- Sugar in equal quantity to the sweet potato

## Directions

1. Cook the sweet potato and pass it through a sieve.
2. Weigh an equal quantity of sugar to the sweet potato.
3. Make a syrup and mix it with the sweet potato.
4. Stir well and it is done when the bottom of the pan becomes visible.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Cascos de Sandías (Candied Watermelon Rind)
body:
## Ingredients

- Watermelon rinds
- 1.5 pounds sugar per 1 pound watermelon rind
- Almond water

## Directions

1. Peel the watermelon rinds and place them in the sun for half a day.
2. Give them whatever shape you desire.
3. Soak them for one day in warm almond water.
4. Place them to cook in boiling water, remove them and drain in cold water until they lose the salt.
5. Strain them in a basket and weigh 1.5 pounds of sugar for every 1 pound of watermelon.
6. When the syrup is at medium point and cooled, add the rinds and bring to point over low fire.
7. If you want to make them candied, remove them from the syrup to dry; heat them and pass them through syrup at caramel point.
8. Melon rinds are prepared the same way.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Cidras, Zamboas o Limas (Citron, Pomelo, or Lime Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- Citrons, pomelos, or limes
- Salt
- Water
- Sugar, one and a half libras per one libra of fruit

## Directions

1. Peel them, place to cook in boiling water with a handful of salt, and when very tender, remove.
2. Drain in cold water until they lose the bitterness, squeeze in a basket and then weigh them; for one libra of fruit use one and a half libras of sugar.
3. Make this in syrup at half point and let cool.
4. Add the fruit, bring to point in three boils.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Ciruelas (Plum Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums
- Salt
- Water
- Sugar, five libras per one hundred plums

## Directions

1. Using a thick thorn or spine, make six punctures: three on the back and three on the other side in the groove that runs along the plum.
2. Make a brine with boiling water and pour it over the plums in a dry earthenware pot.
3. Cover well to retain heat; leave in the brine until they release the gum, which usually occurs in three or four days.
4. With a thorn, remove the gum and place the plums to drain in tepid water, changing the water one or two times until they lose the acidity and salty taste.
5. Bring water to a boil in a saucepan and when boiling, place the plums there to cook.
6. Test with a small feather to know if they are well cooked and place them in distilled water, testing again to ensure they have neither acidity nor salty taste.
7. It is not necessary to drain them; simply wash and place to drip in a cloth set over a basket.
8. After dripping well, place in cold syrup. Do not put on the fire.
9. After two days, give a short boil.
10. Give three or four more boils, always short, leaving two days between each one.
11. When finished, the preserve will be at point.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Damascos (Apricot Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds sugar
- 6 pounds apricots

## Directions

1. Weigh 6 pounds sugar and 6 pounds apricots
2. Remove the pit by pushing with a small stick
3. Place them in an earthenware cup
4. Cover with more than half the sugar, which should be very well ground and white
5. Let sit until the next day
6. Place on low heat and remove when they begin to boil
7. When cold, place again on low heat and remove when they begin to boil again
8. When cold, place the apricots in jars and boil on heat until thick
9. Then pour the hot syrup over the fruit
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Duraznitos (Small Peach Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small peaches
- Ash from thorn wood
- Water
- Sugar, five libras per one hundred peaches

## Directions

1. When a mild lye made from ash of thorn wood is boiling, place the small peaches in a basket and submerge in this lye for a short while.
2. Rinse in two tepid waters and cook in boiling water; remove and place in cold water to drain.
3. Place in warm syrup of half point.
4. Use five libras of sugar per one hundred peaches.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Frutillas (Strawberry Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberries
- Sugar, equal weight to the strawberries

## Directions

1. Remove the stem from the strawberry and clean well with a cloth.
2. Weigh an equal quantity of sugar as strawberries.
3. Arrange in a saucepan layers of strawberries, layer of sugar.
4. Leave thus until the next day.
5. Place on the fire and bring to point.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Guindas (Sour Cherry Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds powdered sugar
- 6 pounds ripe sour cherries

## Directions

1. Take 6 pounds powdered sugar and 6 pounds ripe sour cherries
2. Place in a pan, arranging with a little sugar at the bottom, then cherries and sugar alternately
3. Place on low heat and when they begin to boil, leave for twenty minutes
4. Remove from heat and let cool
5. Then empty into jars, boil the syrup, skimming it until thick, and pour hot over the fruit
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Limoncitos (Small Lemon Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- limoncitos (small lemons)
- salt
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Scrape these with glass to remove the bitter coating and place in cold water so they do not turn black
2. Make a cross cut on the side of the stem and place to precook in boiling water with a little salt
3. After precooked, drain in 2 or 3 cold waters until all salt and acid are gone
4. Place to drain in a basket
5. Then add to syrup that should be cold and of light consistency
6. Give 4 boilings, letting 2 days pass between each one, at the fourth give final point
7. For 1 pound of precook and drained limoncitos, add 1 1/2 pounds sugar
8. If the syrup is seen to be insufficient, 1 or 2 pounds sugar can be added, as it seems best
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Membrillo (Quince Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- Sugar, one libra four onzas per libra of quince
- Egg white for clarification

## Directions

1. Peel the quinces, cook them and pass through a sieve.
2. Make syrup and clarify with an egg white, strain and place on the fire until it reaches sufficient point.
3. Add the quince, stir and place on the fire again, which should be brisk, so that the preserve does not turn black.
4. You will know it is good when, placing a little on a plate, it does not run.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Naranjas (Orange Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges, fresh
- Sugar, four libras per one dozen regular-sized oranges
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges, leaving the rind, and place in cold water so they do not turn black; then cut in half, removing the seeds and place to cook in boiling water.
2. When tender, place to drip in a basket.
3. Place in cold syrup to bring to point, and at the time of removing them add a little lemon juice so they become bright.
4. Use four libras of sugar per one dozen oranges of regular size.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Peras (Pear Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pears
- Sugar, one libra per one libra of pears

## Directions

1. Make a thin syrup and place the pears in slices there, previously passed through boiling water.
2. Place over low heat until it reaches point.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Peras, Imitación a Dulce de Piña (Pear Preserve, Imitation of Pineapple Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pears
- Lemon juice
- Sugar, equal weight to the pears

## Directions

1. Peel the pears, cut into thin slices and soak in lemon juice, which should be ready in a plate.
2. Place in a saucepan one layer of pears and another layer of sugar.
3. Let rest for half a day, or a full day if the pear is hard.
4. Place on the fire, taking care to stir so it does not stick; give a light boil.
5. Repeat this same process three days in a row, and on the third day bring to point.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Piña (Pineapple Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pineapple
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the pineapple, cut into slices and place in a saucepan, one layer of pineapple and another of sugar; let rest until the next day; give a short boil and repeat this the following two days and bring to point on the third boil.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Dulce de Uvas (Grape Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- White Italian grapes, the sweetest and most golden
- A small amount of whey (lagrimilla)
- A little ash

## Directions

1. Deseed the grapes, leaving no stem.
2. Place in a saucepan over the fire with a small amount of whey and a little ash to remove the acidity.
3. Keep the grapes on the fire until the seed loosens; remove immediately so they do not overcook.
4. Extract the seed and rinse in the same whey in which they were blanched until no ash remains.
5. Transfer to a thick syrup that should be ready, because the preserve must be completed on the same day.
6. Do not add too much grape to the syrup as it will become poor and unattractive; it should be well saturated with syrup, and to bring it to point, do not place a large quantity in the saucepan.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Duraznos en Aguardiente (Peaches in Spirits)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 Zaragoza peaches
- 3 pounds sugar
- Spirits
- Water

## Directions

1. Choose thirty healthy and beautiful Zaragoza peaches that are not very ripe; clean them well with a cloth and prick them to the pit with a needle.
2. Make a syrup with 3 pounds of sugar and before it reaches point add the peaches, stir them and let them cook until they yield to the finger.
3. Remove from the fire and peel them.
4. Let the syrup boil until it reaches thick point and cool it.
5. When the peaches are cold, place them in jars.
6. For every cup of spirits calculate two of thick syrup, fill the jars well so that the peaches remain well covered with syrup and spirits.
7. Cap the jars and place soaked bladder on top, tie well with twine so that no air penetrates.
8. If desired, calculate the spirits this way: for three of thick syrup one of spirits about thirty degrees. This method is perhaps safer.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Empanadas de Hornos (Baked Empanadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- ground raw meat
- flour
- water
- flour
- rendered fat
- eggs
- salt
- wine

## Directions

1. Chop onion and fry it until slightly softened.
2. Add finely chopped raw meat without tendons and fry until cooked.
3. Add a tablespoon of flour, mix well, and add a little water so it becomes a sauce.
4. Let cool. Make a dough with a little flour, rendered fat, two eggs, and salted water.
5. Form the empanadas with a little wine inside and put in a very hot oven.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Empanadas Mendocinas (Mendoza-style Empanadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- sugar
- fat
- sweet wine
- salt
- cinnamon
- egg yolks
- egg white
- milk
- raw meat
- onion
- flour
- black pepper
- raisins
- olives
- hard-boiled eggs
- cooked chicken pieces
- chicken broth
- thick syrup
- ground almonds
- candied sour cherries
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make a dough with one pound of flour, half pound of sugar, four pounds of fat, and half cup of sweet wine in which the salt and a little cinnamon are dissolved, six yolks and one white. Form a dough and if it is too hard add a little milk and knead slightly.
2. For the filling, chop raw meat into small pieces and fry in a pan with a little color. Once the meat is cooked add the browned onion. Remove from heat and add a tablespoon of raw flour, roll it around, and season with salt, whole pepper, and a little sugar.
3. Form the empanadas and decorate the filling on top with raisins, olives, sliced hard-boiled eggs, pieces of hard-cooked chicken, and onion also cooked in chicken broth.
4. Put in the oven and when about to serve at the table, top with thick syrup mixed with ground almonds and candied sour cherries.
5. Sprinkle powdered sugar on top of everything and serve.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ensalada de Arvejas (Pea Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- salt
- onion
- hard-boiled eggs
- oil
- orange juice

## Directions

1. Cook the peas with salt
2. Drain the water and let cool
3. Partially cook a little onion, wash well in cold water and add to the peas
4. Chop hard-boiled eggs and add to the mixture
5. Season to taste with oil and orange juice
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ensalada de Arvejas (Pea Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- salt
- onion
- hard-boiled egg
- oil
- orange juice

## Directions

1. Cook peas with salt
2. Drain the water and cool
3. Blanch a little onion, wash well in cold water and add to peas
4. Add chopped hard-boiled egg
5. Dress with oil and orange juice to taste
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ensalada de Bacalao (Cod Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cod, soaked overnight
- potatoes, peeled
- carrots
- onion
- hard-boiled eggs
- egg yolk
- parsley, chopped
- fresh oregano
- ground pepper
- oil
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Soak cod overnight, then cook and let cool
2. Cook peeled potatoes and carrots separately
3. Chop onion as for salad and partially cook it
4. Once everything is cooked, break cod into small pieces
5. Dice the carrot into slices, likewise the cooked potato
6. Squeeze the onion and add everything together
7. Cook hard-boiled eggs and cut into slices to place on top of the salad
8. Make a sauce with beaten egg yolk mixed with chopped parsley, fresh oregano, ground pepper, plenty of oil, vinegar and salt
9. Mix everything well and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ensalada de Bacalao (Codfish Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- codfish
- potatoes
- carrots
- onion

## Directions

1. Soak codfish from one day to the next
2. Cook and cool it
3. Separately cook peeled potatoes and carrots
4. Chop onion as for salad and blanch it
5. When everything is cooked, shred the codfish into small pieces
6. Slice the carrot thinly
7. Slice the cooked potato similarly
8. Squeeze the onion and add everything together
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ensalada de Betarraga (Beet Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- onion
- ground pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cook beets until done
2. Cool and peel them
3. Cut into thin slices
4. Cut onion thinly and blanch it
5. Wash the onion in cold water and squeeze well
6. Add to the beets
7. Season with ground pepper and parsley
8. Dress to taste
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ensalada de Cebollas (Onion Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- ground pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut onion thinly
2. Put in a pot with cold water
3. Heat carefully without cooking it through
4. Remove and wash in cold water
5. Squeeze well
6. Dress as with the previous salads
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ensalada de Coliflor (Cauliflower Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt
- parsley
- ground pepper
- hard-boiled eggs
- olives

## Directions

1. Cook cauliflower in boiling salted water
2. When cooked, remove from water and cool
3. Chop finely or as desired
4. Add parsley, ground pepper, hard-boiled eggs in slices, and olives
5. Dress to taste as the others
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ensalada de Lechuga (Lettuce Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- hard-boiled egg

## Directions

1. Wash lettuce well
2. Cut to desired size
3. Put in a very clean cloth and squeeze well until all water is removed
4. Put in a bowl and dress to taste
5. Add chopped hard-boiled egg
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ensalada de Porotos Verdes (Green Bean Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- onion (optional)

## Directions

1. Tear green beans into four pieces
2. Put in a pot with cold water
3. Cook over active heat; at the first boil the beans are cooked and without losing color
4. Drain the water and cool
5. Add a little blanched onion if desired
6. Dress to taste
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ensalada de Repollo (Cabbage Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salt
- hard-boiled eggs
- parsley
- ground pepper
- olives

## Directions

1. Chop cabbage very finely
2. Put in a dry pot
3. Pour boiling water over it and add salt
4. Cover for half an hour
5. Wash in cold water and squeeze well
6. Put in a salad bowl
7. Add chopped hard-boiled eggs, parsley, and ground pepper
8. Add olives and dress to taste
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ensalada de Zanahoria (Carrot Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- onion
- ground pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as Beet Salad
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Escabeche de Cebollas (Pickled Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- salt
- strong vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Score the onions in a cross pattern
2. Add salt and let them soften in the sun for an entire day
3. Place them in strong vinegar with a little salt and plenty of pepper
4. Put them in glass jars
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Escabeche Inglés (English Pickled Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- salt
- vinegar
- pepper of two kinds
- cloves

## Directions

1. Cut the onions into pieces and wash them very well
2. Place them in a vessel and add ground salt
3. Separately, boil the vinegar with pepper of two kinds and cloves
4. Pour this hot mixture over the onions and cover well
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Espinacas (Spinach - Alternative)
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Butter
- Onion
- Bread crumbs
- Salt
- Milk

## Directions

1. Cook and chop very fine
2. Fry a little chopped onion in butter
3. Add the spinach, a little bread crumbs, salt, and milk, being careful that it is not clear
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Espinacas o Quinas (Spinach or Quinoa)
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach or quinoa
- Salt
- Water or milk
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cook with a little salt
2. Drain and chop well
3. Cook in a little water or milk with breadcrumbs and a little butter
4. Add the vegetables and let them boil for a moment
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Espárragos (Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender asparagus
- Salt
- Water
- Eggs
- Fried oil
- Pepper
- Spices
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Place the most tender asparagus in water and cook with salt and half or less water
2. Add eggs so they set without becoming hard
3. To serve, place in a dish with a little water and drizzle fried oil over the top, pepper, spices, and a few drops of vinegar if desired
4. Alternatively, serve the asparagus simply cooked with salt, arranged in bundles, accompanied by vinegar cruets
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Espárragos al Aceite (Asparagus with Oil)
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Water

## Directions

1. Cut a portion of the tender part from all the asparagus
2. Tie in a bundle
3. Blanch in boiling water and then cook
4. Remove all the water and place on a plate in a single bundle
5. Serve with vinegar cruets
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Espárragos con Huevos Revueltos (Asparagus with Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- White sauce
- Butter
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Cut into small pieces all the part of the asparagus that can be eaten
2. Place white sauce with butter in a pan
3. Add eggs and cook them, stirring constantly
4. At the last moment add the pieces of asparagus
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Espárragos en Salsa Blanca (Asparagus in White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Salt
- Water
- White sauce

## Directions

1. Wash and tie after in small bundles
2. Place to cook in boiling water with salt
3. Remove from water before they become soft
4. Serve hot with white sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Espárragos Revueltos (Scrambled Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small asparagus
- Beef tallow
- Salt
- Whole pepper
- Sugar
- Flour
- Water or broth
- Egg yolks
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Fried bread crusts

## Directions

1. Choose the smallest asparagus and cut into pieces the size of a chickpea
2. Cook as described previously
3. After cooking, remove from boiling water and place in cold water
4. Drain in a strainer to release all water
5. When well dried, place in a pan with beef tallow, salt, whole pepper, and a little sugar
6. Place on high heat, stirring constantly
7. Lightly sprinkle with flour and add a few drops of water or broth
8. A moment later remove from heat and while hot mix with two egg yolks
9. Serve surrounded by hard-boiled eggs in slices or with fried bread crusts
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Estofado de Chancho (Pork Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- eight tablespoons wine
- four tablespoons vinegar
- ground garlic
- cumin
- onions
- carrots
- two hot peppers

## Directions

1. Cut the pork meat and put to fry
2. Add eight tablespoons of wine and four of vinegar, ground garlic, cumin, onions in pieces, carrots in slices, two hot peppers
3. Cover everything in a pot, taking care that steam does not escape, and let it boil gently for one hour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Flan Alemán (German Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half bottles of milk
- half a pound of sugar
- vanilla
- four ounces of ground almond
- lemon peel
- twelve egg yolks
- ten ounces of chuño

## Directions

1. Boil one and a half bottles of milk with half a pound of sugar, vanilla, four ounces of ground almond, and lemon peel
2. Strain and beat twelve egg yolks
3. When the milk is lukewarm, gradually add the yolks with the chuño
4. Keep on the heat until cooked, stirring constantly so it doesn't stick
5. Pour into a mold that should be moistened with cold water
6. Let it cool and serve with wine sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Flan Alemán (German Flan)
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a half bottles of milk
- Half a pound of sugar
- Vanilla
- Four ounces of ground almonds
- Lemon zest
- Twelve egg yolks
- Ten ounces of chuño

## Directions

1. Boil the milk with half a pound of sugar, vanilla, four ounces of ground almonds, and lemon zest
2. Strain the mixture
3. Beat twelve egg yolks and mix them with ten ounces of chuño
4. When the milk is lukewarm, gradually add the egg yolks and chuño while stirring
5. Keep on the fire until it is set, stirring constantly so it does not stick
6. Pour into a mold that has been moistened with cold water
7. Allow to cool and serve with wine sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Flan Blanco i Amarillo (White and Yellow Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- fifteen eggs
- two ounces of sugar dissolved in two and a half glasses of milk

## Directions

1. Beat fifteen eggs
2. For the white, beat the egg whites like for meringues and the yolks for the yellow like for sponge cake
3. Dissolve two ounces of sugar in two and a half glasses of milk and add to the eggs
4. Let the white sit for a while until the foam dissolves
5. Pour into caramelized molds
6. The white needs little boiling, the yellow is done when it pulls away from the mold
7. Cook in water like the previous recipe
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Flan de Café (Coffee Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little roasted unground coffee
- three glasses of milk
- six egg yolks
- two egg whites

## Directions

1. Boil a little roasted unground coffee in three glasses of milk
2. Let it boil until the milk is well colored and has the full flavor of coffee
3. Strain and sweeten to taste
4. Beat six egg yolks and two egg whites and mix together
5. Pour into the caramelized mold
6. Cook in a pan with hot water
7. When it has boiled for a while, cover the mold with a small tin and place some embers on top so it cooks evenly
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Flan de Chocolate (Chocolate Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of chocolate
- a little boiling water
- four ounces of sugar
- a little cinnamon
- a little milk
- six egg yolks
- two egg whites

## Directions

1. Dissolve two ounces of chocolate in a little boiling water
2. Dissolve four ounces of sugar and a little cinnamon in another bit of water
3. Put a little milk on the heat and let it boil briefly
4. Let it cool and mix with six yolks and two egg whites, well beaten
5. Before combining everything, strain the boiled mixture
6. Cook like the coffee custard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Flan de Limón (Lemon Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal quantity of milk and sugar as for the coffee custard
- lemon peel
- a small piece of vanilla
- six egg yolks
- two egg whites

## Directions

1. Take equal quantities of milk and sugar as for the coffee custard
2. Add enough lemon peel to flavor it and a small piece of vanilla
3. Make the rest as with the coffee custard and cook the same way
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fricasé a la Italiano de Ave y de Carne (Italian-Style Fricassee of Poultry and Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- A little butter
- Flour
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Some pieces of ham
- Garlic
- Onion
- Raw poultry or meat
- Salt
- Chopped parsley
- Juice of one lemon
- Three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put a little butter and flour in a saucepan with cloves, cinnamon and some pieces of ham set apart
2. Chop garlic and onion
3. After frying, add broth to the raw poultry or meat, adding sufficient salt, chopped parsley and the juice of one lemon
4. At the time of serving add three beaten egg yolks
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fricasé de Pollo (Chicken Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickens
- Water
- One sprig of parsley
- One sprig of celery
- Three carrots
- Pepper
- One garlic clove
- Onion head
- Three ounces of butter
- Two tablespoons of wine
- Three egg yolks
- One tablespoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash the chickens in three warm waters
2. Put them to cook in a little water with a sprig of parsley, one of celery and three carrots, pepper, one garlic clove and onion head
3. Take three ounces of butter, dissolve it in two tablespoons of wine
4. Add all of this to the broth half an hour before serving
5. Add three egg yolks with one tablespoon of vinegar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Friles (Fried Chicken Strips)
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken, cleaned
- Aromatics (herbs)
- A little bread crumb

## Directions

1. After cleaning the chicken, cut all the meat into thin strips and place in a marinade with all the aromatics and a little bread crumb
2. After coating with all the aromatics, fry them
3. Serve at table together with a vegetable stew
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Frito de Pescado (Fried Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- flour
- oil or lard
- eggs

## Directions

1. In general, for all kinds of fried fish, it must be scaled, opened and cleaned
2. Then cut into rounds, coat with flour and fry in a pan with very hot oil
3. Carp and other fish are also fried with pork lard and served with fried eggs
4. Small river fish and others of this kind are served whole and cleaned
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Arroz (Rice Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- milk
- salt
- sugar
- three egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook rice with milk and a little salt
2. Add a little sugar and three egg yolks
3. Mix well
4. Add beaten egg whites
5. Fry by small spoonfuls in very hot lard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Bacalao (Codfish Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- codfish
- cooked potato, peeled

## Directions

1. Soak codfish from the day before
2. Cook and shred the codfish well
3. Take an equal quantity of cooked peeled potato
4. Mix with the codfish and grind together until a smooth paste forms
5. Add sugar to taste
6. Fry in lard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Coliflor (Cauliflower Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four egg yolks
- cooked cauliflower
- egg whites
- flour (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat three or four egg yolks slightly
2. Mix with cooked cauliflower, removing thick stems first
3. Stir until cauliflower is well broken up
4. Add beaten egg whites
5. If too thin, add a very little flour
6. Fry in lard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Harina (Flour Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- salt
- butter
- five egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Make a cooked paste with flour, salt, and butter
2. When it pulls away well from the pan, let it cool
3. Add five egg yolks
4. When ready to fry, mix in beaten egg whites
5. Drop by small spoonfuls into the pan
6. Strike with a stick so they puff up
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Harina con Leche (Flour Fritters with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- milk
- four eggs minus one white
- a little raw flour
- sugar
- lard or butter

## Directions

1. Dissolve a little flour in cold milk and another portion of milk in a pan to heat
2. Combine with the first and cook until it pulls away from the pan; it should not be very hard
3. Remove from heat and put in a dish
4. Add four eggs minus one white, a little raw flour, and sugar
5. Beat everything together well
6. Fry in lard that should not be very hot
7. Strike with a stick while frying
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Maíz (Corn Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- salt
- sugar
- three egg yolks
- flour (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut and grind corn
2. If too coarse, strain through a coarse sieve
3. Fry in a little lard
4. When cooked, add a little salt and sugar to taste
5. When slightly warm, add three egg yolks
6. If they do not bind well when frying, add a little flour to the corn
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Papa (Potato Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt
- sugar
- three egg yolks
- egg whites
- lard

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes
2. Grind them
3. Season with salt, sugar, and three egg yolks
4. Add beaten egg whites when ready to fry
5. Fry in very hot lard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Papas Crudas (Fried Raw Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Raw potatoes
- Milk
- Salt
- Bread crumbs
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Grate raw peeled potatoes and remove the water they release
2. Add to the potatoes a small cup of milk, a little salt, bread crumbs and four eggs
3. Beat all of this
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Papas Crudas (Raw Potato Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes, peeled
- milk (one small cup)
- salt
- bread crumb
- four eggs
- lard or butter

## Directions

1. Grate raw peeled potatoes
2. Squeeze out the water they release
3. Add one small cup of milk, a little salt, bread crumb, and four eggs to the potatoes
4. Beat everything well
5. Fry by spoonfuls in hot lard or butter
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Porotitos (Green Bean Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- two eggs
- salt
- flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Trim and cook green beans
2. Roll them in two beaten eggs mixed with a little salt and flour
3. Fry in very hot lard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Sesos (Brain Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- eggs
- bread crumb
- lard

## Directions

1. Cook the brains
2. Roll them in beaten eggs and then in bread crumb
3. Fry in hot lard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Fritos de Zapallo (Squash Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- squash pieces
- three egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook pieces of squash
2. Mash them
3. Add three beaten egg yolks
4. Mix well
5. Add well-beaten egg whites
6. Fry in lard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas (Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two whole eggs
- Half pound of sugar
- Half pound of flour
- Lemon zest
- Flour
- Butter and bacon
- Whole anise

## Directions

1. Beat two whole eggs and half pound of sugar with a wooden spoon for half an hour
2. Add half pound of flour and lemon zest
3. Place a little flour on the table and place the dough on top, spread without overworking
4. Roll not too thin and cut
5. Grease the pans with butter and bacon and sprinkle with whole anise
6. Place the cookies and put in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas de Almendras (Almond Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound of almonds with shell
- Three egg whites
- Half pound of sugar
- Half ounce of cinnamon
- Flour with sugar
- Flour and sugar for the mold
- Butter for the pans
- Flour
- Egg yolk and sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Grind half pound of almonds with shell
2. Beat three egg whites, half pound of sugar, half ounce of cinnamon, and combine all
3. Mix a little flour with sugar and place on the table with the dough on top
4. Roll not too thin and cut, always greasing the mold with flour and sugar
5. Grease the pans with butter, a little flour, and put in the oven
6. When cooked, brush the top with a beaten egg yolk and sugar using a feather
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas de Amor (Love Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four ounces of butter, well beaten
- Grated lemon
- Four egg yolks
- Half of half an ounce of cinnamon
- Four ounces of ground almonds
- Sufficient flour to make a soft dough
- Flour for dusting
- One egg white
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- A little flour

## Directions

1. Beat four ounces of butter well and add grated lemon and four egg yolks
2. Add half of half an ounce of cinnamon, stir well, and add four ounces of ground almonds
3. Add sufficient flour to make a soft dough
4. Follow the same procedure as for the others, cut somewhat thick
5. Place on greased and flour-dusted pans
6. Beat one egg white and add sugar, plenty of cinnamon, and a little flour
7. After removing the cookies from the oven, spread this mixture on top with a spoon and return to the oven to dry
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas de Dama (Lady's Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound of butter
- Lemon zest
- Nutmeg
- Cinnamon
- One pound of sugar
- Two egg yolks and one white
- Two pounds four ounces of flour

## Directions

1. Beat half pound of butter and add lemon zest, nutmeg, cinnamon, and one pound of sugar
2. Add two egg yolks and one white, stir everything and beat well with two pounds four ounces of flour
3. When well combined, remove from the bowl, place on a board, roll out, and cut into rounds somewhat thick
4. Place on pans and in a very mild oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas de Huevo (Egg Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- Three dozen eggs
- Three egg whites only
- Twelve ounces of sugar
- Cinnamon
- Lemon
- Anise
- One cup of wine

## Directions

1. Beat three dozen eggs with only three egg whites together with twelve ounces of sugar, cinnamon, lemon, and anise
2. When the beaten mixture is like sponge cake consistency, add one cup of wine
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas de Huevo (Egg Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty-six eggs, with only three egg whites
- twelve ounces of sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon
- anise
- one glass of white wine
- flour as needed
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat thirty-six eggs with only three egg whites together with twelve ounces of sugar, cinnamon, lemon and anise
2. When the mixture is like sponge cake batter, add one glass of white wine and enough flour to make a soft dough
3. Remove the dough onto a table and add a piece of butter and sugar
4. When the butter and sugar are granulated like meringue batter, add the egg little by little, always beating
5. After finishing this operation, sift in one pound of cake flour, mix well and put into two loaf pans and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas de Jenjibre (Ginger Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of flour
- two pounds of chancaca or cane honey
- half pound of dark sugar
- candied orange peel
- one and one-half ounces of ginger
- twelve ounces of melted butter

## Directions

1. Mix all together and let rest for one hour
2. Then place in small pieces on a baking sheet, flatten with the hand, and place in the oven for ten minutes
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas de Mantequilla (Butter Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- half pound of butter
- half pound of powdered sugar
- one small cup of milk
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. To one pound of flour add half pound of butter and half pound of powdered sugar, one small cup of milk, cinnamon and cloves
2. If the dough becomes too soft, add flour until it does not stick
3. Cut the cookies and place in a very hot oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas Imitación de las de Cimño (Imitation Cinnamon Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg yolks
- four ounces of sugar
- half small glass of pisco or strong liquor
- one and one-half small glasses of water with a little salt and cinnamon
- flour as needed
- two ounces of hot fat, well burned

## Directions

1. Beat six egg yolks with four ounces of sugar like sponge cake
2. Add one and one-half small glasses of water with a little salt and cinnamon and enough flour so the dough is not hard
3. Add two ounces of hot fat, well burned, knead much, stretch and cut the cookies
4. These are fried well and dried in a pot of boiling water, taking out those that float on top
5. Wrap in a hot cloth and then place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas Improvisadas (Improvised Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- two and one-half pounds of flour
- ten ounces of sugar
- half pound of butter
- seven whole eggs
- one cup of milk with lemon zest
- cinnamon
- anise

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together and knead well
2. Cut the cookies and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas Ligeras (Light Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of flour
- Half pound of butter
- Half pound of sugar
- One cup of milk

## Directions

1. Combine flour, butter, sugar, and milk, and knead well
2. Roll out, cut into rounds, and bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas Limeñas (Lima-Style Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of flour
- one pound of rice
- two ounces of butter
- five eggs
- a little milk
- egg white
- a little water
- almond, finely ground mixed with sugar

## Directions

1. Make a dough of regular thickness from all these ingredients, as for puff pastry
2. Cut the cookies, brushing them with a beaten egg and a little water
3. Brush others with a little milk using a feather and sprinkle on top a little ground almond mixed with sugar
4. Place on butter-lined baking sheets in an oven heated for sponge cake
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas Reales (Royal Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of butter, desalted the day before by pouring boiling water over it
- six egg yolks
- half pound of sugar
- flour as needed
- egg white
- sugar

## Directions

1. The next day, remove the butter and place in a cup over the mouth of a pot so it melts a little
2. Beat with a spoon until white
3. Add six egg yolks
4. When they are combined with the butter, add half pound of sugar and stir a little
5. Add enough flour to make a very soft dough, place flour on the table and mix the dough well
6. Form a large ball and take small pieces, shaping them into an S directly on a buttered baking sheet
7. On top, brush with egg white and a little sugar using a feather
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Galletas Superiores (Superior Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-four egg yolks
- one pound of powdered sugar
- grated lemon
- cinnamon
- one tablespoon of butter
- one glass of white wine or cognac
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Beat twenty-four egg yolks with one pound of powdered sugar, grated lemon and cinnamon
2. When beaten like sponge cake batter, add one tablespoon of butter, one glass of white wine or cognac and enough flour to make a white dough
3. Cut into thin pastry sheets and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina a la Jardinera (Chicken Gardener Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole hen
- Whole onion
- Celery
- Pepper
- Whole peeled potatoes
- Carrot slices
- One tablespoon of vinegar
- Two tablespoons of oil
- Two tablespoons of broth

## Directions

1. After washing well, put the whole hen in a saucepan
2. Add whole onion, celery, pepper, whole peeled potatoes, carrot slices, one tablespoon of vinegar, two of oil and two of broth
3. Put on the fire well covered
4. Move from time to time so it doesn't stick
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina a lo Muñero (Hen in the Muñero Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Clean hen
- Three onion heads of regular size
- Half a small cup of oil
- Half a small cup of wine
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Celery
- One clove of garlic
- A little rosemary

## Directions

1. Put the clean hen in a pot
2. Add three onion heads of regular size, half a small cup of oil, half a small cup of wine, pepper, parsley, celery, one clove of garlic and a little rosemary
3. Seal the lid with paste and put on regular heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina Clásica (Classic Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole hen
- Onion, whole
- Celery branches
- Peppercorns
- Potatoes, whole and peeled
- Carrot slices
- One tablespoon of vinegar
- Two tablespoons of oil
- Two tablespoons of broth

## Directions

1. Put the whole hen in a saucepan after washing well
2. Add whole onion, celery branches, peppercorns, whole peeled potatoes, carrot slices, one tablespoon of vinegar, two of oil and two of broth
3. Put on the fire well covered
4. Move from time to time so it doesn't stick
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina con Arroz (Chicken with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hen
- Well-washed rice
- Salt
- Onion in large pieces
- Peppercorns
- Water
- One tablespoon of color

## Directions

1. Cook the hen with well-washed rice, salt, onion in large pieces and peppercorns
2. Calculate sufficient water for the rice to cook well, but so it is not clear
3. Once cooked, add one tablespoon of color
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina con Arroz y Salsa de Curri (Chicken with Rice and Curry Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hen
- One tablespoon of curry
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cook the hen as in the previous recipe and serve with a curry sauce prepared as follows: dissolve one tablespoon of curry in a piece of hot butter and serve with the hen
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina con Cebollas (Chicken with Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hen
- Lard
- Salt
- Pepper
- Thyme
- Clove
- Bay leaf
- Fifteen or sixteen small onions
- Broth
- Starch

## Directions

1. Brown the hen in lard with salt, pepper, thyme, clove and bay leaf
2. Put in the saucepan fifteen or sixteen small onions
3. Let them brown, add a little broth and cook on low heat five or six hours until the hen is well cooked
4. Then thicken the sauce with starch and serve the hen surrounded by the onions
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina con Espárragos (Chicken with Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fat hen
- A little pot broth
- Onions in slices
- Asparagus
- Parsley
- Oregano
- Salt
- Pepper
- Three potatoes

## Directions

1. Cook the hen in a little pot broth with onions in slices and asparagus, parsley, oregano, salt, pepper and three potatoes
2. Leave on the fire until well cooked
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina con Repollo (Chicken with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hen to be stewed
- Tender cabbage, chopped
- Half a glass of white wine
- Seasonings
- Pepper
- Cloves
- A little chili

## Directions

1. Place the hen to be stewed in a saucepan in which tender cabbage will be chopped
2. Pour over it half a glass of white wine, seasonings, pepper, cloves and a little chili
3. Cover the saucepan and cook on low heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina con Salsa de Leche (Chicken with Milk Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- A little oil
- One tablespoon of butter
- One glass of milk
- Hen, cut into pieces
- Carrots in slices
- One egg
- Flour

## Directions

1. Put a little oil, one tablespoon of butter and one glass of milk in a saucepan
2. Once the butter is dissolved, put the hen cut into pieces and carrots in slices
3. After everything boils, add one egg beaten with flour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina Fiambre en Relleno (Cold Stuffed Hen)
body:
## Ingredients

- hens
- vinegar
- cinnamon
- cloves
- pimientos
- garlic
- raw meat
- bacon, chopped
- salt

## Directions

1. Kill the hens carefully so the neck does not break
2. Pluck in hot water, split along the backbone from the neck to the rump and remove the eggs carefully so they come out whole
3. Wash the carcasses well and place them in a little vinegar seasoned with cinnamon, cloves, two pimientos and some crushed garlic cloves; keep in this vinegar for four to six hours
4. Meanwhile mash well a piece of raw meat and mix with part of the pimientos that were added to the vinegar and a little chopped bacon
5. Stuff the hens with this and strain the seasoned vinegar over them and cook in water with a little salt
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina Frita (Fried Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hen, disjointed
- Onions
- Parsley
- Salt
- Whole pepper
- Lemon juice
- Flour, one tablespoon
- Broth
- Butter
- Fried eggs
- Parsley
- Onions
- Bay leaves
- Oregano
- Whole pepper and ground pepper
- One glass of vinegar
- Butter
- Broth
- Breadcrumbs or flour

## Directions

1. Disjoint the hen and place in a saucepan with onions, parsley, salt, whole pepper and lemon juice
2. Leave for one hour in this sauce, either raw or cooked
3. Remove and place the pieces in a frying pan with one tablespoon of flour, a little broth and butter so they brown
4. Serve with fried eggs around them and a sauce made as follows: put in a saucepan parsley, onions, bay leaves, oregano, whole and ground pepper, one glass of vinegar and butter
5. Bring to a boil and add broth
6. Strain through a sieve and thicken with breadcrumbs or flour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina Mechada (Larded Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hen
- Bacon strips
- Oil or fat for the paper
- Watercress
- Salt
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Lard with bacon strips
2. Wrap in oiled paper and put on the roaster
3. When almost roasted, remove the paper so it takes color
4. Baste the bacon leaves
5. If possible, place on watercress on a plate, sprinkled with salt, with a little vinegar and serve watered with the same
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina o Capón con Aceite i Vinagre (Hen or Capon with Oil and Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- one hen or capon
- water
- onion heads
- sweet peppers
- whole peppercorns
- two tablespoons oil
- vinegar to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the bird with water, onion heads, sweet peppers, and some whole peppercorns
2. When tender, add two tablespoons of oil and vinegar to taste
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina o Capón con Aceite y Vinagre (Chicken or Capon with Oil and Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bird
- Water
- Onion heads
- Sweet peppers
- Whole peppercorns
- Two tablespoons of oil and vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the bird with water, onion heads and sweet peppers and some whole peppercorns
2. When tender, add two tablespoons of oil and vinegar to taste
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina Picante (Spicy Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Raw hen
- Butter
- Two sweet or green peppers, or dried peppers, seeded and cut
- Ground saffron
- Flour, about one tablespoon
- Broth

## Directions

1. Put the raw, disjointed hen in a saucepan with butter
2. Add peppers cut without seeds, ground saffron, flour like one tablespoon and broth
3. Cover and cook on low heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina Rellena (Stuffed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hen or chickens
- Bread crumbs
- Eggs
- Onions
- Parsley
- Livers and gizzards, finely chopped
- One tablespoon of vinegar
- Two tablespoons of broth
- One small tablespoon of flour
- Butter

## Directions

1. Fill the hen or chickens with bread crumbs, eggs, onions, parsley, livers and gizzards, all finely chopped
2. Place in a saucepan with one tablespoon of vinegar, two of broth and one small tablespoon of flour dissolved in the same broth
3. Put on the fire with butter
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina Rellena (Stuffed Hen)
body:
## Ingredients

- One fat hen, killed the day before
- Apple, finely chopped
- Butter
- Small pieces of bread
- A little sugar

## Directions

1. Take a fat hen killed the day before, being careful not to remove its fat or lard when plucking
2. Stuff it with finely chopped apple fried in butter
3. When well fried, add small pieces of bread
4. Once this is well cooked, add a little sugar and stuff the raw hen
5. Begin roasting early, putting a piece of butter on it, roasting slowly
6. Remove it, let it rest for a while and baste it with the same broth it releases and with a piece of butter
7. Repeat this several times
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gallina Rellena de Manzana (Chicken Stuffed with Apple)
body:
## Ingredients

- One well-fattened hen, killed the day before
- Apple, finely chopped
- Butter
- Bread, small pieces
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the hen and ensure no fat or lard remains when cleaning it
2. Fill with finely chopped apple fried in butter
3. When the apple is well fried, add small pieces of bread
4. Once this is well cooked, add a little sugar and stuff the raw hen
5. Roast early, adding a piece of butter
6. Roast slowly
7. Remove and let rest for a while
8. Baste with the same broth the hen releases and with a piece of butter
9. Repeat this basting several times
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ganso con Mostaza (Goose with Mustard)
body:
## Ingredients

- One tender goose
- Goose liver
- Small onions
- Laurel
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Butter
- One tablespoon of mustard
- Bread crumbs
- Mustard
- A little vinegar
- Broth
- Pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Stuff the tender goose with its liver, small onions, laurel, parsley and thyme, all finely chopped
2. Spit it and roast it, basting occasionally with butter
3. When almost roasted, add one tablespoon of mustard to the butter and spread it all over
4. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and return to the fire to finish roasting and browning
5. Serve with the following sauce: put butter in a saucepan with one tablespoon of mustard, a little vinegar, broth, pepper and salt, and let it cook until it thickens
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ganso con Salsa (Goose with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- One goose
- Butter
- Potatoes
- Flour
- Broth
- Chopped parsley
- A little vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the goose
2. Fry potatoes cut into thin slices in butter
3. Make a sauce of flour, butter and broth from the goose
4. Add chopped parsley and a little vinegar to the sauce
5. At the time of sending to table add the potatoes
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ganso de San Martín (St. Martin's Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- One tablespoon of butter
- About six chopped apples
- A little bread crumbs
- Peeled and ground almonds
- A few raisins
- Sugar to taste
- One glass of wine
- One raw goose

## Directions

1. Put one tablespoon of butter in a pan and fry about six chopped apples there
2. Once they are fried, add some bread crumbs, peeled and well ground almonds, a few raisins, sugar to taste and one glass of wine
3. Let it cook well and fill the raw goose with this mixture
4. Put it in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ganso Guisado (Stewed Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- One goose
- Broth
- Apple
- A little burnt flour
- Lemon zest
- Raisins
- Almonds
- A little sugar

## Directions

1. Cut into pieces and cook the goose
2. In a little broth in which the pieces have been cooked, add chopped apple, a little burnt flour, lemon zest, raisins and some almonds
3. When the apples are very soft, add the pieces to this sauce and add a little sugar
4. Let it boil for a moment and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gelatina de Almendras (Almond Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four ounces of almonds
- A little milk
- Lemon peel
- Whole cinnamon
- Two cups of milk
- One ounce of isinglass
- A little water
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Peel four ounces of almonds and sprinkle them with a little milk in a saucepan with lemon peel and whole cinnamon, and boil for a while
2. In another saucepan, put the almonds without straining with two cups of milk and put to boil over low heat
3. Place one ounce of isinglass with a little water at the edge of the fire to dissolve
4. Combine the two milks, strain, and pour into the saucepan where the isinglass is
5. Sweeten to taste, let boil for a while, and pour into a mold
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gelatina de Leche (Milk Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Lemon peel
- One ounce of isinglass
- A little milk

## Directions

1. Season the milk with sugar, cinnamon, and lemon peel and bring to a boil
2. Dissolve one ounce of isinglass in a little of this milk
3. Combine everything and pour into a mold
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gelatina de Membrillo (Quince Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve quinces
- More than one jar of water
- Four pounds four ounces of sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from twelve quinces with a cloth and cook them
2. Place in cold water, remove from the water, and break down dry with the pulp
3. Add more than one jar of water to this, stir, and pour into a straining bag
4. Take four pounds four ounces of sugar and make a syrup, strain it, and in the same strainer strain the distilled broth
5. After both are strained they should be almost the same amount
6. Test the point and when it does not run on a plate it is ready
7. Pour into a mold to cool
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gelatina de Naranja (Orange Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Cloves
- One ounce of isinglass

## Directions

1. Squeeze some oranges and season the juice with sugar, cinnamon, and cloves
2. Bring to a boil
3. Add one ounce of dissolved isinglass, strain, and let cool in molds
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gelatina de Vino (Wine Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef feet
- Eight egg whites
- Juice or peel of eight lemons
- Eight oranges
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Half bottle of port

## Directions

1. Cook beef feet and strain the broth
2. Season with eight egg whites, juice or peel of eight lemons and eight oranges, cloves, cinnamon, and half a bottle of port
3. Bring to a boil, then pour into a flannel bag of two or three layers to strain
4. If it comes out cloudy the first time, strain again
5. Pour into a mold and let cool
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Gloria para el Té (Tea Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four eggs
- Equal weight of powdered sugar
- Equal weight of flour
- Equal weight of butter
- Four egg yolks
- Four egg whites
- Peeled almonds
- Currants
- Cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Place four eggs on one side of a balance scale and weigh out equal amounts of powdered sugar, flour, and butter
2. Beat the butter, add the four egg yolks, and beat again
3. Add the sugar and flour
4. Fold in the four egg whites, beaten separately as for meringues
5. Place the batter in a mold greased with butter
6. Cover the top with peeled almonds that have been cut, currants, and cinnamon powder
7. Bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ground Lamb (Picadillo de Carnero)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover lamb meat
- fat
- fine herbs, chopped
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- broth

## Directions

1. Heat a little fat in the pan
2. Add fine chopped herbs, a glass of white wine, salt and pepper
3. Add a little broth
4. Only let it heat without boiling
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ground Lamb with Eggs (Picadillo de Carnero con Huevos)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb meat
- croutons
- fried eggs
- tomato sauce or other sauce

## Directions

1. Chop the meat very finely
2. Place on a plate
3. Serve with fried croutons around and fried eggs on top of a tomato sauce or other sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ground Meat Meatballs au Gratin (Albóndigas de Picadillo al Gratín)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ground meat
- Flour
- Lard
- Breadcrumbs
- Broth

## Directions

1. Prepare the ground meat as described before
2. Form balls and roll them in flour
3. Coat a dish well with lard that can be placed on fire
4. Place the balls in it, one against the other without touching
5. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs and moisten with a little broth
6. Place a small piece of lard on each ball
7. Brown in the oven
8. The broth is not absolutely necessary
9. Eat these balls plain or with a sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ground Meat with Brains (Otro Picadillo)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ground meat
- Cooked brains
- Flour or breadcrumbs
- Lard

## Directions

1. Instead of soaked breadcrumbs, use cooked and chopped brains
2. Wrap the meatballs in flour or breadcrumbs
3. Brown them in a pan with a little lard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Guachalomos Salpresos (Preserved Guachalomos)
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- water
- salt
- cumin
- oregano

## Directions

1. Use a regular amount of vinegar, if it is very strong add a little water
2. Season with salt, cumin and a little oregano
3. Leave the guachalomos in this seasoning for four hours
4. Remove and sprinkle oregano on them and place in a regular press
5. Leave there as long as desired
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Guindas en Aguardiente (Sour Cherries in Spirits)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sour cherries
- Spirits
- Thick syrup

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the guindas and wash them in spirits.
2. Place them in a jar and fill it with spirits.
3. Cap it well so no air penetrates.
4. When you want to eat them, uncap and remove all the spirits and fill with thick syrup.
5. Cap and leave for fifteen days without moving them so they become impregnated with sugar; if desired they can be made another way.
6. After washing the guindas, arrange them in the jar with a layer of guindas and another of ground sugar, fill the jar well with spirits and cap it well.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Guisado de Gallina (Chicken Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted hen
- Lard
- Onion, chopped
- Parsley
- Broth
- Wine or vinegar
- Fresh lard
- Spices
- Vegetables, chopped
- Sorrel, in particular

## Directions

1. After roasting, cut into pieces and brown with lard, chopped onion and parsley
2. When in point, add the necessary broth to cover the pieces and a little wine or vinegar and fresh lard
3. Season with spices
4. If desired, add some chopped vegetables, or particularly sorrel, which make a good stew
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Guisado de Truchas (Stewed Trout)
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- bacon
- lettuce
- bread
- pepper
- orange juice
- lemon

## Directions

1. Scaled, split down the middle and cut into rounds, fry with fatty and lean bacon
2. Cook in seasoned water some heads of white lettuce
3. When the trout are fried in their own fat, fry some slices of bread and then the lettuce heads so they do not dry out
4. Remove and place on slices of bread over other heads of cabbage and pieces of trout
5. Sprinkle with pepper and orange juice, add thin slices of lemon between the cabbage and trout
6. Serve hot
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Guiso de Calabazas (Squash Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- Squash
- Tender squash
- Corn
- Tomato
- Onion
- Spices
- Cheese
- Eggs
- Fat

## Directions

1. Peel the squash and remove the seeds
2. Chop finely with a little tender squash
3. Cook and when done, drain the water
4. Empty into a dry dish and break up with a ladle
5. Make a fry in fat with tender corn, tomato, onion, and spices
6. Add the squash and place on low heat
7. Grate cheese, beat some eggs, and add this before serving
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Guiso de Callampas (Mushroom Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion
- Mushrooms
- Cumin
- Pepper
- Salt
- Bread
- Grated cheese
- Broth

## Directions

1. Fry a little onion until it begins to brown
2. Add the mushrooms
3. Add a little cumin, pepper, salt, bread, and grated cheese
4. Add a little broth and let it simmer
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Guiso de Pescado Frito (Fried Fish Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- fried fish
- long onion
- tomato
- potatoes
- cheese
- broth

## Directions

1. Fry long onion with a little tomato; separately fry very thin slices of potatoes
2. Arrange one layer in a serving dish or pot and grate a little cheese on it
3. Place the fish and cover it with a layer equal to the first
4. Over all pour one cup of broth, place over low heat and do not stir but only move it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Habas Verdes (Green Fava Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fava beans
- Water
- Salt
- Oil
- Roasted hazelnuts
- Toasted bread
- Water
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Eggs

## Directions

1. When small, remove only the ends
2. When large, peel entirely
3. Boil in water and salt
4. Drain and drizzle with oil
5. Make a sauce with roasted hazelnuts, a little toasted bread soaked in water and vinegar, pepper, and eggs corresponding to the sauce
6. Dilute the sauce with seasoned water
7. Pour over the beans and bring to a boil on gentle heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Almendra (Almond Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of almonds for a regular mold
- Water or milk
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Toast the almonds, rub with a cloth, and grind with water or milk
2. Strain, adding sufficient milk before straining
3. If it becomes too thick, increase the water or milk in which the sugar is dissolved
4. Calculate the amount needed for the mold and season with cinnamon
5. These ices can also be made and are very good without toasting the almonds
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Aurora (Aurora Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of almonds
- Four egg yolks
- Cinnamon infusion

## Directions

1. Make a light almond milk (horchata) from one pound of almonds and place on the fire
2. When it boils, add four well-beaten egg yolks that have first been dissolved in a little of the same almond milk
3. Leave it on the fire for a while and beat with a chocolate mill (molinillo)
4. When the egg is cooked, remove it, sweeten, and add a little cinnamon infusion
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Bocado (Cream Custard Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk for a two-pound mold
- Eight eggs, well beaten
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Lemon zest
- Coffee infusion (optional)

## Directions

1. Boil the necessary milk for a two-pound mold
2. When cooked, add eight well-beaten eggs and let cook
3. When the eggs are cooked, remove and strain
4. Sweeten and add cinnamon and some lemon zest
5. A little coffee infusion can also be added to the milk
6. Freeze
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Café con Leche (Coffee and Milk Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk for a regular mold
- One pound of roasted unground coffee
- Lemon zest
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Calculate the milk needed for a regular mold
2. Bring it to boil with one pound of roasted unground coffee and a piece of lemon zest
3. Give it three boils and strain
4. Sweeten to taste and freeze
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Canela (Cinnamon Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a half ounces of fine cinnamon
- Water
- Sugar
- Gum arabic

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the cinnamon and let it infuse quietly for a while in sufficient water
2. When well infused, strain the clear part
3. Add sugar and a little gum arabic dissolved in hot water
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Damascos (Apricot Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots
- Milk, cold and cooked
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the apricots, wash them, and crush them in earthenware pots, first removing the pits
2. Have ready the desired amount of milk, which should be cold and cooked
3. Pass the apricot through a sieve, combine with the milk, and sweeten to taste
4. These ices can also be made with water
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Duraznos (Peach Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds sliced peaches
- 2 pounds sugar
- Juice of 4 lemons

## Directions

1. Place the sliced peaches to boil for a little while and then pass them through a sieve.
2. To this add 2 pounds of sugar made into syrup and the juice of four lemons; mix everything and freeze it.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Duraznos (Peach Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pounds of sliced peaches
- Two pounds of sugar
- Juice of four lemons

## Directions

1. Boil the sliced peaches for a short time
2. Pass them through a sieve
3. Mix in two pounds of sugar made into syrup and the juice of four lemons
4. Mix all together and freeze
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Frutillas (Strawberry Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberries
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Crush the strawberries well and pass through a sieve
2. Combine this juice with sugar dissolved in sufficient water, but calculate that it remains slightly thick for good results
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Leche (Milk Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of milk
- Twelve ounces of sugar
- Zest of a lemon
- Zest of a citron
- Zest of an orange
- Two tablespoons of orange flower water

## Directions

1. Bring everything to a boil
2. When sufficiently thick, let it cool and freeze
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Limón o de Naranja (Lemon or Orange Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fruit (lemon or orange)
- Sugar
- Water
