- Gum arabic (for lemon variety)
- Egg white (for lemon variety)

## Directions

1. Peel the fruit and cut it to squeeze well
2. Add sugar made into syrup and the necessary water so it is not too acidic
3. For lemon ice, a little gum arabic dissolved in hot water and a well-beaten egg white can be added when it begins to set
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Manzanas (Apple Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty apples
- Ten ounces of water
- Twelve ounces of powdered sugar
- Juice of two lemons

## Directions

1. Cut the apples in half and remove the interior
2. Crush them and place them in a pot with ten ounces of water
3. Let them boil until one part is consumed
4. Pass through a sieve
5. Add twelve ounces of powdered sugar
6. When cooled, add the juice of two lemons and freeze
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Helados de Nueces (Walnut Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk
- Peeled and ground walnuts
- Syrup (almíbar)
- Eight egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook milk with peeled and ground walnuts
2. After cooking, pass through a sieve
3. Sweeten with syrup
4. Beat eight egg yolks and add to the milk
5. Place back on the fire
6. Remove, let cool, and freeze
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Hielo de Manzanas (Apple Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked apples
- sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Once well cooked, pass the apples through a fine sieve
2. Add sugar, juice of 1 lemon, and 10 beaten egg whites
3. Mix everything well and pour into a baking dish
4. Bake in the oven for 1/4 hour
5. Remove and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Hojaldra (Puff Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 whole eggs
- sufficient flour
- 1 large spoonful of well-rendered lard
- 1 spoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. Knead and roll out the dough very thin
2. Add 1 spoonful of vinegar so the dough remains soft
3. Stretch with your hands, cut, and fry
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Horchata en Botella (Bottled Almond Horchata)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound almonds
- Water
- 2 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the almonds and grind them like paste with a little water.
2. After grinding well, add a little water, enough to extract all the juice from the almonds, which should result in a thick milk.
3. Strain it, make a syrup of 2 pounds of sugar, and when it reaches the thread stage, add the milk and stir well over the fire until it reaches point.
4. Let it cool and bottle it.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Horchata en Cajas (Almond Horchata in Boxes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds peeled almonds
- Slightly more than half a liter of water
- 4 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Grind the peeled almonds like paste and place in a basin, add slightly more than half a liter of water and stir well.
2. Strain it and add an equal quantity of water to the coarse almonds; strain again and add this milk to the syrup when it is crystallizing and let it boil over high fire.
3. The syrup is made from 4 pounds of sugar; it is done well when placing a little on a plate it does not run.
4. The box should be left three days uncovered so it forms a crust.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Hueso de Cabeza de Chauebo (Head Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- chauebo head
- garlic
- pepper
- chili
- cumin
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Partially cook the head to debone it
2. Remove all the meat and cut into regular pieces
3. Remove the skin from the tongue and cut into pieces
4. Cook in water with garlic, pepper, chili, cumin, salt and vinegar
5. After the meat is cooked place it in a mold
6. Cover above and below with the skin of the head which should also be cooked
7. Put the mold lid on to be able to place a weight on top
8. When cold remove the weight
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevo Mol del Asiento (Egg Custard from the Pan)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 4 ounces almonds
- 1.5 handfuls of sponge cake powder
- 1 glass good wine
- ground cinnamon
- ground clove

## Directions

1. To 1 pound of flour at the proper point, add 4 ounces of almonds and 1.5 handfuls of sponge cake powder, and bring everything to a boil again for a short time
2. Remove from heat and add the sponge cake
3. Add 1 glass of good wine and bring to the proper point by adding the prepared egg
4. When done, add ground cinnamon and ground clove
5. Pour onto a table with powder on top and mark the sponge cakes so that after they cool they can be cut with a knife
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevo Molí (Egg Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 10 to 8 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make a syrup of 1 pound sugar
2. Beat the egg yolks until they turn white
3. Combine with the warm syrup and place over gentle heat
4. Stir constantly until the egg is cooked
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevo Molí del Asiento (Settled Egg Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour at good point
- 4 ounces almonds
- 1.5 handfuls of powdered sponge cake
- 1 glass of good wine
- egg prepared for this purpose
- cinnamon and ground clove

## Directions

1. Add the almonds and sponge cake powder to the flour at good point
2. Boil everything for a short time
3. Lower from heat and add the sponge cake
4. Add a glass of good wine
5. Bring to point by adding the prepared egg
6. When done, add cinnamon and ground clove
7. Pour onto a table
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevo Molí Fino (Fine Egg Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour, well reduced
- 4 ounces almonds reduced to paste
- 14 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with the almonds reduced to paste
2. Lower the pan when the syrup is at good point
3. Add the egg yolks all at once and stir well
4. Place over low heat, scraping the bottom so it does not stick
5. It should move very slowly and will be done when it fries from the bottom and separates from the sides
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevo Monstruo (Monster Egg)
body:
## Ingredients

- A dozen eggs
- Clean bladder

## Directions

1. Break a dozen eggs and separate the yolks from the whites
2. Take a clean bladder and fill it with well-beaten yolks
3. Tie it very well so air does not penetrate and suspend it in boiling water until the yolks take on consistency
4. Remove it, cool it and carefully break it open
5. Take another larger bladder and put in the whites, placing the already curdled yolks in the middle
6. Cook again and when you think it convenient, remove it, and breaking the bladder the egg will be done, which can be served with a poultry sauce or in rounds
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos Blandos (Soft Boiled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Eggs
- Cold water

## Directions

1. Bring water to a boil, put in the eggs you desire
2. Cook for five minutes and immediately put them in cold water
3. The white will be cooked and the yolk soft
4. Remove the shells carefully
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos Chimbos (Chimbos Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks for each 1 egg white
- butter
- clear syrup
- peeled almonds
- candied cherries

## Directions

1. For every 12 yolks, add 1 egg white and beat well
2. Butter the mold so it does not stick
3. Pour the batter into the mold and place it to cook in a saucepan with water
4. Cover the mold with a metal sheet with fire on top
5. Be very careful when removing it so that air does not hit it and cause it to fall
6. Make a clear syrup and pour the chimbos cut into pieces into it and let them soak for two days
7. Top with peeled almonds cut lengthwise and some candied cherries
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos con Leche (Eggs with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- Melted butter
- Finely chopped parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- One glass of milk
- One tablespoon of flour
- Hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. To the melted butter add very finely chopped parsley, salt, pepper and a glass of milk in which one tablespoon of flour has been dissolved beforehand
2. When the sauce is ready, place the hard boiled eggs cut into rounds in it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos con Leche (Eggs with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- milk
- flour
- hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. To the melted butter add very finely chopped parsley, salt, pepper and a glass of milk, in which a spoonful of flour has been dissolved beforehand
2. When the sauce is ready, place the cooked eggs in it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos con Perejil (Eggs with Parsley)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- saffron
- salt
- pepper
- mint leaf
- fine herbs
- eggs

## Directions

1. In a wide vessel put parsley, saffron, salt, pepper, a mint leaf and fine herbs, all in branch
2. Pour the fried eggs and let them come to a boil
3. Remove with a large skimmer
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos con Queso (Eggs with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cheese
- Bread
- Eggs
- Cheese
- Pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make a crust composed of half cheese and half bread
2. Break the eggs over it
3. Sprinkle with cheese, pepper and nutmeg
4. Place over low heat, browning them with a palette made of hot coals
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos Duros (Hard Boiled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Salt
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the eggs in their shells and when they are hard, remove them and take off the shells
2. Cut them into rounds
3. Place them on a plate and serve with salt, oil, salt, vinegar and pepper
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos Guisados (Stewed Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumb
- parsley
- onion
- butter
- egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- cream or milk cream

## Directions

1. Make a hash with bread crumb, parsley, onion, butter and egg yolks, with which to cover the bottom of a plate
2. On top pour the eggs one by one and place over gentle heat
3. Brown with a shovel made of hot coals, sprinkle with a little ground salt and a pinch of pepper and serve
4. Pour four spoonfuls of cream or milk cream on a plate and mix ten eggs into it, taking care that the yolks do not burst
5. Add a little grated cheese and a pinch of pepper
6. Cook in a portable oven and serve soft
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos Rellenos (Stuffed Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard boiled eggs
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- fresh butter
- onions
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- fine spices
- raw egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut hard boiled eggs in two pieces lengthwise
2. Mash the yolks with an equal amount of bread crumb soaked and squeezed of milk
3. Add the same amount of fresh butter
4. Add chopped onions and parsley, salt, pepper, fine spices
5. Mash this seasoning and soften with raw egg yolks
6. Fill the whites with these and put the rest on a fire-resistant plate, forming a layer one finger thick
7. Arrange the eggs on top, place the plate in the oven with gentle heat above and below so it takes color
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos Revueltos (Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- cream or broth

## Directions

1. Put in the pan, seasoning with salt and pepper
2. Add a spoonful of strained broth, cream or broth
3. Place over gentle heat, stirring until they thicken
4. Remove immediately
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos Revueltos con Cabezas de Espárragos (Scrambled Eggs with Asparagus Tips)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- asparagus tips
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Beat as the previous ones, mixing them with clean asparagus tips that have been scalded beforehand
2. Beat all together, finish and place on the serving plate garnished with other asparagus tips
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Huevos Revueltos con Tomate (Scrambled Eggs with Tomato)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- eggs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Fry the tomatoes and after seasoning them pour in the well-beaten eggs, adjusting the quantity
2. Stir everything until they are well curdled
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Humitas (Corn Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh corn kernels
- fat
- sweet or hot pepper

## Directions

1. Cut some fresh corn kernels and grind them. Fry in a little hot fat and add sweet or hot pepper as desired.
2. Arrange some corn husks, place a tablespoon of fried corn mixture in them, and cook in water or brown in the oven.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Hígado de Vaca a la Casera (Beef Liver Homestyle)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef liver
- thick pieces of bacon
- bacon strips
- veal scraps
- bay leaves
- clove
- good broth
- half bottle of white wine

## Directions

1. Lard the liver with thick pieces of well-seasoned bacon
2. Place in a casserole on bacon strips with veal scraps, bay leaves, and clove
3. Add good broth and half a bottle of white wine
4. Skim everything
5. Cover with other slices of bacon
6. Seal the casserole hermetically and place fire on top also
7. Ensure it does not boil in large bubbles
8. When cooked, strain the sauce taking half of it, reduce it, thicken with butter and serve pouring this sauce over it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Jalea de Patas para Señoritas (Calf's Foot Jelly for Young Ladies)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two large beef feet, parboiled
- Twelve egg whites with shells
- Juice of twelve oranges
- Juice of twelve lemons
- Peel of one lemon
- Peel of one orange
- Sticks of cinnamon
- A little cloves
- About three pounds of refined sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the two large parboiled beef feet into pieces, removing all the black part and fat
2. Wash this with three waters and put to cook in a pan with the bones until they are well cleaned
3. It is necessary to have boiling water to add to the pan as it reduces
4. Pass this broth through a sieve and leave in a tureen until the next day
5. If it has fat on top, remove it with a spoon until the jelly is completely clean of it
6. Put this in a pan and add twelve egg whites with shells, the juice of twelve oranges and twelve lemons, the peel of one lemon and one orange, some sticks of cinnamon, and a little whole cloves
7. Stir to combine everything and in the middle add about three pounds of refined sugar
8. Place the pan over the fire and without stirring let boil until it sets
9. Lower the pan from the fire and strain the jelly through a flannel bag that has been moistened beforehand in boiling water and wrung out
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Jamón Cocido con Vino Blanco (Ham Cooked with White Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- white wine
- cinnamon strips
- whole cloves
- bay leaves
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Soak the ham for twelve or more hours.
2. Cut off the shank and boil in water for at least an hour and a half.
3. Remove and drain, then place in a pot or suitable vessel with white wine to cover it.
4. Add some cinnamon strips, half a dozen whole cloves and some bay leaves.
5. If sweetness is desired, add one pound of sugar.
6. Can be served cold, but sprinkle sugar on top and toast it with a hot iron.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Jamón en Asador (Ham in the Roaster)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- white wine
- onions
- carrots
- thyme
- bay leaf
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare and place in adobo in good white wine with onions, carrots in slices, thyme and bay leaf
2. Be sure the vessel is hermetically sealed with a cloth and its lid or another vessel of equal size
3. Place in the roaster and while roasting, sprinkle with its own adobo
4. When it is half cooked, remove the rind, sprinkle with grated bread and return to the fire to brown it
5. Serve with its adobo, which will have been placed and reduced to consistency
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Jamón en Asador (Ham on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- good white wine
- onion
- carrots
- thyme
- bay leaf
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare and place the ham in good white wine with onion, sliced carrots, thyme and bay leaf.
2. Ensure the vessel is hermetically sealed with a cloth and its lid or another vessel of equal size.
3. Place on the spit and while roasting, baste it with its own marinade.
4. When half cooked, remove the skin, dust with bread crumbs and return to the fire to brown it.
5. Serve with its marinade, which should be reduced to a consistent sauce in a pan.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Jamón Frito (Fried Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- bread slices
- tomato sauce or eggs

## Directions

1. Before frying it is necessary to give it a boil to desalt it
2. Then cut into slices and thin strips and fry so that they do not become hardened or too toasted
3. Serve with slices of bread fried in the same substance, with a tomato sauce or with fried eggs
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Jelatina de Almendras (Almond Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four ounces of almonds
- Milk
- Lemon peel
- Cinnamon stick
- One ounce of isinglass
- Water

## Directions

1. Peel four ounces of almonds and sprinkle with a little milk in a pot with lemon peel and whole cinnamon
2. Boil briefly
3. In another pot, place the almonds without straining with two cups of milk and bring to a slow boil
4. Place one ounce of isinglass at the edge of the fire with a little water to dissolve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Jelatina de Patas (Calf's Foot Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two calf's feet
- Two pounds of sugar
- Juice of five or six lemons
- Ten eggs with shells
- Half a glass of sherry wine

## Directions

1. Cook the calf's feet well until the meat separates from the bone
2. Strain and when cold, remove the fat by passing a cloth through it
3. Bring to a boil with two pounds of sugar, lemon juice, and ten eggs (with shells) broken into pieces
4. Leave everything quiet on the fire until the egg is well cooked
5. Add half a glass of sherry wine and remove from heat
6. Pour through a flannel cloth strainer over a mold that must be very dry
7. If it does not come out clear, pour back through the flannel
8. If it cools and cannot finish straining, put everything back on the fire to clarify and pour into the flannel again
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Kuchen con Almendras (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 1/2 pound powdered sugar
- 2 ounces bitter almonds reduced to very fine paste with a little egg white
- 4 whole eggs
- zest of 1 lemon
- 2 ounces flour

## Directions

1. Beat all ingredients together for half an hour
2. Then add 2 ounces of flour
3. Place small pieces of this dough on sheets of paper buttered and placed on baking tins
4. Top with almonds cut lengthwise
5. Bake in a gentle oven for half an hour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lamb Blood (Sangre de Carnero)
body:
## Ingredients

- Onions
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lamb blood
- Oil

## Directions

1. Cut onions lengthwise in abundance and season with salt and pepper
2. When the lamb is slaughtered, stir the blood and mix it with the onion
3. Put everything in a roasting pan and place in the oven
4. When well toasted, remove and it can be eaten hot or cold
5. Alternatively, if not using the oven, roast the pan over strong fire with coals on the lid
6. Or heat oil in a pan and when the blood is freshly drawn, add it by spoonfuls until it forms like fritters, serve with pepper and salt
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lamb Breast (Pecho de Carnero)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb breast
- bacon strips
- onions
- bay leaf
- parsley
- thyme
- salt
- broth
- butter
- bread crumbs
- pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare a pot with bacon strips
2. Place the meat on top and cover with more bacon strips
3. Add three onions in pieces, bay leaf, parsley, thyme, salt and a plate of broth
4. Leave on low heat for three hours, with heat also from above
5. It can be served two ways: first way - brown with melted butter, soak with bread crumbs, sprinkle with pepper and salt, place on the grill and serve with spicy sauce; second way - remove the sauce and present garnished with endive, celery or other vegetables
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lamb Feet Gallineta Style (Patitas de Carnero a la Gallineta)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb feet
- Salt
- Water
- Chicken sauce

## Directions

1. Scald and clean the lamb feet carefully
2. Remove the large bones
3. Cook in salted water for four or five hours
4. When tender and the bones come loose easily, they are cooked
5. Make a chicken sauce and let the feet simmer in it for about half an hour to take on flavor
6. Thicken the sauce and serve very hot
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lamb Feet or Testicles in Locro (Patitas o Criadillas en Locro)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb feet or testicles
- Locro ingredients

## Directions

1. Parboil the lamb feet or testicles
2. Cook them with a locro as for shellfish
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lamb Feet or Testicles with Rice (Patitas o Criadillas con Arroz)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb feet or testicles
- Onion
- Grease
- Rice
- Eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. Parboil the lamb feet or testicles
2. Fry a little onion in two spoonfuls of burned grease
3. Add the rice and fry it a little
4. Add the lamb feet or testicles with mashed eggs
5. Add plenty of water and let it boil
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lamb Liver (Hígado de Carnero)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb liver
- broth (boiling)

## Directions

1. When the broth is boiling, add the liver cut into thin and long slices
2. Prepare it the same way as the lungs
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lamb Offal (Menudillos de Carnero)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb head
- liver
- lungs
- feet
- heart
- salt
- hot water
- spicy sauce or preferred sauce
- two egg yolks
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the jaws and snout from the head
2. Cut the offal into slices
3. Drain and wash well with hot water
4. Place in a pot and cook with salt
5. Just before serving, drain them
6. Except for the head, add to the rest a spicy sauce or preferred sauce made with two beaten egg yolks, a little butter and lemon juice
7. Arrange in a serving platter with the head in the middle
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lamb Shoulder Roasted (Espaldilla de Carnero Asada)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb shoulder
- bacon slices
- meat scraps
- carrots
- onion
- herbs bouquet
- broth

## Directions

1. Break the bone on top with a blade
2. Cook on a spit prepared with bacon slices
3. Add meat scraps, carrots, onion and a bouquet of herbs
4. Pour broth over it
5. Serve with the reduced cooking liquid, or over endives or other vegetables with the desired seasoning
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lamb Tongue (Lengua de Carnero)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb tongue
- bacon
- oil
- parsley
- bay leaf
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Blanch the tongues and add with thin bacon
2. When half cooked, remove the skin
3. Continue cooking for half an hour over low heat
4. Serve hot with a sauce made of oil, parsley, bay leaf, pepper and vinegar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lamb Tongue on the Grill (Lengua de Carnero en Parrilla)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb tongue (cooked as described above)
- onions
- butter
- flour
- broth or white wine
- parsley
- scallions
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut cooked tongues into slices
2. Slice two or three onions and sauté in butter with a handful of flour
3. Soak in broth or white wine
4. Add finely chopped parsley and scallions, salt, pepper and lemon juice
5. Place on the grill until done
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Langostas o Camarones con Aceite o Vinagre (Lobsters or Shrimp with Oil or Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster or shrimp
- onion
- tomato
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. It is advisable that for this dish the shellfish be as fresh as possible
2. Parboil so the shells loosen and peel them
3. Place in a serving dish with onion slices, tomato, whole pepper, bread crumbs, all in small amounts
4. When they have boiled, add oil and vinegar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche Apina (Seething Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sugar
- twenty-four egg yolks
- flavoring to taste
- milk

## Directions

1. Beat one pound of sugar with twenty-four egg yolks until they are well thickened with the flavorings desired
2. Pour into a regular-sized pastry pan and fill with milk
3. Place at the oven mouth stirring until it is well heated
4. Then push it inside the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche Asada (Baked Milk Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 24 egg yolks
- milk
- desired flavorings

## Directions

1. Beat 24 egg yolks with 1 pound sugar until well thickened with desired flavorings
2. Pour everything into a regular-sized pastry pan and fill with milk
3. Place at the oven mouth, stirring until well heated
4. Push into the oven and leave until baked
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche Borracha (Drunken Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- sponge cakes
- cognac or sherry
- milk
- sugar
- lemon peel
- vanilla
- flour
- six egg yolks

## Directions

1. Arrange a platter with sponge cakes and moisten with cognac or sherry
2. Cook a little milk with sugar, lemon peel and vanilla
3. Thicken with a little flour dissolved in milk
4. When cooked, remove and cool for a moment
5. Add six egg yolks and place on the fire
6. When the eggs are cooked, pour over the sponge cakes
7. Let rest and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche con Claras (Milk with Egg Whites)
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of milk
- cinnamon
- vanilla
- lemon peel
- sugar
- eight egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook three cups of milk with cinnamon, vanilla, lemon peel and sugar as for manjar blanco
2. Let boil for a good while until the milk begins to thicken
3. Remove, let cool and strain
4. Add eight well-beaten egg whites, stir and place on the fire until it begins to clear
5. Remove and strain
6. Let cool and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche con Vainilla (Milk with Vanilla)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- vanilla
- lemon
- six egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook milk with plenty of sugar, cinnamon, vanilla and lemon
2. When it has boiled well, add six well-beaten egg yolks
3. Stir for a moment and add the beaten egg whites
4. Mix well and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche Cortada (Broken Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- sugar
- eight egg yolks
- cinnamon
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Heat a little milk and sweeten it
2. Beat about eight egg yolks, add one-third to the boiling milk
3. Stir for a moment and add half of the remaining beaten mixture
4. Leave on the fire until it comes to a boil, remove and add the rest of the beaten mixture flavored with cinnamon and lemon peel
5. Pour into a platter and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche de Natillas (Custard Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- one pound of sugar
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- fifteen egg yolks
- four ounces of cornstarch

## Directions

1. Fill a regular-sized platter well with raw milk
2. Cook and cool it
3. Add one pound of sugar, lemon peel, and cinnamon and boil a little
4. When lukewarm, add fifteen egg yolks, stirring gently, and four ounces of cornstarch dissolved in milk
5. Place on the fire stirring constantly
6. Let cool and garnish with the same milk reduced to a thicker consistency
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche Dorada (Golden Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- five glasses of cooked milk
- lemon peel
- cinnamon sticks
- ground cinnamon
- sugar to taste
- two tablespoons of chuño
- ten egg yolks
- ten egg whites

## Directions

1. Bring five glasses of cooked milk to a boil with lemon peel, cinnamon sticks, and a little ground cinnamon; sugar to taste
2. After boiling for a while, add two tablespoons of chuño dissolved in milk
3. When cooked, remove and let cool
4. Add ten well-beaten egg yolks
5. Place on the fire to cook the eggs
6. Remove, let cool for a moment, add ten beaten egg whites
7. Pour into a platter and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche en Budinera (Milk in a Mold)
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty egg yolks
- three egg whites
- half a pound of sugar
- cinnamon
- one-third mold of cooked and cold milk
- caramelized sugar

## Directions

1. Add three egg whites at half beat to twenty egg yolks, half a pound of sugar, a little cinnamon, and about one-third mold of cooked and cold milk
2. Have the mold ready with caramelized sugar so it does not stick
3. Place in boiling water
4. To know if it is cooked, insert a small feather which should come out dry
5. To remove it from the mold let it cool
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche en Fuente (Milk in a Platter)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- six ounces of sugar
- vanilla
- one pound of bread crumb
- a piece of butter
- two whole eggs
- four egg yolks
- four egg whites

## Directions

1. Heat milk with six ounces of sugar and vanilla and bring to four boils
2. Add one pound of bread crumb to the hot milk
3. Drain and cool
4. Pass through a cloth and grind a piece of vanilla with a little strained milk and combine with the rest of the milk
5. Add a piece of butter, two whole eggs and four egg yolks
6. When combined, strain
7. Pour all into a platter and beat the four egg whites, stir and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche en Pocilio (Milk in a Cup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two egg yolks
- one egg white
- one cup of milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil milk with sugar, cinnamon and vanilla
2. Strain and mix with two half-boiled egg yolks and one egg white that have been beaten
3. Cook in cups in a pan with water
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche Frita (Fried Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- two bottles of milk
- four ounces of sifted rice flour
- sugar to taste
- three ounces of good grated cheese
- five egg yolks
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- lemon zest
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Bring everything to a boil until it reaches the consistency of mazamorra, stirring constantly so the rice does not settle
2. Remove from heat and add three ounces of good grated cheese
3. Add five beaten egg yolks, cinnamon, nutmeg, and lemon zest, stirring for some time until all is incorporated
4. Spread on a platter to cool
5. Cut into pieces that are dipped in well-beaten egg yolks and rolled in breadcrumbs
6. Fry
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leche Nevada (Snowy Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- egg whites
- egg yolks
- milk
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat egg whites as for meringues and add them to boiling milk to cook
2. Beat egg yolks well in the same manner and add a little milk and the necessary sugar dissolved in the same milk
3. When the whites are cooked, remove them and add the beaten yolk mixture
4. Boil this gently over low heat, not stirring but moving the milk from side to side
5. It should not be very thick
6. Place in a platter with a little cinnamon on top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lechugas Rellenas (Stuffed Lettuce)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuces
- salt
- water
- filling (minced)

## Directions

1. Remove the stalks at the base of the leaves
2. Wash them
3. Put them to cook in salted water
4. When half-cooked, remove them carefully and let them drain on a board, squeezing them one by one
5. Place filling about the size of an egg in the middle
6. With the same minced filling, gather the leaves from one side to the other so that they turn toward the stalk
7. Place them loosely in a vessel
8. Cook them with broth from the bones of the minced meat or from the stew
9. Finally make them boil with a hazelnut sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leg of Lamb English Style (Pierna de Carnero a la Inglesa)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb leg
- linen cloth
- bay leaves
- thyme
- spices
- pepper
- water
- caper sauce

## Directions

1. Wrap in linen with bay leaves, thyme, spices and pepper
2. Place in boiling water
3. Cook as many hours as the leg weighs in pounds
4. Remove and unwrap
5. Serve with caper sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leg of Lamb Stewed with Hare (Pierna de Cordero Guisada con Liebre)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leg of lamb
- onion pieces
- bay leaves
- cloves
- whole pepper
- red wine (one bottle)
- vinegar (one glass)
- butter
- fine herbs for larding
- potatoes
- cooked potatoes
- bread crumbs
- eggs
- nutmeg
- chuño (potato starch)

## Directions

1. Cut the tip of the lamb leg to give it a round shape
2. Remove the fat and skin
3. Pound it well and rub with salt on all sides
4. Place in a deep dish with onion pieces, bay leaves, two cloves, whole pepper, a bottle of red wine and a glass of vinegar
5. Leave for three days entirely in this marinade, well covered, turning it from side to side occasionally
6. Remove from marinade and lard finely on both sides
7. Place a piece of butter in a pot and put the leg there just to take a nice color
8. Add the same marinade gradually with a little hot water
9. Leave to boil until cooked, basting occasionally with the sauce
10. Remove the meat and strain the sauce and thicken with a little chuño
11. Place the meat in a dish and sauce on top, surrounded by potato balls
12. For the balls: mix cooked grated potatoes, bread crumbs, eggs and nutmeg
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leg of Lamb with Flour Sauce (Pierna con Salsa de Harina)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb leg
- butter
- flour
- broth from the leg
- celery
- parsley
- onion heads
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Melt a tablespoon of butter until it takes a dark color
2. Dissolve a tablespoon of flour in it
3. Gradually add the broth from the leg to make a regular sauce
4. Cook the leg with celery branches, parsley and onion heads
5. Fry thin slices of potatoes in butter or fat
6. Combine everything and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Leg of Mutton English Style (Pierna de Carnero a la Inglesa)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leg of mutton
- Linen cloth
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Spices
- Pepper
- Water
- Capers

## Directions

1. Wrap the leg of mutton in linen cloth with bay leaves, thyme, spices and pepper
2. Place it in boiling water and cook for as many hours as pounds the leg weighs
3. Remove and unwrap it
4. Serve with a caper sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lengua Asada (Roasted Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- paper

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue
2. Spread butter on it and roll in breadcrumbs
3. Wrap in paper and place in the oven to brown
4. Serve with pebre or any other vegetable stew
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lengua con Salsa de Alcaparras (Tongue with Capers Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- capers sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue
2. Fry in butter with breadcrumbs
3. Serve with capers sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lengua de Vaca a la Marinera (Beef Tongue Sailor-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- broth
- two glasses of red or white wine
- small onions
- butter
- flour
- fried bread

## Directions

1. After cooking the tongue, remove the skin
2. Cut into wheels and strain the broth in which it was cooked, skim off fat and put everything in a casserole with two glasses of red or white wine, small onions fried in butter with a little flour
3. When the sauce is reduced to its point, place on pieces of fried bread, pouring the sauce on top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lengua de Vaca al Gratín (Beef Tongue Gratinéed)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- broth
- vinegar
- pickles
- parsley
- small onion
- chervil
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Roast the tongue and then cut into thin wheels
2. In a plate or platter where it is to be served, place a little broth, a dash of vinegar, pickles, parsley, a small onion and chervil, all finely chopped and seasoned with salt, pepper and a little breadcrumbs
3. Layer the wheels on top, and scatter the same condiments on each layer, covering everything with breadcrumbs
4. Place the plate in an oven at low heat and let it cook until it browns on the bottom
5. Serve pouring a little broth over it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lengua de Vaca en Papel (Beef Tongue in Paper)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- butter
- bay leaf
- thyme
- breadcrumbs
- pepper
- salt
- paper

## Directions

1. Cook and cut the tongue into more or less thick pieces
2. Cover with butter mixed with bay leaf and thyme, breadcrumbs, pepper and salt
3. Then cover with paper spread with butter
4. Toast over gentle heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lengua de Vaca Mechada y en Salsa (Beef Tongue Larded in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- bacon strips
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- brown sauce or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Soak in cold water for twenty-four hours, changing the water several times
2. Scald in boiling water, scraping to remove the skin
3. Lard with bacon strips, seasoning with pepper, salt and chopped parsley
4. Let it cook slowly for about four or five hours
5. Remove from heat, open it down the middle without dividing it and place on the plate
6. Remove the fat from the cooking liquid and soak in a brown sauce or tomato sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lengua Encebollada (Tongue with Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- onions
- tomato
- corn
- broth

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue and cut into thin slices
2. Fry until browned
3. Separately fry also until browned: onions, tomato and chopped corn
4. Mix everything with the tongue and add a little broth
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lengua Habanera (Havana-Style Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- salt
- parsley
- small onions
- flour
- two glasses of good white wine
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Clean the tongue well in hot water, remove the skin and cut into wheels
2. Cook in a casserole with salt
3. When it is seen to be cooked, strain the broth and add it back with the tongue
4. Add a little chopped parsley, small onions fried in lard with a little flour, two glasses of good white wine and wheels of potatoes
5. Season with salt, let it cook for a while
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lengua Rellena (Stuffed Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- ground meat
- breadcrumbs
- eggs
- butter
- salt
- grated lemon
- capers sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue until very tender
2. Split it lengthwise in half
3. Make a mixture of ground meat, breadcrumbs, eggs, butter, salt and grated lemon
4. Place this mixture on one half of the tongue and cover with the other half
5. Roll in egg and breadcrumbs
6. Pour melted butter on it and place in the oven
7. Serve with capers sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lenguado a la Gratín (Sole Gratinée)
body:
## Ingredients

- sole
- butter
- flour
- parsley
- onions
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- white wine
- brandy
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the sole well
2. Put butter kneaded with flour in the serving dish to be used for cooking
3. Add to this mixture parsley, onions and mushrooms, all finely chopped and seasoned with salt and pepper
4. Place the fish on top, putting another layer equal to the one below and surrounding it with half a glass of broth, white wine or one tablespoon of brandy
5. Cover the dish and place in the oven
6. Can also be topped and bottomed with bread crumbs and put fire on the lid so it sticks to the bottom of the dish, which is the Gratinée preparation
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lenguado Frito (Fried Sole)
body:
## Ingredients

- sole
- milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Clean and empty, dip in milk, coat with flour on both sides and put in very hot oil
2. Cover the serving dish with a napkin, place the sole on it with whole lemons
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lenguado Relleno (Stuffed Sole)
body:
## Ingredients

- sole
- flour
- butter
- parsley
- onion
- pepper
- nutmeg
- vinegar

## Directions

1. After cleaning well, make a lengthwise cut along the spine next to the dark part
2. Coat with flour and fry
3. Remove the spine and introduce the following filling: one tablespoon of butter kneaded with chopped parsley and onion, ground pepper, nutmeg and vinegar
4. Cook all this and place the fish in it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lenguados Asados (Roasted Sole)
body:
## Ingredients

- sole
- butter
- fine herbs
- salt
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- mustard
- white wine
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put in a pot a sauce of butter and fine herbs, salt and chopped pepper
2. Place the sole on top covered with a light layer of grated bread crust and a little melted mustard
3. Pour a little white wine and place in the oven over low heat
4. When done, remove and serve with lemon juice
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lentejas a la Ama de Casa (Lentils Housewife Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lentils
- Salt
- Water
- Butter
- Parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fried bread crust

## Directions

1. Cook the lentils in slightly salted water
2. Drain and place in a pan with a good piece of butter, chopped parsley, salt, and pepper
3. Sauté for several minutes
4. Place in the dish garnished with fried bread crust and serve very hot
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lentejas con Perejil (Lentils with Parsley)
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- water
- salt
- abundant butter
- chopped parsley
- pepper
- fried bread crusts

## Directions

1. Cook the lentils in water and salt
2. Drain them
3. Put them in a saucepan with abundant butter, chopped parsley, salt and a little pepper
4. Stir for a few minutes
5. Serve with fried bread crusts, all very hot
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lentejas con Perejil (Lentils with Parsley)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lentils
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Parsley
- Pepper
- Fried bread crusts

## Directions

1. Cook in water and salt
2. Drain and place in a pan with abundant butter, chopped parsley, salt, and a little pepper
3. Sauté for several minutes and serve with fried bread crusts, all very hot
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Linchen de Chocolate (Chocolate Meringue)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg whites
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 4 ounces finely grated chocolate
- a little vanilla powder

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites and powdered sugar as for meringues
2. Add 4 ounces of very finely grated chocolate and a little vanilla powder
3. With a spoon, place portions of this dough on buttered baking tins
4. Bake in a gentle oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Linchen de los Israelitas (Israelite Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces butter
- 5 eggs
- 3/4 pound sugar
- 3/4 pound flour
- zest of 1/2 lemon
- 1 egg yolk
- ground almonds

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and mix with 5 eggs and 3/4 pound sugar and beat a little
2. Then add 3/4 pound flour and the zest of 1/2 lemon
3. Butter the mold and place the dough inside
4. Brush the top with 1 egg yolk mixed with a little ground almonds mixed with sugar and place on top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lobes de Carnero (Lamb Lobes)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb lobes
- lard
- salt
- tomato
- water
- spices

## Directions

1. After boiling, cut into small slices and reheat with lard and salt, being careful to skim frequently
2. Then fry with tomato, and before serving, stew with warm water and spices
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Locro para Langostas, Camarones, Jaivas, Ostiones y Erizos (Locro for Lobsters, Shrimp, Crabs, Oysters and Sea Urchins)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- carrots
- beans
- squash
- peas
- chard
- corn
- shrimp
- sea urchins
- oysters
- lobsters

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes, carrots, beans, squash, peas, chopped chard and sliced corn
2. When all is cooked, drain the water, fry in color and add trimmed corn so it fries as well
3. For shrimp add the broth in which they have already been cooked, also add the bodies so they boil with everything
4. When serving add the tails and if the locro has not become thick enough, mash some potatoes
5. For sea urchins prepare in their own broth and the tongues are not added until serving time
6. For other shellfish prepare the locro in water
7. Note that oysters and lobsters should be soaked from the day before and the water changed frequently
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lomo (Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of loin
- half tablespoon of butter or color
- breadcrumbs
- broth
- pepper
- salt
- bay leaves
- half glass of wine

## Directions

1. Place a piece of loin in a casserole with half a tablespoon of butter or color and let it brown
2. Add a little breadcrumbs, broth, pepper, salt, bay leaves and half a glass of wine
3. Simmer until tender
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lomo con Salsa (Loin with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of meat
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- celery sprigs
- parsley
- large onion
- butter or color
- flour
- vinegar
- broth
- French mustard

## Directions

1. Cook a piece of meat with salt, pepper, vinegar, celery sprigs and parsley
2. Cut a large onion into slices and fry in butter or color until well fried and golden
3. Add a spoonful of flour and another of vinegar and a cup of broth
4. Let it cook
5. At the time of placing it on the meat, add a spoonful of French mustard diluted in broth
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lomo con Salsa de Callampas (Loin with Mushroom Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- celery sprigs
- parsley
- whole pepper
- mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the loin with celery sprigs, parsley and whole pepper
2. Serve with mushroom sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lomo con Salsa de Nuez (Loin with Walnut Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- walnut
- soaked bread
- loin broth

## Directions

1. Peel the walnut, drain it and grind it with soaked bread
2. Cook it in the broth of the loin which should already be cooked
3. When the sauce is ready, pour it over the loin and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lomo con Tomate (Loin with Tomato)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- broth
- fat
- soaked bread
- red tomatoes

## Directions

1. Cook the loin well
2. With a spoon remove all the fat from the top of the broth in which it has been cooked
3. Place this fat in a casserole and add soaked bread and some red tomatoes ground and placed with the bread
4. Mix together and gradually add the broth
5. Place the loin in this sauce and let it rest
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lomo con Tomate (Loin with Tomato)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- fat from cooking broth
- soaked bread
- red tomatoes, ground
- cooking broth

## Directions

1. Cook the loin well
2. With a spoon, remove all the fat from the top of the broth in which it has been cooked
3. Put this fat in a saucepan and add soaked bread and some ground red tomatoes mixed with the bread
4. Gradually add the broth little by little
5. Place the loin in this sauce and let it rest
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lomo Guisado (Braised Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- thick end of loin
- thin strips of bacon
- butter
- four carrots
- four onions
- parsley sprigs
- celery sprigs
- two bay leaves
- oregano
- salt
- two glasses of water
- whole pepper
- flour
- water

## Directions

1. Take the thickest end of a loin and cut thin strips of bacon
2. Put them on one side of the loin and arrange it in a saucepan buttered, making sure the side with the bacon goes down
3. Add four carrots, four onions, parsley and celery sprigs, two bay leaves, a little oregano, salt, two glasses of water and some whole pepper
4. If the piece of meat is of regular size, boil for three hours
5. When the loin is somewhat softened, increase the heat to reduce the sauce
6. A little before serving, remove the carrots, onions, pieces of bacon and meat
7. Dissolve a spoonful of flour in the broth and add a glass of water
8. Let this sauce cook and pour it over the meat which should be arranged on a platter with carrots and onions
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lomo Mechado (Larded Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- bacon strips
- potatoes
- onions
- pepper
- salt
- celery branches
- parsley
- cup of brandy

## Directions

1. Lard the loin with bacon strips and place in a casserole with potatoes, onions, pepper, salt, celery branches, parsley and a cup of brandy
2. Cover and bring to a boil
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lomo Mechado (Larded Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- strips of bacon
- potatoes
- onions
- pepper
- salt
- celery sprigs
- parsley
- a glass of brandy
- broth

## Directions

1. Lard the loin with strips of bacon
2. Place it in a saucepan with potatoes, onions, pepper, salt, celery sprigs, and parsley
3. Add a glass of brandy
4. Cover and bring to boil
5. If it becomes too dry, add a small cup of broth
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Lomos Salpresos (Preserved Loins)
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- salt
- chili
- cumin
- garlic
- oregano

## Directions

1. Season a regular vinegar with salt, chili, cumin, garlic and a little oregano
2. Place the loins there for two days
3. Remove and place in a heavier press than that used for guachalomos
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Magdalenas (Magdalenas)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ounces butter
- 6 eggs
- 12 ounces powdered sugar
- 1 pound flour
- zest of lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the butter with the eggs and sugar for half an hour
2. Then add the zest of 1 lemon and the flour
3. When incorporated, place the dough 1/2 finger thick on a bordered baking tin
4. Dust with sugar and place in a hot oven for half an hour
5. Immediately after removing from the oven, cut into pieces and let cool on the same tin
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Malayas Arrolladas (Rolled Malayas)
body:
## Ingredients

- malayas
- salt
- vinegar
- cumin
- oregano
- garlic
- chili
- water
- string

## Directions

1. Season these in the same seasoning as for guachalomos
2. Remove and air dry
3. Roll by tying with string
4. Cook in seasoned water and serve cold
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Masa de Empanadas (Empanada Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds flour
- two pounds cow butter
- one pound lard
- six egg yolks
- sufficient amount of very hot water

## Directions

1. Take four pounds flour, two pounds cow butter and one pound lard
2. Skim well before incorporating them into the flour
3. Add six egg yolks and sufficient amount of very hot water
4. Knead for a long time and roll many times
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Masa de Hojas (Puff Pastry Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef lard
- flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Take one pound of beef lard per pound of flour. Mix the dough very soft by adding cold water.
2. Flatten with the rolling pin until it is the thickness of a silver coin. Coat the surface well with beef lard, fold in half, pass the rolling pin again and continue.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Masa de Hojas (Puff Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound cow butter
- one pound flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Take one pound cow butter per pound of flour
2. Mix the dough very soft, adding cold water
3. Flatten with the roller until it reaches the thickness of a strong peso
4. Coat the surface well with cow butter
5. Fold in half and pass the roller over it again
6. Coat with butter and flour as many times as desired to give the dough layers. The minimum is three
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Masa Dura (Hard Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- beef lard
- cold water
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of flour with slightly less beef lard, add a little cold water and sufficient salt.
2. Knead with the rolling pin and make sure it comes out very hard.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Masa Dura (Hard Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- slightly less cow butter
- a little cold water
- sufficient amount of salt

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of flour with slightly less cow butter
2. Add a little cold water and sufficient amount of salt
3. Knead with the roller and ensure it becomes very hard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Masa para los Pasteles de Hoja (Dough for Puff Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- egg yolks
- water

## Directions

1. Take one pound of butter and one pound of flour and four egg yolks. Take half of the flour and form a small mound, place the yolks in the middle; a little water and a small piece of butter that is not salted, join all this together and make a very soft dough.
2. Beat it well with the hand until it becomes very smooth. Add a little flour, knead well and roll it out, fold in three times.
3. Then divide the butter into three parts, spread the dough and add the butter in small pieces, sprinkle with flour, fold, and roll out with the rolling pin. Repeat this three times. Use a very hot oven.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Masa para Pasteles de Hoja (Dough for Leaf Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds flour
- 2 egg whites
- salt
- sufficient water
- 1 pound butter

## Directions

1. To 1.5 pounds flour add 2 egg whites, salt, and sufficient water, being careful that it does not become too soft
2. Mix everything and knead for a while
3. Take 1 pound of butter and squeeze it with your hand in pieces to remove all the water
4. Join them together and make a cake of it and place on a cloth to dry it more
5. Roll out the dough until it is the size of the butter cake and place on top, leaving a small border of dough around it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Mayonesa (Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight to ten egg yolks
- oil
- two tablespoons of oil
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat eight to ten egg yolks for a regular serving dish
2. Add a little oil and continue beating
3. When combined, add two tablespoons of oil
4. Beat well for about three hours, adding two tablespoons of oil from time to time until you calculate about half a bottle of regular size
5. Once the beating is done and by taste you can see that the oil is cooked, add vinegar to taste
6. Stir well and add the necessary salt
7. Arrange a dish with lettuce leaves, tender radishes and pieces of celery opened in water
8. In the middle place preserved shellfish or some cold roasted poultry
9. Pour the beaten mixture on top of everything and cover with lettuce hearts, radishes and celery
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Mayonesa Alternativa (Alternative Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- seven hard egg yolks
- two raw egg yolks
- half a bottle of oil
- salt
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Dissolve seven hard egg yolks with a spoon and unite them with two raw egg yolks
2. Add half a bottle of oil little by little until they are well beaten
3. Add salt and vinegar to taste
4. If it breaks add a little vinegar and a splash of water until the broken appearance is removed
5. Arrange as the previous recipe
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Melados de Leche con Almendras (Honeyed Milk with Almonds)
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk
- Cinnamon
- Vanilla
- Lemon zest
- Sugar
- Almonds

## Directions

1. Make an infusion in a little boiling milk with cinnamon, vanilla, and a piece of lemon zest
2. Add the milk necessary for two pounds of sugar
3. Also add the juice of half a pound of almonds, which have been ground with the same milk
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Melindres de Almendras (Almond Wafers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces ground almonds
- 1/2 shell of very thick quince paste
- grated lemon
- ground cinnamon
- ground clove
- 1 pound refined powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together and make a small paste
2. Place on a table with powder on top and bottom
3. Roll out and cut to about the thickness of a peso coin
4. Pinch the shape you want to cut on one side
5. When they are aired, place them in a hot oven on papers and a board
6. After they are cooked and browned on one side, remove and let cool
7. Turn them and brown again
8. Put together in pairs and fill with desired sweet
9. When the filling is aired, dust with powder
10. These wafers can also be made with 2 more ounces of almonds and 1 egg white
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Merengues al Gusto de Roque (Roque's Meringues)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg whites
- 4 ounces powdered sugar
- 4 ounces sweet almonds made into paste

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg whites with 4 ounces powdered sugar, evaporating everything over hot ash and stirring constantly
2. Add 4 ounces of sweet almonds made into paste
3. Once the mixture is done, form a round or oval meringue the size of a spoon, being careful to leave a hollow in the middle of each one
4. Dust with very fine sugar and place in the oven
5. When they are rising, remove them and place inside whipped cream or preserves to cover with the other half
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Mermelada de Damasco (Apricot Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots
- Equal quantity of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the apricots in water
2. When cooked, remove and pass through a sieve
3. Mix with an equal quantity of powdered sugar
4. Put over the fire and cook until it reaches the point of setting
5. You know it is ready when a little placed on a plate does not run
6. This jam keeps well for a long time and can be used in frozen desserts
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Morcillas de Sal Americanas (American Salt Blood Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe
- cooked pork blood
- spices
- pieces of fat pork meat
- salt water
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the tripe to be filled, tying it at the point with small strings.
2. Fill with cooked pork blood mixed with ground and toasted spices, adding some small pieces of fat pork cut into very small pieces.
3. Mix everything together and after filling, tie another string at the mouth.
4. Boil in salted water and when done, remove and cut into pieces.
5. Fry and add fried tomato sauce.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Orejas de Chancho a la Leonesa (Pork Ears Leonese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork ears
- onions
- lard
- flour
- broth
- vinegar or lemon juice
- fried croutons (optional)

## Directions

1. In a sauce of onions chopped and sautéed in lard, place the ears cut into strips.
2. Add a little flour, moisten with broth and reduce.
3. Serve adding a few drops of vinegar or lemon juice, and if desired with fried croutons.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Orejas de Chancho con Pan Rayado, Asadas en Parrilla (Pork Ears with Bread Crumbs, Grilled on the Grill)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork ears
- bread crumbs
- eggs
- clear juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Place over hot coals after passing through the flame, washed, cleaned and blanched.
2. When tender and cold, coat in bread crumbs, dip in beaten eggs, pass again through bread crumbs and grill on the grill.
3. Serve dry or with clear juice.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ostras a la Pollada (Oysters Prepared with Chicken Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters without shells
- Beef butter
- Flour
- Mushrooms
- Parsley
- Broth from the pot
- Salt
- Black peppercorns
- Lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Blanch the oysters in boiling water
2. Pass them to cold water and drain well
3. In a saucepan, place a piece of beef butter mixed with flour, mushrooms and minced parsley
4. Turn them a few times on the fire
5. Moisten with broth from the pot and cook this sauce
6. When sufficiently reduced, place the oysters in it
7. Season with salt and black peppercorns
8. A moment before serving, add lemon juice or at least vinegar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ostras Asadas (Roasted Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters with their liquid
- Piquant sauce
- Oil
- Parsley
- Flour
- White wine
- Broth
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the shell and reheat for a few minutes in the water they contain
2. Drain and place in piquant sauce
3. Reheat in oil and parsley
4. Add a spoonful of flour
5. Add white wine and broth, letting it reduce until little sauce remains
6. Pour a little lemon juice over them
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ostras sin Concha (Oysters without Shell)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large shells
- Oysters
- Minced oysters and corresponding sauce
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Choose large shells
2. In each shell place four or six oysters with their minced mixture and corresponding sauce
3. Cover with breadcrumbs
4. Place on the grill and serve roasted
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Otras Guindas en Aguardiente (Other Sour Cherries in Spirits)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherries
- Cherry pits
- Spirits
- 2 pounds sugar per 1 pound guindas

## Directions

1. Crush cherries, even the pits; place them in a pot and cover with spirits and let them infuse for one week.
2. Strain them, filter and in this liquid add whole cherries and sugar: for every pound of guindas, add 2 pounds of sugar.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Otras Guindas en Aguardiente (Sour Cherries in Brandy)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherries, with pits
- Brandy (aguardiente)
- Whole cherries
- Sugar: two pounds per one pound of cherries

## Directions

1. Crush the cherries, including the pits
2. Place them in an earthenware pot and cover with brandy
3. Leave to infuse for one week
4. Strain and press out the liquid
5. In this liquid, add whole cherries and sugar (two pounds of sugar per one pound of cherries)
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Otro (Another Fried Fish Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- parsley sprigs
- bread
- chili pepper
- pepper
- salt
- broth

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the fish and fry it
2. Make a sauce from parsley sprigs, bread soaked and ground, half a hot or sweet chili pepper, pepper or salt
3. Fry all this and add a little broth
4. Pass through a sieve and pour over the fried fish
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Otro de Acelgas (Another Chard Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- chard
- carrots
- chickpeas
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Cook the chard with carrots and chickpeas
2. Chop them well and fry them in butter or lard
3. Add a little broth or lard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Otro Guiso de Pescado Seco (Another Dried Fish Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- pescado seco (dried fish)
- aceite (oil)
- mantequilla (butter)
- ajo (garlic)
- cebollas (onions)
- perejil (parsley)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- dos yemas de huevo (two egg yolks)
- caldo (broth)

## Directions

1. Prepare as above.
2. Put equal amounts of oil and butter in a pot with one minced garlic clove, onions, and parsley all finely chopped.
3. When half-fried, add the fish in pieces and a little vinegar.
4. Once everything is well fried, add water and let it cook.
5. When ready to serve, beat two egg yolks and cook them in a little fish broth.
6. Pour this mixture over the top, being careful it does not boil so the egg does not curdle.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Otro Plato Delicioso (Another Delicious Duck Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fat duck
- Large whole peeled potatoes
- Butter
- Lemon juice
- Bread crumbs
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Celery, finely chopped
- Broth

## Directions

1. Place a fat duck in a casserole
2. Add whole peeled potatoes, a piece of butter, lemon juice, bread crumbs, and finely chopped parsley and celery
3. Cover with broth
4. Cover the casserole well and place over low heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Otros Huevos Rellenos (Other Stuffed Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard boiled eggs
- bread crumb soaked in cream
- butter
- egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- fish
- oil

## Directions

1. Harden the eggs and cut in two halves
2. Remove the yolks and crush in a mortar with bread crumb soaked in cream
3. Add a part of butter, one or two yolks, salt and pepper
4. Fill the eggs with this mixture and cover with bread crumb moistened with butter
5. Pour the rest of the filling, which is thinned with a little broth and let it boil
6. It can also be filled with chopped fish and oil is used
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pajaritos Asados (Roasted Small Birds)
body:
## Ingredients

- small birds
- bacon piece
- bread crumb

## Directions

1. Place a piece of bacon on the breast, plenty of bread crumb and put them in the oven or on the grill
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pajaritos Asados (Roasted Small Birds)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small birds
- Bacon
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Place a piece of bacon on the breast of each bird
2. Cover with breadcrumbs
3. Place in the oven or on a grill to roast
4. Serve with some vegetable stew
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pajaritos Fritos (Fried Small Birds)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small birds
- Butter
- Salt
- Thyme
- Pepper
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Pluck the birds
2. Cut off the legs and half of the wings
3. Fry them whole in butter with salt, thyme, pepper, and chopped parsley
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pan de Damas (Ladies' Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds flour
- thirty-five egg whites
- one and a half pounds sugar
- twelve ounces butter
- one ounce bitter almond

## Directions

1. Beat egg whites four at a time until they resemble meringues
2. Add flour, powdered sugar, and butter combined with almond ground as for paste
3. Mix well and pour into buttered molds
4. Bake in a moderate oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pan de Vida (Bread of Life)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces sifted rice flour
- four ounces ground almond
- four ounces powdered sugar
- seven beaten egg whites
- a little milk

## Directions

1. Combine four ounces sifted rice flour, four ounces ground almond, and four ounces powdered sugar
2. Mix with seven beaten egg whites and a little milk
3. Pour into a buttered mold and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Panqueque (Basic Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- fifteen egg yolks
- nine egg whites
- one pound powdered sugar
- one pound butter
- one pound starch powder
- lemon zest
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Place fifteen egg yolks and nine egg whites in an earthenware bowl
2. Beat all with one pound powdered sugar as for cake
3. Take one pound of butter and beat well with a spoon
4. Mix everything and beat again by hand
5. Add one pound starch powder, beating constantly
6. Add lemon zest and cinnamon
7. Pour into molds
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Panqueque de Almendras (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs with shells, weighed
- sugar equal to the weight of the eggs
- five ounces butter
- four ounces almonds
- flour equal to the weight of four whole eggs

## Directions

1. Weigh six eggs without breaking them and measure sugar equal to their weight
2. Combine the butter with the flour and almond
3. Beat the egg yolks well with the sugar
4. Combine all ingredients and add the egg whites beaten separately as for meringues
5. Pour into a buttered mold and place in a not-too-hot oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Panqueque de Canela (Cinnamon Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound butter
- one pound sugar
- twelve eggs
- a small piece of vanilla
- cinnamon
- one pound flour

## Directions

1. Beat one pound butter with one pound sugar until foamy
2. Add twelve beaten eggs
3. Add a small piece of vanilla, cinnamon, and one pound flour
4. Combine all and pour into the mold
5. Bake in an oven as for the previous cakes
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Panqueque de Claras (Egg White Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces well-washed butter
- six ounces powdered sugar
- nine egg whites
- six ounces flour
- lemon zest
- Corinth raisins (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat three ounces well-washed butter with six ounces powdered sugar until foamy
2. Beat nine egg whites as for meringues
3. Beat six ounces flour, a little lemon zest, and Corinth raisins if desired
4. Combine all ingredients
5. Pour into a buttered mold and place in an oven heated as for bread
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Panqueque de Yemas (Egg Yolk Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound melted butter
- ten egg yolks
- one pound powdered sugar
- seven ounces Corinth raisins
- ten egg whites
- one pound flour
- one small cup cognac

## Directions

1. Beat one pound melted butter with ten egg yolks
2. Add one pound powdered sugar
3. Add seven ounces Corinth raisins
4. Add ten egg whites beaten as for meringues
5. Add one pound flour and one small cup cognac
6. Combine all and pour into a buttered mold
7. Place in an oven heated as for bread
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Papas a la Parisién (Potatoes Parisian-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- finely chopped onion
- water
- peeled potatoes cut into small pieces
- pepper
- salt
- mint
- parsley
- tarragon

## Directions

1. Put butter and very finely chopped onion in a saucepan
2. Let it fry until it takes a good color
3. Add a glass of water and the peeled potatoes in small pieces with pepper and salt
4. Add a bundle made of mint, parsley, tarragon, etc.
5. Let everything cook slowly
6. Before serving this dish, remove the bundle
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Papas con Cebollas (Potatoes with Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- cow butter
- potatoes
- broth

## Directions

1. Fry onions in cow butter
2. Cut half-cooked potatoes
3. Finish cooking them in the fried onion
4. Mix with broth from the stew or fish
5. Cook very slowly
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Papas en Salsa (Potatoes in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- potato starch
- butter
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- anchovy or capers (optional)

## Directions

1. Have the cooked and cut potatoes as hot as possible
2. Dissolve potato starch with butter, broth, salt and pepper on low heat
3. As soon as this sauce is sufficiently thickened, add chopped anchovy or capers if desired
4. Dress the potatoes with this sauce
5. Serve as hot as possible
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Papas Estofadas (Braised Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chopped onion
- flour
- meat or fish broth
- wine

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes in water
2. Peel and cut them into slices
3. Put them in a saucepan with butter, salt, pepper, parsley and chopped onion with a little flour
4. Add meat or fish broth and a good glass of wine
5. Serve with little sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pasta para Empanadas, Tortas i Fiambres (Dough for Empanadas, Cakes and Cold Dishes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds flour
- a pound and a half butter
- half ounce salt
- four beaten eggs
- two glasses of water

## Directions

1. Make a dough with two pounds flour, pound and a half butter, half ounce salt, four beaten eggs and two glasses of water
2. Mix it gradually without kneading
3. Stretch with the roller or kneader, like puff pastry
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pastel de Ave (Poultry Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken or pullet
- Butter
- Parsley
- Seasonings
- Salt
- Onion
- Water
- Flour
- Color

## Directions

1. Cut up a chicken or pullet into pieces
2. Heat butter in a pot and place the pieces on top
3. Add parsley, seasonings, salt, and chopped onion
4. When well fried, add about two cups of hot water
5. Let simmer slowly
6. Add a little flour and about four tablespoons of color
7. Arrange in a serving dish
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pastel de Maíz (Corn Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender corn
- corn meal
- burned fat
- sugar
- raisins
- cinnamon
- egg yolks
- cow meat
- olives
- chicken
- hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cut tender corn and grate or grind the corn
2. Put the corn in burned fat until it is cooked
3. Add sugar, raisins, cinnamon and some beaten egg yolks
4. If desired, make a hash of cow meat with raisins, olives, chicken, slices of hard eggs, cinnamon and sugar
5. Empty the hash into the pastry dish or pan, taking care to put plenty of fat on the bottom
6. Then cover with the corn that has been prepared and on top sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
7. Then cover with a sheet, put plenty of fire on top and very little on the bottom
8. Let it rest until it browns well and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pastel de Maíz Seco (Dry Corn Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- butter
- beaten egg
- flour
- sugar
- cinnamon
- raisins
- cow meat
- olives
- chicken
- hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Soak the corn for two days
2. Then grind it on stone, sieve it in a coarse sieve to remove the husk
3. Prepare a little butter and fry the corn until half cooked
4. Then add beaten egg, a little flour, sugar, cinnamon and raisins
5. Prepare the hash as before, assemble the cake and put it to fire in the same manner
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pastel de Pan (Bread Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two large loaves of beer bread
- milk
- six beaten eggs
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter
- cow meat
- raisins
- olives
- chicken
- hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Take two large loaves of beer bread and remove the crumb
2. Soak this crumb in milk
3. Add six beaten eggs, sugar, cinnamon
4. Everything is fried in butter
5. Then prepare the hash as before and assemble the cake, putting it immediately to fire in the same manner
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pastel de Papas (Potato Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- beaten eggs
- flour
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter
- cow meat
- raisins
- olives
- chicken
- hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes and grind them on stone
2. Beat eggs into them, add a little flour, sugar, cinnamon and a little butter
3. Knead everything well until forming a paste with which to cover the cake
4. Prepare the hash as before and use fire in the same manner
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pastel de Pescado (Fish Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish
- Vinegar
- Onion pieces
- Whole peppercorns
- Butter or fat
- Breadcrumbs
- Nutmeg
- Hard-boiled egg
- Broth
- Puff pastry or delicate pastry

## Directions

1. Place fish in vinegar from the day before with onion pieces and whole peppercorns
2. The next day remove the vinegar, remove the bones and mince finely
3. Fry in butter or fat
4. Add breadcrumbs, a little nutmeg, minced hard-boiled egg and sufficient broth so it is not too thin
5. Place in a dish with thin puff pastry or any other delicate pastry
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pasteles de Jaivas (Crab Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion
- Butter
- Crab meat
- Three hard-boiled eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Broth
- Sugar
- Puff pastry
- Crab shells
- Eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Fat

## Directions

1. Mince onion very finely, wash and fry in butter
2. When fried, add the crab meat, three finely minced hard-boiled eggs and breadcrumbs
3. When slightly fried, add broth so it reaches regular thickness and add a little sugar
4. Empty into a dish and cover with puff pastry, place in the oven
5. Alternatively, fill well-washed crab shells, roll in eggs and bread crumbs and fry in very hot fat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pastelitos de Ostras (Oyster Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good piece of butter
- Three egg yolks
- Grated lemon
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- Water from the oysters
- Minced mushrooms
- Capers
- Breadcrumbs
- Piece of roasted veal or cold fowl
- Egg whites
- Broth
- Puff pastry shells
- Oysters
- Lemon juice
- Egg yolk
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Beat butter until it resembles foam
2. Mix with three egg yolks, grated lemon, nutmeg and salt, and the water from the oysters
3. Add minced mushrooms, capers, breadcrumbs and a finely minced piece of roasted veal or cold fowl
4. At the last moment add half the beaten egg whites; if too dry add a little broth
5. Fill uncooked puff pastry shells with this mixture
6. Place three or four oysters on top of each one
7. Brush with a little lemon juice, beaten egg yolk and bread crumbs
