8. Place in the oven, which should be set as for baking bread
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pastelés de Choros (Mussel Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels
- Onion rings
- Olives
- Oil
- Butter
- Coloring
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Whole peppercorns
- Grated cheese
- Breadcrumbs
- Broth from the mussels
- Puff pastry

## Directions

1. Roast the mussels until they release their broth
2. Cook them in the same broth after removing from the shell
3. When cooked, place in a dish with onion rings, olives, a little oil, butter about half a spoonful, a little coloring, sliced hard-boiled eggs and whole peppercorns
4. Over all this a layer of grated cheese and another of breadcrumbs
5. Pour the broth from the mussels over it, place puff pastry on top and put in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Patas de Chancho Fritas (Fried Pork Feet)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork feet
- wine
- water
- pepper
- parsley
- thyme
- bay leaf
- salt
- vinegar
- egg
- flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the bones and cook with one part wine and two parts water.
2. Make a marinade with pepper, parsley, thyme, bay leaf, salt and a little vinegar.
3. If serving this way, serve very hot.
4. To fry them, coat with a batter of beaten egg and flour and a dusting of sugar.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pato (Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Sugar
- Lemon juice
- Cinnamon
- Broth

## Directions

1. After cleaning, leave in open air for twenty-four hours
2. Cook in a pot
3. When half-cooked, drain and place on a roasting spit coated with butter and dusted with salt and pepper
4. Insert cloves into the breast and legs
5. Wrap well in straw paper, tie securely, and place over low heat
6. When well roasted, make a sauce with sugar, lemon juice, cinnamon, pepper, and broth, and pour over the top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pato con Aceitunas (Duck with Olives)
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck, well prepared and arranged
- Bacon pieces
- Half pound olives

## Directions

1. Turn the duck several times in a casserole with bacon pieces
2. Add half a pound of olives to the casserole where the duck is and leave for a few minutes
3. Serve the duck surrounded by olives
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pato con Chorizos (Duck with Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sausages (chorizos)
- Duck
- Broth
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Salt
- Onion
- Clove
- Mushrooms (callampas)
- Flour
- Potatoes (optional)

## Directions

1. Fry sausages alone
2. Cook the duck in broth with pepper, parsley, salt, one onion, and one clove
3. In the fat rendered by the sausages, fry the mushrooms and add a little flour
4. Place the duck in this mixture until it browns slightly
5. Small potatoes may be fried together with the mushrooms
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pato con Legumbres (Duck with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- onion
- celery
- whole pepper
- whole peeled potatoes
- carrot slices
- vinegar

## Directions

1. After washing the duck well, add onion, celery, whole pepper, whole peeled potatoes, carrot slices and vinegar to the duck
2. Cover the pot well and move it from time to time
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pato con Trufas y Callampas (Duck with Truffles and Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour
- Butter
- Broth
- Truffles, finely chopped
- Mushrooms (callampas), finely chopped
- Pepper, ground
- Salt
- Duck, raw and cut into pieces
- Onion heads, cut into small pieces
- Parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Fry a little flour in butter
2. Add broth and when the sauce is cooked and of regular thickness, add finely chopped truffles and mushrooms
3. Season with ground pepper and salt
4. Place raw duck pieces, onion heads cut into small pieces, and chopped parsley with the sauce
5. Bring to a boil, stirring frequently so it does not stick
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pato Delicioso (Delicious Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck
- Onion heads
- Celery sprigs
- Whole pepper
- Whole peeled potatoes
- Carrot slices
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Place well-washed duck in a casserole with onion heads, celery sprigs, whole pepper, whole peeled potatoes, carrot slices, and vinegar
2. Cover well and shake occasionally
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pato en Nabo a la Casera (Duck with Turnips, Country Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck
- Butter
- Turnips
- Flour, one tablespoon
- Broth
- Parsley sprigs
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Place a little butter in the casserole and turn the duck several times, along with some turnips
2. Remove them, then add one tablespoon of flour to the butter and brown this mixture
3. Increase with broth from a pot
4. Return the duck and turnips with parsley sprigs, salt, and pepper
5. If the turnips are already tender, do not add until the duck is nearly cooked
6. Let the stew reduce if necessary and serve everything together
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pato Relleno (Stuffed Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck, boned
- Duck breast meat
- Duck heart
- Duck liver
- Four eggs
- Butter
- Bread crumbs
- Lemon zest
- Beef, finely chopped (optional)
- Milk (optional)
- Extra egg (optional)

## Directions

1. Bone the duck
2. Take meat from the breast, heart, and liver, chop very finely
3. Warm a little butter and beat until foamy
4. Mix four eggs, the chopped meat, bread crumbs, and lemon zest
5. To increase volume, may add finely chopped beef, milk, and another egg
6. Combine everything together
7. Stuff the duck and stitch closed so the filling does not escape
8. Place in oven to roast
9. Make a mushroom sauce, to which chestnuts may be added
10. At meal time, remove the thread from the duck, place over the sauce, and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pavo Asado (Roasted Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Brine
- Vinegar
- Bread
- Butter

## Directions

1. Place turkey in brine three days before roasting
2. On the third day, add a little vinegar
3. Three hours before the meal, soak bread in vinegar and stuff the crop well
4. Place in a hot oven
5. When it begins to brown, remove from oven, coat with butter on all sides so it comes out juicy, and return to oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pavo en Trufas (Turkey with Truffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Two pounds truffles, cleaned and drained
- One pound bacon, chopped
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Aromatic herbs
- Truffle peelings, crushed

## Directions

1. In a casserole, combine one pound chopped bacon, salt, pepper, nutmeg, aromatic herbs, and crushed truffle peelings
2. Keep over fire for half an hour
3. Insert this mixture into the well-emptied turkey through the crop and tie
4. Prepare turkey this way two days before roasting so it has time to absorb the truffle flavor
5. Place on a roasting spit for two hours until tender
6. Let it brown and serve on a platter decorated with the reserved truffle peelings
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pavo Relleno (Stuffed Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- lamb, pork or chicken meat
- vinegar
- pepper
- garlic
- cumin
- salt

## Directions

1. Debone the turkey
2. Remove the meat from the breast and legs and chop it, adding a piece of lamb, pork or chicken meat
3. Season this with desired seasonings
4. Stuff the turkey and cook in water seasoned with a little vinegar, pepper, garlic, cumin and salt
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pavo Relleno (Stuffed Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ground meat and ham
- Salt
- Pepper
- Two eggs
- Parsley
- Dried apricots or prunes
- Raisins
- Butter

## Directions

1. Make a mixture of ground meat and ham, salt, pepper, two eggs with parsley, dried apricots or prunes, and raisins
2. Fry with butter
3. Stuff the turkey and then roast
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pebre en Leche (Potato Purée in Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled potatoes
- milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook the peeled potatoes
2. Mash them with a spoon until they become like paste
3. Add milk to make it like a porridge
4. Put it in a saucepan with a piece of butter
5. Put it on the fire for a while
6. If desired, empty it onto a platter and put it in the oven to brown
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pedazos de Congrio con Finas Yerbas (Pieces of Conger Eel with Fine Herbs)
body:
## Ingredients

- congrio (conger eel)
- manteca (lard)
- cebollas (onions)
- perejil (parsley)
- setas (mushrooms)
- sal (salt)
- pimienta (pepper)
- nuez moscada (nutmeg)
- zumo de limón (lemon juice)

## Directions

1. Take a piece of conger eel from the end of the belly toward the tail.
2. Cut it into slices half a finger thick after removing the skin.
3. Lightly fry the pieces in lard with finely blanched onions, parsley, and sliced mushrooms seasoned with salt, pepper, nutmeg, and lemon juice.
4. Cook over good heat for about twenty minutes without adding water.
5. Remove the fish and let the herbs soften a bit more by adding more sauce, a little lard, and lemon juice.
6. Serve the conger eel with the remaining sauce on the side.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pejerreyes Arrollados (Rolled Silverside Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion
- Hard-boiled egg
- Bread crumbs
- Silverside fish
- Water
- Salt
- Chopped egg
- Minced onion

## Directions

1. Mince onion very finely and hard-boiled egg, add bread crumbs
2. Fry this mixture and use to fill the silverside fish, which should already be prepared, cleaned and boned
3. Arrange in a dish, add salted water and bring to boil
4. Top with chopped egg and finely minced onion
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pejerreyes Cocidos (Boiled Silverside Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- pejerreyes (silverside fish)
- water
- aceite (oil)
- ácido de limón or vinagre (lemon juice or vinegar)
- perejil (parsley)
- pimienta (pepper)
- sal (salt)

## Directions

1. Pour hot water over the pejerreyes to clean them, but do not remove the backbone.
2. Use the water that drains after washing to cook them.
3. Place the fish in a pot with this water.
4. When the fish is partially cooked, add a seasoning composed of oil, lemon juice or vinegar, parsley, pepper, and salt.
5. Continue cooking over low heat until done.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pejerreyes Cocidos (Cooked Silverside Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Silverside fish
- Hot water
- Oil
- Lemon juice or vinegar
- Parsley
- Peppercorns

## Directions

1. Pour hot water over them to remove the bones, but without removing the spine
2. The water that drains after washing is sufficient for them to cook
3. Place them thus in the saucepan
4. When somewhat cooked, pour over them a dressing composed of oil, lemon juice or vinegar, parsley and peppercorns
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pejerreyes con Salsa de Mostaza (Silverside Fish with Mustard Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Silverside fish
- Flour
- Warm brine
- Four egg yolks
- Egg whites
- Oil
- Fat

## Directions

1. Bone the silverside fish well
2. Make a batter of flour with a little warm brine and mix four egg yolks with egg whites beaten as for meringues
3. Add one and a half spoonfuls of oil and combine everything
4. Dip the fish in this sauce before putting it in the hot fat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pejerreyes en Vinagre (Silverside Fish in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- Silverside fish
- Butter
- Vinegar
- Laurel leaves
- Oil
- Garlic
- Black peppercorns

## Directions

1. Fry the silverside fish (not much) in butter
2. Place in vinegar that has been boiled beforehand with laurel leaves and is now cold
3. Pour over it half a bottle of oil boiled with ground garlic
4. Add finely ground black peppercorns over all this
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pejerreyes Fritos (Fried Silverside Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- pejerreyes (silverside fish)
- harina (flour)
- salmuera tibia (warm brine)
- four egg yolks
- egg whites
- one and a half tablespoons of aceite (oil)

## Directions

1. Clean the pejerreyes well and remove the spines.
2. Make a batter by mixing flour with a little warm brine.
3. Beat four egg yolks and whip the egg whites as for meringues.
4. Add one and a half tablespoons of oil to the mixture.
5. Combine all ingredients together.
6. Dip the pejerrey in this sauce before frying it in hot fat.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pepinos en Vinagre (Pickled Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- whole pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers and cut them in half to remove the seeds
2. Add plenty of salt and press for one hour so they release all the water
3. Fill the jars in layers with whole pepper and salt
4. Add vinegar and cover
5. The next day, uncover, refill with vinegar and tie a piece of bladder over the top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pepinos Rellenos (Stuffed Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- minced meat filling

## Directions

1. Remove the insides and peel them
2. Fill with minced meat
3. Cover so the filling does not come out
4. Cook them
5. Make a little sauce to taste
6. It takes about half an hour to cook
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pepitoria de Gallina (Chicken Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hen, cut into pieces
- Butter
- Parsley sprigs
- Small onions
- Mushrooms (setas)
- Flour, one tablespoon
- Broth
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut hen into pieces
2. Scald for three minutes in boiling water, drain
3. Place in a casserole with butter, parsley sprigs, small onions, and mushrooms
4. Brown everything together
5. Add one tablespoon of flour and moisten with broth
6. Season with salt and pepper, cook
7. Remove the hen quarters and reduce the sauce
8. Strain the sauce and thicken with egg yolks without boiling
9. Pour over the hen pieces with lemon juice
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pepitoria de Gallina (Hen Pepitoria)
body:
## Ingredients

- hen
- water
- butter
- parsley branches
- small onions
- mushrooms
- flour
- broth

## Directions

1. Cut into pieces
2. Blanch three minutes in boiling water
3. Drain and place in a pot with butter, parsley branches, small onions, and mushrooms
4. Reheat everything
5. Add one tablespoon flour and moisten with broth
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Perdices a la Española (Partridges Spanish Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- two onions
- water
- one cup oil
- half cup wine
- salt
- pepper
- cooked partridges

## Directions

1. Cut two onions into long, thin slices and soften them with boiling water
2. Squeeze them and fry them in a pan where one cup of oil should already be hot
3. When they are well fried, add half a cup of wine, salt and pepper
4. Add the cooked partridges so they heat through and serve immediately
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Perdices Borrachas (Drunken Partridges)
body:
## Ingredients

- whole onion
- bacon pieces
- two tomatoes
- two tablespoons oil
- two tablespoons vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- half glass brandy
- partridge

## Directions

1. Put plenty of whole onion, pieces of bacon, two tomatoes, two tablespoons of oil and two of vinegar, salt, pepper and half a glass of brandy in a pot
2. Add the partridge and cover well so no steam escapes
3. Do not turn it but shake it frequently
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Perdices con Crema (Partridges with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- salt
- bacon slice
- vine leaves
- butter
- water
- sour cream
- bread crumb

## Directions

1. Clean the partridges and lard them very finely
2. Add a little salt and a thin slice of bacon, wrap each one in two vine leaves, tie and put them in boiling butter with a little water so they do not roast
3. Then add sour cream (aged like butter) and a little bread crumb that has been fried a moment in butter
4. Remove the leaves and bacon and serve apart
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Perdices con Limón (Partridges with Lemon)
body:
## Ingredients

- raw partridges
- butter
- onion
- lemon juice
- pepper
- parsley
- oregano
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put the raw partridges in the pot
2. Take a little more than one tablespoon of butter, one onion, the juice of half a lemon, pepper, parsley and oregano
3. A little while before serving, beat two egg yolks
4. When they are beaten, add the juice of the other half lemon and pour this sauce over the partridges
5. Move the pot well so it does not stick and the sauce cooks
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Perdices con Repollo (Partridges with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- carrots
- onion
- celery
- parsley
- bacon piece
- sausage
- cabbage
- salt
- nutmeg
- broth

## Directions

1. Clean the partridges well
2. Put carrots, onion, celery, parsley, the partridges, a piece of bacon, a sausage and a good cabbage (which should have been soaked in boiling water beforehand, seasoned with salt and well squeezed) in a pot
3. Moisten with a little broth and add a little nutmeg
4. Boil for three hours with fire below and above if the partridges are old, and one and a half hours if they are new
5. Put the cabbage on a platter and the partridges on top with the bacon and sausage around it cut into small pieces
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Perdices con Salsa de Almendra (Partridges with Almond Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- sweet pepper
- almonds peeled
- cooked partridge
- onion heads
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Soak bread and sweet pepper and grind with peeled almonds
2. Add this sauce to the cooked partridge with onion heads
3. At the time of serving, beat in an egg yolk
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Perdices en Escabeche (Partridges in Escabeche)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- two partridges
- oil
- vinegar
- bay leaves
- garlic
- allspice
- black pepper
- parsley
- celery
- oregano
- salt
- orange leaves

## Directions

1. Cut onion into large pieces
2. Place a layer of onion in an earthenware pot, place two raw partridges, another layer of onion
3. Add one cup of oil and half cup of vinegar, four ground bay leaves, one head of garlic, whole allspice and black pepper, parsley, celery, oregano and salt
4. Cover with orange leaves
5. Well covered, cook over low heat, moving them often so they do not stick
6. Before serving let them cool for four hours so the broth incorporates
7. For two partridges use four onions
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Perdices en Salsa (Partridges in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- butter
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- raw oil
- bay leaves
- lemon or orange slices
- broth
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Well cleaned and buttered, broil them on the grill
2. Then put them in a pot with parsley, pepper, salt, raw oil, bay leaves, some slices of lemon or orange and broth that covers them
3. When they are cooked, set them aside, and for each bird beat an egg yolk and add to the sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Perdices en Salsa (Partridges in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridges
- Butter
- Parsley
- Pepper
- Salt
- Raw oil
- Bay leaves
- Lemon or orange slices
- Broth
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clean the partridges well and coat them with butter
2. Sear them on a grill
3. Place them in a pot with parsley, pepper, salt, raw oil, bay leaves, lemon or orange slices, and broth to cover
4. When cooked, remove them
5. For each bird, add one beaten egg yolk
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Perdices Rellados con Sardinas (Partridges Stuffed with Sardines)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- sardines
- lard or bacon
- tomato
- lemon juice
- pepper
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. After cleaning, place two clean sardines without heads inside the body of each partridge
2. Roast them with lard or bacon, and if there is tomato, add it on top, or lemon juice, a pinch of pepper, salt and parsley
3. Roast them between two fires and when serving, remove the sardines
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pernil Asado (Roasted Leg of Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- desalted pork leg
- white wine
- bread crumbs
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take a desalted pork leg and soak in white wine for ten or twelve hours.
2. Place on the spit and roast for six hours over moderate heat, continuously basting it with its own drippings.
3. When done, remove the skin and dust with bread crumbs.
4. When serving, add lemon juice.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pescado Asado (Roasted Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- pescado (fish)
- tres huevos duros (three hard-boiled eggs)
- perejil (parsley)
- orégano (oregano)
- pimienta (pepper)
- mantequilla (butter)
- miga de pan (bread crumbs)

## Directions

1. Fill the fish with finely chopped hard-boiled eggs, parsley, oregano, and pepper.
2. Place it in a buttered pan lined with bread crumbs.
3. Coat the fish with butter on all sides.
4. Roll it in bread crumbs.
5. Put it in the oven.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pescado Asado (Roasted Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- pescado (fish)
- pan rayado (grated bread)
- aceite (oil)
- agrio de limón (lemon juice)
- pimienta molida (ground pepper)
- hígado del pescado (fish liver)

## Directions

1. Place the whole fish in a pan.
2. Sprinkle grated bread, oil, lemon juice, and ground pepper on top.
3. Put it in the oven.
4. The ground fish liver can also be added if desired.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pescado Borracho (Drunken Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- pescado (fish)
- pimienta (pepper)
- perejil (parsley)
- vino (wine)
- salsa de callampas (mushroom sauce)

## Directions

1. Place the fish in the pot in which it will be cooked.
2. Add pepper and parsley.
3. Add a layer of wine and another layer of mushroom sauce.
4. Cook over low heat.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pescado con Aceite i Vinagre (Fish with Oil and Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- pescado (fish)
- cebollas (onions)
- tomate (tomato)
- apio (celery)
- ajíes dulces (sweet peppers)
- escabeches (pickled vegetables)
- aceite (oil)
- vinagre (vinegar)

## Directions

1. Cut large slices of onions, tomatoes (fresh or dried if fresh is not available), and celery branches.
2. Place these in a dish.
3. Put the whole fish or pieces of fish on top.
4. Cover with another lid of equal size.
5. Add sweet peppers (fresh halved or soaked if dried) and pieces of pickled vegetables.
6. When it comes to a boil, add oil and vinegar.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pescado con Salsa Amarilla (Fish with Yellow Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- harina (flour)
- agua (water)
- agrio de limón (lemon juice)
- dos yemas de huevo (two egg yolks)
- mantequilla (butter)
- pescado cocido (cooked fish)

## Directions

1. Dissolve a little flour in water.
2. Add lemon juice and two egg yolks.
3. Mix well.
4. Add a piece of butter and a little water.
5. Serve with cooked fish.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pescado con Salsa de Pan (Fish with Bread Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pescado (fish)
- pan (bread)
- papas (potatoes)
- harina (flour)
- caldo or agua caliente (broth or hot water)
- cebollas (onions)
- pimienta (pepper)
- vinagre (vinegar)

## Directions

1. Fry the fish.
2. Break bread into pieces and cut potatoes into thin slices; coat the potatoes in flour.
3. Place the potatoes in a pot and put the fish on top.
4. Grind the bread and add a little broth or hot water, minced onions, pepper, and vinegar.
5. Put the fish on top and keep it on the fire until serving time.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pescado con Salsa de Pan (Fish with Bread Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pescado (fish)
- cebollas (onions)
- sal (salt)
- perejil (parsley)
- mantequilla (butter)
- miga de pan (bread crumbs)

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well and cook it with onions and salt.
2. Add parsley and butter.
3. Thicken with bread crumbs.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pescado en Vino (Fish in Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- pescado (fish)
- pimienta (pepper)
- mantequilla (butter)
- cebollas (onions)
- two tablespoons of color (coloring agent)
- half a cup of vino (wine)
- harina (flour)

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well and wash it.
2. Season inside and outside with pepper and butter.
3. Place sliced onions in a dish with two tablespoons of coloring agent.
4. Put the fish on top of the onions.
5. Add another layer of onions above the fish and half a cup of wine.
6. Cover the dish and bring to a boil.
7. If preserved, add half a tablespoon of flour when it boils.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pescado Frito con Salsa de Mostaza (Fried Fish with Mustard Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pescado (fish)
- rama de apio (celery branch)
- perejil (parsley)
- pimienta (pepper)
- sal (salt)
- papas (potatoes)
- mantequilla (butter)
- mostaza francesa (French mustard)
- harina (flour)

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well and cook it in a fish poacher with a celery branch, parsley, pepper, and salt.
2. Serve at the table with large boiled and peeled potatoes.
3. For the sauce: melt a tablespoon of butter and add a teaspoon of French mustard and finely chopped parsley.
4. Add a little flour and dilute with the water in which the fish was cooked to the desired thickness.
5. Serve the cooked fish with this sauce.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pescado Frito Seco en Fuente (Dry Fried Fish in a Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- pescado (fish)
- pimienta (pepper)
- cebollas (onions)
- ajo (garlic)
- harina (flour)
- huevo (egg)
- papas (potatoes)

## Directions

1. Soak the fish overnight.
2. Cut it into pieces.
3. Add pepper, sliced onions, and a little garlic.
4. Bring to a boil.
5. Make a flour sauce with the water in a silver or ceramic dish.
6. Layer the fish, onions, hard-boiled eggs sliced, and fried potatoes in slices.
7. Cover everything with the flour sauce.
8. Put it in the oven or over fire.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pescado Seco a la Vizcaína (Dried Fish Biscay Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pescado seco (dried fish)
- aceite (oil)
- miga de pan (bread crumbs)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- cebollas (onions)
- ajo (garlic)
- tomate (tomato)

## Directions

1. Soak the dried fish cut into pieces from the day before.
2. The next day, cook it.
3. Heat four tablespoons of oil in a pot with bread crumbs soaked in vinegar to remove the oil's smell.
4. Fry a plate of onions cut into large pieces and one garlic clove until they begin to brown.
5. Add about a plate of fresh chopped tomato (or resoaked dried tomato if fresh is not available, or preserved if desired).
6. When the tomato begins to wrinkle and has released all its juices, add the well-shredded fish and let it boil briefly.
7. It should remain quite dry.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pfefferkuchen de Nuremberg (Nuremberg Gingerbread)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight egg yolks
- one pound powdered sugar
- eight egg whites
- one pound four ounces peeled almonds, sliced very thin
- twelve ounces flour
- four ounces candied lemon, chopped
- cinnamon
- clove
- cilantro

## Directions

1. Beat eight egg yolks with one pound powdered sugar for half an hour
2. Add eight egg whites without beating
3. Add one pound four ounces peeled almonds sliced very thin, twelve ounces flour, four ounces candied lemon chopped, cinnamon, clove, and cilantro
4. Mix all these ingredients well
5. Spread the mixture on the dough, extending it to the thickness of half a finger
6. Cut into long pieces and place on buttered baking pans in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pfefferkuchen de Nuremberg (Nuremberg Gingerbread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 8 egg whites
- 1 pound 4 ounces peeled almonds cut into very fine slices
- 12 ounces flour
- 4 ounces candied lemon, chopped
- cinnamon
- clove
- cilantro

## Directions

1. Beat 8 egg yolks with 1 pound powdered sugar for half an hour
2. Then add 8 unbeaten egg whites, 1 pound 4 ounces of peeled almonds cut into very fine slices, 12 ounces of flour, 4 ounces of candied lemon and chopped, cinnamon, clove, and cilantro
3. Mix all these ingredients well, place on the dough
4. Extend the dough until 1/2 finger thick
5. Cut into long pieces and place in the oven on buttered baking tins
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Picadillo de Conejo (Rabbit Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- rabbit bones
- veal scraps
- lean bacon
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- milk

## Directions

1. Remove bones and membranes from the rabbit and mince very finely what remains.
2. Sauté in lard the crushed bones with veal scraps, lean bacon, salt and pepper, dusting everything with a little flour.
3. Mix well and add milk.
4. After it has boiled for one hour, remove and pass through a sieve, reducing to the consistency of a stew, mixing with the hash which should cook alone.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pichones (Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- lard
- ham pieces
- chopped onion
- squabs
- egg yolk
- lemon acid

## Directions

1. Fry ham pieces and chopped onion in lard
2. Once everything is fried, remove it and brown the squabs in the same lard
3. When they are tender, mix them with water and the fried ingredients
4. Add an egg yolk and lemon acid
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pichones (Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork lard
- Ham pieces
- Onion, chopped
- Squabs
- Water
- Egg yolk
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Fry pieces of ham and chopped onion in pork lard
2. Once everything is fried, remove and brown the squabs in the same lard
3. When tender, mix with water and the previously fried ingredients
4. Add an egg yolk and lemon juice
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pichones a la Casera (Squabs Home Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- bacon strips
- meat broth
- parsley bundle
- fine herbs

## Directions

1. Empty and scald them, fold their legs inside, then leave them in cold water
2. Put them in a pot between two strips of bacon with meat broth and a bundle of parsley and fine herbs
3. When they are cooked, skim the broth and put it in a sauce that is clarified and thickened
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pichones con Masa por Encima (Squabs with Topping)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- four ounces butter
- seven to eight egg yolks
- four ounces almonds
- one glass milk
- sugar
- nutmeg
- salt
- ten ounces bread crumb
- egg whites

## Directions

1. After cleaning the squabs, roast them in the oven, dry them and arrange them in a dish, whole or split in half
2. Beat four ounces of butter until it looks like foam
3. Add seven to eight egg yolks, four ounces of almonds, one glass of milk, a little sugar, nutmeg, salt and about ten ounces of bread crumb
4. At the end, fold in the beaten egg whites until it becomes soft
5. Pour it over the squabs and put them in the oven
6. The squabs should be so well covered that it looks like a pastry
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pichones Embasados (Squabs in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- giblets
- tomato
- water
- broth
- spices
- soaked bread crumb

## Directions

1. Clean and remove the giblets
2. Fry a little tomato with the whole squabs until they are well browned
3. Add the corresponding water and two cups of broth
4. Crush the giblets with some spices and soaked bread crumb, dilute everything and add it to the squabs
5. Let it boil once
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pichones en Asador (Squabs on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- vine leaf
- bacon strip

## Directions

1. After plucking, emptying and cleaning well the feather shafts, tie them, being careful to leave their legs lengthwise
2. Place a vine leaf on the breast, if it is in season, and over it a strip of bacon
3. Put them on the spit: three quarters of an hour are sufficient for them to be roasted
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pichones Otro (Squabs Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- lard
- squabs
- tomato pieces
- water
- saffron
- pepper
- bread crumb

## Directions

1. Fry the squabs with tomato pieces in lard until they are tender
2. Add water and when they are soft, add saffron, pepper and bread crumb
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pichones Rellenos (Stuffed Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- veal
- poultry breast
- bacon
- onion fried
- eggs
- parsley
- pepper
- cumin

## Directions

1. Remove the wings and necks, trim the tail, and make a fine hash of veal, poultry breast, bacon and fried onion, eggs and parsley, ground pepper and cumin
2. Stuff two squabs with this mixture and cross their legs over the opening to prevent the filling from coming out
3. Then wrap them in brown paper and roast them in a pot between two slow fires
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Picos Fritos (Fried Clams)
body:
## Ingredients

- Clams
- Egg batter
- Bread crumbs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Roast the clams and remove from the shell
2. Clean them well and pass through beaten egg
3. Roll in bread crumbs and fry in butter
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pimientos i Tomates (Peppers and Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- finely chopped onion
- oil
- tomato (peeled)
- fine peppers cut into small pieces

## Directions

1. Chop the onion finely and put it to simmer in oil
2. Add peeled tomato
3. Add fine peppers cut into small pieces
4. Let it simmer a lot until reduced
5. Serve with fried eggs or with meat, as an appetizing side dish
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Piñonates (Pine Nut Candy)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds syrup
- one pound paste, not too finely ground

## Directions

1. Boil two pounds of syrup with one pound of paste not too finely ground
2. When it reaches the thread stage, remove the pan from heat and beat until it becomes white
3. Boil again once more
4. Pour quickly onto a damp table and spread to desired thickness
5. Before it cools, cut into squares or biscuits
6. Separate with a knife and place in a basket
7. This can also be made with Panama coconut or local coconut, grating the coconut instead of grinding it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Plumpudding (Plum Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a bottle milk
- eight eggs
- one pound finely chopped beef suet, made into crumbs
- one pound flour
- one pound Corinth raisins
- twelve ounces large raisins
- one glass rum
- two tablespoons sugar
- half a teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Place half a bottle of milk with eight eggs in a pot and beat for a while
2. Add one pound finely chopped beef suet made into crumbs and one pound flour
3. Mix well and add one pound Corinth raisins and twelve ounces large raisins
4. Add one glass of rum
5. Mix everything with two tablespoons sugar and half a teaspoon salt
6. The next day, five hours before serving, boil water in a large pot with a stone in the middle
7. Pour all into a buttered napkin, tie well leaving space for it to rise
8. Boil, cover, and keep it boiling on the stone
9. When removing, place on a serving dish and in the hole left by the stone, pour rum with sugar and set it aflame
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos a lo Campesino (Chickens Country Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- piece of butter
- two tablespoons oil
- carrot
- onions
- parsley branches
- broth

## Directions

1. Disjoint the chicken
2. In a pot place a piece of butter and two tablespoons oil
3. Place over a very hot fire and add the chicken pieces to take color
4. When half cooked, add carrot cut in rounds and two onions in slices with parsley branches
5. Add broth and let cook slowly for a quarter hour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos Asados (Roasted Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- raw chicken
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- breadcrumb

## Directions

1. After cleaning the raw chicken well, season with salt and pepper
2. Melt a piece of butter and coat all sides
3. Add considerable breadcrumb
4. Three quarters of an hour before eating, place on the grill to roast
5. Serve with some vegetable stew
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos con Arvejas (Chickens with Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- peas
- onion
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cook the chickens and in the same broth cook the peas
2. Add a little onion fried until colored
3. At the time of serving, beat an egg yolk and add a little color
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos con Salsa (Chickens with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- two tablespoons butter
- carrot
- chopped onion
- parsley
- vinegar
- flour if needed

## Directions

1. Place chickens in a pot with two tablespoons butter, carrot, chopped onion, and parsley
2. Bring everything to boil with a little vinegar
3. Strain the sauce afterward
4. If the sauce is clear, add a little flour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos con Salsa Amarilla (Chicken with Yellow Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Three egg yolks
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Fry a little flour in butter and make a regular sauce
2. At meal time, add three beaten egg yolks and lemon juice
3. Pour over the chickens
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos con Salsa Amarilla (Chickens with Yellow Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- three egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Fry a little flour in butter and make a regular sauce
2. At the time of eating, add three beaten egg yolks and lemon juice
3. Pour over the chickens
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos con Tomate (Chickens with Tomato)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- tomato
- corn
- bay leaves
- oregano
- chicken

## Directions

1. Chop a little onion and fry until colored
2. When it begins to brown, add about one plate of chopped tomato and a little corn cut up, bay leaves, and oregano
3. When everything is fried, add the chicken, either raw or roasted and disjointed
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos en Cochifrito (Chickens in Cochifrito)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- butter
- flour
- water
- salt
- ground pepper
- nutmeg
- parsley branch
- onion
- mushrooms
- asparagus
- three egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. After washing the chickens well, disjoint them and soak for one or two hours in cold water to whiten the meat
2. Drain them
3. In a pot place a piece of butter and one tablespoon flour, stir until the butter melts
4. Add one glass of water, salt, ground pepper, nutmeg, a parsley branch, and onion
5. Place the chickens there and let cook three quarters of an hour
6. Separately make the sauce with three egg yolks and lemon juice
7. When the chickens have cooked a little, add chopped mushrooms and small pieces of asparagus
8. Unite the sauce with the chickens
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos Encebolladis (Chicken with Onion and Corn)
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion
- Corn (choclo), cut
- Chicken, disjointed, raw or roasted

## Directions

1. Fry onion until colored with cut corn
2. When the onion is fried, add the disjointed chicken, either raw or roasted
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos Encebollados (Chickens with Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- corn
- chicken

## Directions

1. Fry onion in color with cut corn
2. When the onion is fried, add the disjointed chicken, raw or roasted
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos Fritos (Fried Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- egg
- bread crumb
- lard

## Directions

1. Disjoint the raw chicken
2. Beat an egg with breadcrumb
3. Fry in very hot lard
4. Serve with pebre or peas, mushrooms, or chard, in short with any vegetable stew desired
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos Guisados a la Peruana (Peruvian-Style Stewed Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- pork lard
- ham slices
- green peppers
- parsley
- tomatoes
- onions
- cinnamon
- pepper
- clove
- vinegar
- raisins
- toasted and crushed almonds
- capers
- fried plantain slices
- two small cups chicken broth

## Directions

1. Pluck well and pass a flaming paper over it to burn any remaining down
2. Open and remove the entrails and clean well, then cut into quarters
3. Fry in lard with ham slices
4. When half browned, add green peppers in pieces, parsley, tomatoes, and onions, all chopped
5. Fry all this with the chickens, adding a little cinnamon, pepper, and ground clove with some vinegar
6. When the chickens are half cooked, add raisins, toasted and crushed almonds, whole capers, fried plantain slices, and two small cups of chicken broth
7. Let simmer, ensuring the sauce becomes half thick
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos o Perdices al Horno (Chickens or Partridges Roasted)
body:
## Ingredients

- bird
- one tablespoon flour
- butter
- broth
- cheese
- one glass cognac or sherry

## Directions

1. Cook the bird
2. In the same broth make the sauce by dissolving one tablespoon flour in a little butter
3. Add the necessary broth so it reaches a regular thickness
4. Arrange the bird in pieces on a platter
5. Pour the sauce over it and add one glass of cognac or sherry
6. Grate cheese so the pieces are well covered
7. Place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos o Plebones con Salsa Prieta (Chickens with Dark Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- bird
- onion
- celery stalks
- salt
- bird blood
- vinegar
- flour
- butter
- bird broth

## Directions

1. Cook the bird with onion, celery stalks, and salt
2. Receive the bird blood in a cup with vinegar so it doesn't clump
3. Make a sauce of flour with butter and bird broth
4. When cooked, add the blood, taking care it doesn't clump and remains smooth
5. Place the bird on a platter with the onion and celery
6. Pour the sauce over it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos Rellenos (Stuffed Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- Gizzard, chopped
- Liver
- A little meat
- A little chopped onion
- Bread crumbs
- Parsley
- Eggs (the egg and meat cooked)
- One tablespoon of vinegar
- Two tablespoons of broth
- One small tablespoon of flour
- Butter

## Directions

1. Chop the gizzard, liver and a little meat
2. Add a little chopped onion, bread crumbs, parsley and eggs (the egg and meat cooked) and with this stuff the chickens
3. Put in a saucepan one tablespoon of vinegar, two of broth, one small tablespoon of flour and boil with butter
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos Rellenos (Stuffed Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken giblets
- chicken liver
- some chicken meat
- small amount of chopped onion
- breadcrumbs
- parsley
- eggs
- one tablespoon vinegar
- two tablespoons broth
- small tablespoon flour
- sufficient butter

## Directions

1. Chop the giblets, liver, and some meat
2. Mix with chopped onion, breadcrumbs, parsley, and cooked eggs and meat
3. Stuff the chickens with this mixture
4. In a pot combine one tablespoon vinegar, two tablespoons broth, and a small amount of flour
5. Bring to boil with sufficient butter
6. Place the raw chickens in the pot and cook
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos Revueltos (Wrapped Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- puff pastry

## Directions

1. Cook the chickens whole
2. Let them cool
3. Wrap them in puff pastry
4. Place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Pollos Saltados (Sautéed Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- peeled potatoes
- good piece of butter
- potatoes
- onion
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the chicken with peeled potatoes
2. When well cooked, place a good piece of butter in a pot and heat
3. Fry the disjointed chicken
4. When browned, add potatoes cut in slices, finely chopped onion and parsley
5. Stir until the potatoes are browned
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ponderaciones (Fried Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- four heaping tablespoons flour
- a little melted butter
- a little salt
- powdered sugar to taste
- a little milk

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs a little
2. Add four heaping tablespoons flour, a little melted butter, a little salt, and powdered sugar to taste
3. Add a little milk to form a pancake batter
4. Dip the ponderaciones mold into the batter, ensuring it enters only halfway into its height
5. Submerge in very hot lard
6. When the ponderación is golden, place on a serving dish and immediately sprinkle with sugar mixed with a little cinnamon
7. Note: the first two or three ponderaciones may not separate from the mold, but continuing the operation they will come out easily
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Porotitos Verdes a la Paisana (Green Beans Country-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- chopped parsley
- small green beans
- lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Melt butter in a saucepan with chopped parsley
2. Add the small green beans, which should be cooked beforehand
3. Stir and reduce to a sauce
4. When serving, dress them with lemon juice or vinegar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Porotitos Verdes en Salsa de Limeyos (Green Beans in Egg-Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- chopped parsley
- small green beans
- egg yolks
- lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Melt butter in a saucepan with chopped parsley
2. Add the small green beans
3. Stir and reduce the sauce
4. Before serving, add a beaten mixture of egg yolks diluted with lemon juice or a little vinegar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Porotos Verdes Picados (Chopped Green Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- water
- salt
- onion
- abundant fat
- meat (to roast in advance)

## Directions

1. Break the beans into small pieces
2. Cook them in salted water
3. Once cooked, discard the water
4. Make a sofrito of onion and abundant fat
5. Put the beans in a dry platter and break them up with a spoon until somewhat ground
6. Pour everything into the onion sofrito
7. Leave on low heat
8. Just before serving, add the meat that was roasted in advance
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Potaje de Coles (Cabbage Pottage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Salt
- Oil or butter
- Pepper
- Nutmeg powder
- Milk

## Directions

1. Wash cabbage well and cook with salt.
2. Fry and drain the cabbage, cutting it into thick pieces.
3. Brown with oil or butter, salt, pepper and nutmeg powder.
4. Add a little milk and cook over low heat until the sauce reduces and the cabbage is well bound.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Potaje de Garbanzos (Chickpea Pottage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two onions, cooked
- Oil
- Beaten eggs or bread soaked and dissolved in water
- A little clove and pepper

## Directions

1. Cook two onions well.
2. Stew with oil.
3. Add beaten eggs or bread soaked and dissolved in water.
4. Add a little clove and pepper.
5. Serve.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Potaje de Lentejas (Lentil Pottage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lentils
- Salt
- Oil or butter
- Chopped parsley
- Fried crusts or bread, optional

## Directions

1. Cook lentils with salt.
2. Place in a pot with oil or butter and chopped parsley.
3. Brown slightly.
4. Add water.
5. Serve with or without fried bread crusts.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Potaje de Verdura (Vegetable Pottage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two ounces of rice
- Two ounces of peeled chickpeas
- Chard
- Carrots, cooked and finely chopped
- One spoonful of coloring
- A little stock
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cook two ounces of rice in water.
2. Cook two ounces of peeled chickpeas and cooked chard and carrots.
3. Finely chop the chard and carrots.
4. Mix everything together in a dish with one spoonful of coloring, a little stock, salt and pepper.
5. Place over low heat to keep warm.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Puchero a la Mejicana (Mexican-Style Puchero)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of veal
- 2 pounds of beef
- bacon
- cold water
- lettuce
- 2 or 3 turnips
- carrots
- chickpeas
- turkey scraps
- half chicken
- lamb bones
- parsley
- 3 cloves
- 1 roasted onion

## Directions

1. Take a pot containing about 2 jars of cold water
2. Add 2 pounds of veal, 2 of beef and half a pound of bacon, put on low heat
3. Add 2 jars of water when the first portion has lost its coldness
4. Increase the heat to produce foam
5. Remove this as it rises until no more appears
6. After boiling about 8 hours over regular heat, when it has been cooking 4 hours, add half a cabbage, 1 lettuce, 2 or 3 turnips, equal quantity of carrots, a handful of soaked chickpeas, turkey scraps, half a chicken and some roasted lamb bones, a little parsley, 3 cloves and 1 roasted onion
7. Add hot water if needed, continuing until 8 hours total
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Puchero for the Sick
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken or mutton
- chickpeas
- ham
- broth

## Directions

1. It is made as a rule with chicken or mutton, adding a little bit so it is substantial, some chickpeas or ham
2. The broth should be strained before serving
3. If greater substance is desired, pound the chicken in a mortar with bread crumb and thin it in broth
4. Strain it and once seasoned, put on heat but without boiling
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Puré de Fréjoles, Lentejas, Arvejas, Habas, etc. (Puree of Beans, Lentils, Peas, Fava Beans, etc.)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh legumes of choice
- Stock
- Small slices of carrot and onion
- A little butter

## Directions

1. Cook any of these fresh legumes in stock, adding small slices of carrot and onion with a little butter.
2. When cooked, crush in a mortar and pass the juice through a sieve.
3. Add more stock if it is too thick.
4. Pour this juice over a few small pieces of fried bread and when well arranged, serve.
5. To preserve the green color of the legume, the juice of crushed spinach may be added.
6. On fasting days, use fish stock and oil instead of butter.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Puré de Garbanzos (Chickpea Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of chickpeas
- Stock from meat
- One onion
- Bacon
- Carrot
- A small bundle of herbs

## Directions

1. Soak chickpeas the day before with a little salt.
2. Cook with meat stock, adding one onion, bacon, carrot and a small bundle of herbs.
3. Cook gently and slowly.
4. Finish as instructed for lentil puree.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Puré de Lentejas (Lentil Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of lentils
- Stock from puchero
- A little bacon
- Two carrots, thinned
- Two onions, thinned and browned in butter
- Two cloves
- Fried bread crusts

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of lentils in stock from puchero with a little bacon, two thinned carrots, two thinned onions browned in butter, and two cloves.
2. When everything is cooked, pass through a sieve.
3. Thin the puree with stock.
4. Cook in a pot to skim it.
5. Serve over fried bread crusts.
6. For fasting days, this puree can be made by substituting oil for bacon.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Purée of Chickpeas
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of chickpeas
- broth from meat
- 1 onion
- bacon
- carrot
- a bundle of herbs

## Directions

1. Soak 2 pounds of chickpeas the day before with a little salt
2. Cook them with meat broth, adding 1 onion, bacon, carrot and a bundle of herbs
3. Ensure the cooking is done slowly
4. When cooked, remove the onion, carrot and bacon
5. Pass everything through a sieve, pressing well
6. Remove the fat and season
7. Heat and serve the purée
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Purée of Green Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of green beans
- water
- salt
- sugar
- broth
- fried bread pieces

## Directions

1. Cook 2 pounds of green beans in boiling water with salt over strong heat so they do not lose their color
2. After cooking, remove the beans and place in cold water, passing them through a sieve
3. Place in a clean pot with a little sugar
4. Add enough broth so it is neither too thin nor too thick
5. Serve very hot with some pieces of fried bread
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Purée of Lentils
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of lentils
- broth from puchero
- a little bacon
- 2 carrots
- 2 onions
- butter
- 2 cloves

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pound of lentils in puchero broth with a little bacon, 2 carrots, 2 onions thinned and sautéed in butter, and 2 cloves
2. When everything is cooked, pass through a sieve
3. Thin the purée with broth
4. Cook in a pot to skim it
5. Serve over fried bread crusts
6. This purée can be made for fast days by substituting oil for the bacon fat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Purée of Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds of white potatoes
- water
- salt
- butter
- poultry broth
- 4 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook 4 pounds of white potatoes, peeled and cut into quarters, in water and salt
2. Before they are completely cooked, drain well all the water
3. In the same covered pot, place under the heat so they finish cooking and become floury
4. Stir with a spoon, adding a little butter
5. Pass through a sieve
6. Mix with poultry broth and pass through a fine sieve
7. At the time of serving, add 4 beaten egg yolks
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ragout de Capón con Ostras (Capon Ragout with Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- Capon
- Butter
- Onion
- Bay leaves
- Pepper
- Broth
- Mushrooms
- Lemon peel
- Oysters
- Flour
- Liver
- Breadcrumbs
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place butter, onion, bay leaves, whole pepper, and a little broth in a pot
2. Cook the capon in this
3. Make a sauce from broth, ground mushrooms, lemon peel, and oysters
4. Cook briefly and thicken with flour fried in butter
5. If desired, stuff the capon before cooking with its liver, breadcrumbs, butter, some oysters, and pepper
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ragout de Pollos o Pichones (Ragout of Chickens or Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken or squab
- Balls made for German vol-au-vent
- Caper sauce
- Puff pastry or other delicate dough

## Directions

1. Cook the bird and cut it into pieces
2. Place in a serving dish with balls made for German vol-au-vent
3. Add a small caper sauce
4. Cover with puff pastry or other delicate dough
5. Place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ragout en Fuente (Ragout in a Serving Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridges or chickens
- Butter
- Lemon peel
- Broth
- Wine
- Breadcrumbs
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut up partridges or chickens into pieces
2. Place in a serving dish with butter, lemon peel, broth, and a little wine and breadcrumbs
3. Let simmer
4. When cooked, add lemon juice and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Repollo Frito (Fried Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salt
- onions
- seasonings
- beaten egg
- grated cheese (optional)

## Directions

1. Chop the cabbage finely and cook it with salt
2. Remove and squeeze it well
3. Make a sofrito of onions and seasonings
4. Add the cabbage to this
5. Fry it well for an hour
6. Add beaten egg and grated cheese if desired
7. Serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Riñones con Salsa (Kidneys with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- kidneys
- minced onion
- butter
- flour
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the kidneys into slices with a little minced onion
2. Put a spoonful of butter in a saucepan, two of flour, two of vinegar and heat everything over low heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Riñón de Vaca a la Casera (Beef Kidney Home Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef kidney
- lard
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion
- vinegar
- broth

## Directions

1. Cut a kidney into thin strips
2. Put on heat with lard, salt, pepper, parsley and onion
3. After cooking, add a little vinegar and some strained broth, or in its place, pot broth
4. Do not cook further so it does not become tough
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Roasted Lamb Kidneys (Riñones de Carnero Asados)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb kidneys
- Lard
- Butter
- Fine herbs
- Anchovy butter
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and split the kidneys without separating them completely
2. Place them on a small skewer to keep them open
3. Put a little lard at the bottom of a casserole with the kidneys inside
4. Place on a strong fire
5. When nearly done, place inside a small meatball of butter dissolved with fine herbs or anchovy butter and a little lemon juice
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Rolled Sirloin (Arrollado de Lomo)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sirloin steak
- Breadcrumbs
- Nutmeg
- Bacon
- Flour

## Directions

1. Cut long thin pieces of sirloin, beat them well
2. On one side place breadcrumbs and nutmeg
3. Cut bacon pieces of the same size
4. Roll and tie with string
5. Cook slowly in a casserole and baste with the juice they release
6. Make a sauce by adding a spoonful of flour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ropa Vieja (Shredded Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of roasted loin from the day before
- onion slices
- plenty of tomato
- slices of fried potatoes
- fried bread toasts
- fat
- celery
- parsley
- whole pepper
- two spoonfuls of color

## Directions

1. Cut slices of a roasted loin from the day before
2. Put in a dish slices of onions, plenty of tomato, slices of fried potatoes, and fried bread toasts in fat, celery, parsley, whole pepper, two spoonfuls of color
3. Cover well and let it boil
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Roscas (Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- sixteen eggs with six and a half egg whites
- half pound sugar
- one large tablespoon lard
- sufficient flour

## Directions

1. Beat sixteen eggs with six and a half egg whites well
2. Add one large tablespoon lard
3. Add sufficient flour to make a regular dough
4. Knead, form rings, and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Rosquetes (Small Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 4 ounces hot fat
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat 12 eggs with 1/2 pound sugar
2. When well beaten, add necessary flour to make a dough
3. Add 4 ounces hot fat
4. Work the dough, form rings, and coat them
5. Pass through boiling water and remove as they rise
6. Coat again by placing fire underneath and with great quickness place in the oven
7. They are also very good if lard is used instead of fat
8. Cut with a knife on the sides when placing them in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Rosquitas (Thin Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 6 whole eggs
- 1 teaspoon yeast
- cinnamon
- flour
- more than 1 ounce butter
- burned fat

## Directions

1. Add 6 whole eggs to 1 pound powdered sugar and beat a little
2. Add 1 teaspoon yeast, plenty of cinnamon, and sufficient flour to make a dough, with more than 1 ounce butter and burned fat
3. Form thin ring shapes
4. Place in boiling water until they rise to the surface of the water
5. Remove and place on baking sheets
6. Bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Rosquitas de Agua (Water Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound powdered sugar
- six whole eggs
- one small teaspoon baking powder
- abundant cinnamon
- sufficient flour
- more than one ounce butter and rendered fat

## Directions

1. Add six whole eggs to one pound powdered sugar and beat a little
2. Add one small teaspoon baking powder and abundant cinnamon
3. Add sufficient flour to make the dough with more than one ounce butter and rendered fat
4. Form thin little rings
5. Place in boiling water until they rise above the water
6. Then place on baking pans and in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Rosquitas de Yemas (Egg Yolk Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten well-beaten egg yolks
- six ounces sugar
- four ounces butter
- one glass sherry or white wine
- a little milk
- cinnamon
- grated lemon
- sufficient flour for a soft dough

## Directions

1. To ten well-beaten egg yolks add six ounces sugar
2. Add four ounces butter, one glass sherry or white wine, a little milk, cinnamon, and grated lemon
3. Add sufficient flour for a soft dough
4. Knead everything a little and form the rings
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Rosquitas de Yemas (Egg Yolk Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 6 ounces sugar
- 4 ounces butter
- 1 glass sherry or white wine
- a little milk
- cinnamon
- grated lemon
- sufficient flour

## Directions

1. Beat 10 egg yolks well
2. Add 6 ounces sugar, 4 ounces butter, 1 glass sherry or white wine, a little milk, cinnamon, and grated lemon
3. Add sufficient flour for a soft dough
4. Knead everything a little
5. Form rings
6. Bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salmón a la Española (Salmon Spanish Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- leeks
- pepper
- fish broth
- parsley
- onion

## Directions

1. Cook the salmon with leeks and pepper in a light white fish broth
2. Serve with the same leeks, or with a mixture of chopped parsley and onion, dusted with pepper
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salmón a la Genovesa (Salmon Genoese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon wheel or steak
- red wine
- broth
- equal quantity of wine and broth
- parsley, chopped
- spices
- salt
- nutmeg powder
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Cook a salmon wheel in equal quantities of red wine and broth
2. Add chopped parsley, spices, salt and nutmeg powder
3. When cooked, remove the salmon
4. To the liquid remaining in the pan, add a piece of butter mixed with flour
5. Reduce the sauce after passing it through a sieve
6. When it reaches the right consistency, add the salmon
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salmón en Parrilla (Grilled Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon slices
- oil
- salt
- onion pieces
- parsley
- white sauce with capers or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Place raw salmon slices in a marinade of oil to which salt, pieces of onion and parsley have been added
2. After one hour, remove and place on a grill, moistening it with the said marinade
3. Serve with a white sauce of capers or a tomato sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salmón Guisado (Stewed Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh salmon
- oil
- salt
- lemon juice
- pepper
- or salted salmon soaked twenty-four hours in water
- or salmon on a spit with salt, oil and pepper, served with a sauce of beef butter and capers

## Directions

1. If the salmon is fresh, cook it and drizzle raw oil over it, seasoning with salt, lemon juice and a little pepper
2. If it is salted, soak it twenty-four hours in water, preferably changing the water; then remove it and scrub it well to prepare it as described or with any other dish
3. Alternatively, place the salmon on a spit with salt, oil and pepper, and when it is ready, serve it with a sauce of beef butter and capers
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salpicón de Ave (Poultry Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked poultry
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Lettuce
- Pickles
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Anchovies

## Directions

1. Cook a bird and remove the meat after cooling
2. Chop the meat coarsely
3. Place in a marinade with salt, pepper, and vinegar
4. After a few moments, dress with chopped lettuce, pickles, hard-boiled eggs, and strips of anchovies
5. Pour the substance over the top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa (Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- thyme
- bay leaf
- parsley
- onion
- pepper, powdered
- bread
- broth
- salt

## Directions

1. In a saucepan put vinegar, thyme, bay leaf, parsley, onion and powdered pepper
2. Fry a little bread that is dissolved in broth
3. Add salt and let it boil for a quarter hour
4. Pass it through a sieve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa a la Kari (Curry Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- A good piece of butter
- Pieces of chopped pepper
- Flour
- A little saffron (optional)
- Broth
- Nutmeg zest
- Hot pepper powder (optional)

## Directions

1. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan with pieces of chopped pepper, flour, and if desired a little saffron
2. Add a little broth and bring to a boil
3. Add nutmeg zest and serve very hot
4. This sauce is of very good taste and is very suitable for arranging leftover poultry from other days
5. It is very digestive and gives much warmth to the stomach
6. It can be simplified by simply adding a pinch of hot pepper
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa a la Kari (Curry Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- pepper, chopped
- flour
- saffron
- broth
- nutmeg, grated

## Directions

1. In a saucepan put a good piece of butter, some pieces of chopped pepper, flour and if desired a little saffron
2. Add a little broth and make it boil
3. Add grated nutmeg and serve very hot
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa a la Mahonesa (Mayonnaise Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- egg yolk
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, put an egg yolk, salt, pepper and some drops of vinegar
2. Beat well to mix
3. Add oil drop by drop, always without stopping stirring
4. Incorporate vinegar, adding it little by little and without ceasing to stir the beaten mixture
5. This sauce needs a quarter hour of beating to be made
6. Serve with all kinds of cold meat and poultry
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa a la Maître d'Hôtel (Maître d'Hôtel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter, well beaten
- shallots or sorrel, crushed

## Directions

1. In a lightly heated serving dish put a good piece of well beaten butter
2. According to each person's taste, with crushed shallots or sorrel
3. When the mixture is completely made, place on top the fish or meat, which must have been kept constantly hot
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa a la Provenzal (Provençal Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two egg yolks
- one tablespoon lemon juice
- pepper, powdered
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. To two egg yolks add one tablespoon of lemon juice and powdered pepper
2. Season and put on very low heat, stirring constantly
3. Add a little oil
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa Amarilla (Yellow Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- fried bread
- cumin
- clove
- saffron threads
- broth
- bread crumb

## Directions

1. Grind in a mortar fried bread and cumin, a grain of clove and some saffron threads
2. Dissolve in broth, thicken with bread crumb and give it some boils
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa Común (Common Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- parsley, very finely chopped
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. In a plate put a piece of butter and very finely chopped parsley, salt and pepper
2. Mix and stir everything very well
3. Add a little vinegar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Alcaparra (Caper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- butter
- water
- capers
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Dissolve a tablespoon of flour in another of butter
2. Add water to make a regular porridge
3. Add the capers with a little vinegar
4. Serve with the dishes already mentioned
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Arvejas (Pea Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas, raw
- butter
- onion

## Directions

1. Shell the raw peas well and then pass them through a sieve
2. Fry onion in butter
3. Once the onion is fried, mix it with the peas and continue frying until done
4. If you want to make this sauce with meat or poultry, cook it in very little water and add the resulting broth to the sauce, being careful that it does not remain too light
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Callampas (Mushroom Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- butter
- bread crumbs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash them well
2. Fry them in butter
3. Once they have released most of their water
4. Add bread crumbs, salt and pepper
5. Serve with some meat dish or as previously mentioned
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Crema (Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- salt
- pepper
- one and a half glasses of cooked milk
- two or three egg yolks
- one tablespoon of milk

## Directions

1. Melt a little fresh butter and add one tablespoon of flour
2. Stir well and season with salt and pepper
3. Add one and a half glasses of milk cooked beforehand and let it boil
4. Remove from heat at once and slowly add while stirring two or three egg yolks dissolved in one tablespoon of milk
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Espuma para Budín (Foam Sauce for Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight egg yolks
- ten ounces of sugar
- one glass of sherry or white wine
- juice of two lemons
- zest of one lemon
- eight egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix well eight egg yolks with ten ounces of sugar
2. Add one glass of sherry or white wine, the juice of two lemons, and the zest of one
3. Place over heat and beat constantly until foamy
4. Fold in the eight well-beaten egg whites
5. Give it a light boil, beating constantly
6. Serve with the pudding
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Manteca Negra (Black Butter Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- parsley
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. In a pan or saucepan fry butter until it becomes almost black
2. Then fry a little parsley in it
3. After frying, add vinegar and salt
4. Let it cook near the fire for an instant
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Mayonesas (Mayonnaise Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two egg yolks
- juice of one lemon
- salt
- spices
- oil
- strong aromatic vinegar
- mustard (optional)

## Directions

1. Dissolve one or two egg yolks with the juice of one lemon
2. Add salt and spices mixed with these eggs
3. Add oil little by little while beating without ceasing
4. This will begin to thicken very soon
5. From time to time add a little strong aromatic vinegar
6. If desired add mustard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Mostaza (Mustard Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four glasses good broth
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon mustard

## Directions

1. Reduce three or four glasses of good broth with salt and pepper to half in a saucepan
2. Add one tablespoon of mustard
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Serve very hot
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Naranjas (Orange Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of some oranges
- sugar
- clove
- cinnamon
- colapiz

## Directions

1. Extract the juice from some oranges and season with sugar, clove and cinnamon
2. Put to boil
3. Add dissolved colapiz
4. Strain and let cool
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Naranjas (Orange Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges
- sugar
- cloves
- cinnamon
- isinglass

## Directions

1. Extract the juice from some oranges
2. Season with sugar, cloves, and cinnamon
3. Bring to a boil
4. Add dissolved isinglass
5. Strain and let cool
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Pepinillos en Vinagre (Gherkin in Vinegar Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- gherkins
- flour
- butter
- pepper
- broth

## Directions

1. Chop the gherkins a lot, putting them afterwards in a saucepan with a pinch of flour, butter, pepper and sufficient broth
2. After the first boil, remove from the fire
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Perejil (Parsley Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley shoots
- mint
- bread, soaked
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Take parsley shoots, mint and soaked bread
2. On the stone where everything will be ground, add vinegar and pepper
3. When it is well ground, add vinegar again, salt and oil, balancing the oil with the vinegar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Salmis (Salmis Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter kneaded with flour
- white wine
- broth
- parsley sprigs
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice
- toasted bread slices
- brandy
- truffle

## Directions

1. Melt, without letting it turn red, a piece of butter kneaded with flour
2. Add in equal quantity white wine and broth with a sprig of parsley, pepper and salt
3. Make it boil, then place the foods to be cooked with a little lemon juice and remove from the fire as it boils
4. Place the pieces of meat or pieces of poultry you want to prepare on toasted bread slices and pour the sauce over them
5. A small glass of brandy and a truffle cut into very fine pieces make an admirable dish
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Tomate para Guardar (Tomato Sauce for Preserving)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- salt
- pepper, ground

## Directions

1. Cut the tomatoes in half and salt them
2. Leave them from one day to the next
3. Squeeze and strain them through fine cloth, pressing until only the seeds remain in the cloth
4. Put this to cook until it boils and reaches the desired thickness
5. Before bottling, add plenty of ground pepper
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Trufas (Truffle Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles
- butter
- broth or stock
- more butter
- cornstarch or flour

## Directions

1. Make by chopping some truffles and sautéing them in butter
2. Add broth or stock
3. When the sauce has reduced, add more butter
4. Let it boil, skim it and let it thicken naturally, or by means of cornstarch or flour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa de Vino para Budines (Wine Sauce for Puddings)
body:
## Ingredients

- one glass of wine
- water
- sugar
- lemon peel
- pieces of whole cinnamon
- one tablespoon of chuño dissolved in water

## Directions

1. Boil one glass of wine with water, sugar, lemon peel, and pieces of whole cinnamon
2. Add one tablespoon of chuño dissolved in water
3. Let it boil and serve with the pudding
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa Habanera Cruda (Raw Havana Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow squash, cooked
- three roasted tomatoes without skin
- salt
- pepper
- half a small cup vinegar
- double the amount of broth
- small onion
- parsley, finely chopped
- sweet pepper

## Directions

1. Take yellow squash cooked in a pot, three roasted tomatoes without skin, sufficient salt and some grains of pepper
2. Pound everything in a mortar
3. Add half a small cup of vinegar with double the quantity of broth
4. Add a small onion, a little finely chopped parsley and a sweet pepper, all raw and cold
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa Mayonesa o Bayonesa (Mayonnaise or Bayonnaise Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one hard egg yolk
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- oil
- olives, anchovies, or smoked herring fillets (optional)

## Directions

1. Dissolve one hard egg yolk in a few drops of vinegar with pepper and salt
2. Finish dissolving by pouring oil drop by drop while always stirring
3. When the mixture is well made clarify it to the consistency of sauce by adding vinegar little by little and always stirring
4. Olives, anchovies or smoked herring fillets can be added
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa Muy Buena para Ave o Carne (Very Good Sauce for Poultry or Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- three slices of bread
- lard or butter
- three or four hard egg yolks
- broth
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take three slices of bread and fry them in lard or butter
2. After they are well browned mix them with three or four hard egg yolks
3. Dissolve with a little broth
4. Add pepper and parsley and place over the poultry or meat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa para Ave (Sauce for Poultry)
body:
## Ingredients

- four onions
- butter
- broth or water
- poultry, partridge or hen
- a little sugar water
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Cook four onions well and pass them through a sieve
2. Add a small piece of butter and a little broth or water sufficient so it becomes of regular thickness
3. Put on low heat with the poultry, partridge or hen with a little sugar water and the seasonings you wish
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa para Budín (Sauce for Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little milk
- a small piece of vanilla
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil a little milk with a small piece of vanilla
2. Beat two egg yolks and mix with the milk
3. Place on heat for a moment
4. Pour over any pudding
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa para Pastel de Ave o Carne (Sauce for Poultry or Meat Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- aromatics and celery
- water
- butter
- onion
- flour
- chicken broth

## Directions

1. Fry the chicken with aromatics and celery
2. Let it boil with a little water
3. Fry a little chopped onion in butter
4. Add one tablespoon of flour and let it fry
