5. Gradually add a little chicken broth to the sauce and let it boil until it thickens a little
6. Pour it into the pastry dish and when it is fried put the dough on top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa para Pescado o Carne (Sauce for Fish or Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- One tablespoon of onion, finely chopped and washed
- Four ounces of butter
- One tablespoon of chopped parsley
- One tablespoon of flour
- A little milk
- Salt
- One whole clove
- Fish, poultry or meat cooked beforehand with salt, pepper, celery and onion

## Directions

1. Fry the finely chopped and washed onion in four ounces of butter until it is softened
2. Add one tablespoon of chopped parsley and another of flour, fry a little
3. Add a little milk, salt and a whole clove
4. Leave to boil for a while with the fish, poultry or meat that has been previously cooked with salt, pepper, celery and onion
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa para Todo (All-Purpose Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- six hard eggs
- two tablespoons vinegar
- one tablespoon English mustard
- onion, very fine and well washed
- parsley, plenty
- celery, very finely chopped
- pickled vegetables
- salt
- pepper, peppery
- one quarter of a bottle of oil

## Directions

1. Take the yolks of the six hard eggs and dissolve them with two tablespoons of vinegar
2. Add one tablespoon of English mustard and a little very fine and well washed onion
3. Add plenty of finely chopped parsley and celery and some pickled vegetables
4. Season with salt and plenty of peppery pepper
5. Add one quarter of a bottle of oil
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Salsa Picante (Spicy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three leeks
- butter
- one quarter of a glass vinegar
- gherkins in vinegar
- capers in vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut two or three leeks very fine, which will be fried with a little butter
2. Add one quarter of a glass of vinegar, which will be allowed to reduce
3. Let everything cook on low heat for a quarter hour at the corner of the stove
4. Degrease it
5. When serving, add finely cut gherkins and capers in vinegar
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: San Cloud (Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 22 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- 1/2 pound almond paste
- zest of 1 lemon
- 1 1/2 pounds flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat 1 pound powdered sugar with 22 egg yolks and 4 egg whites
2. When beaten and white, add 1/2 pound almond paste, the zest of 1 lemon, and 1 1/2 pounds flour
3. When everything is well incorporated, place pieces of this dough the size of a peso on buttered baking sheets
4. Sprinkle chopped almonds on top
5. Place in a moderate oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sesos de Vaca en Caldereta (Veal Brains in Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brains
- bacon strips
- white wine
- broth
- carrots
- parsley
- onions
- thyme
- bay leaf
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Remove the coagulated blood, veins and tissue covering the brains
2. Place in warm water to clean them
3. Cover with two bacon strips and cook in two glasses of white wine and the same amount of broth with carrots, parsley, onions, thyme, bay leaf, pepper and salt
4. After cooking, place butter in another pot and sauté the liquid in which the brains were cooked, strained through a sieve
5. Add some mushrooms and let everything cook together
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sesos Fritos (Fried Brains)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked brains
- frying batter
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. If after cooking brains to eat à la marinière or with black butter some leftovers remain, divide them into pieces
2. Dip or soak them in a batter for frying
3. Fry this batter until it takes on a golden color and serve at the table with fried parsley
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sierra Fiambre (Cold Sierra Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- sierra fish
- salt
- celery branches
- parsley
- bay leaf
- peppercorns
- lemon
- oil
- vinegar
- ground pepper
- dark sauce

## Directions

1. Clean carefully and cook with salt, celery branches, parsley, a bay leaf and peppercorns
2. When it is seen to be cooked remove the skin, bones and all the dark meat
3. While still hot squeeze a lemon over it, add plenty of oil, vinegar, salt, ground pepper, a bay leaf and a little dark sauce
4. Let the fish rest about three hours, then serve with that sauce
5. It is also good with mayonnaise sauce, cold of course
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sierra Frita (Fried Sierra)
body:
## Ingredients

- sierra fish
- three tablespoons of oil
- onion
- tomato, finely chopped
- celery sprigs
- parsley
- oregano
- salt
- hard-boiled egg, chopped
- whole pepper

## Directions

1. Fry some onion in three tablespoons of oil, then add finely chopped tomato, celery sprigs, parsley and oregano
2. Place the clean fish on a serving platter
3. Put all of this underneath the fish, then add salt, chopped hard-boiled egg and whole pepper
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa a la Conde (Soup à la Conde)
body:
## Ingredients

- four carrots
- broth
- butter
- nutmeg
- bread

## Directions

1. Cook four carrots and when cooked mash with a spoon and pass through a sieve
2. Prepare the broth and half an hour before serving add the carrot purée, a piece of butter and a little nutmeg
3. Fry some toasted bread pieces the size and shape of a dime and place in the soup tureen when serving
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa a la Jardinera (Garden Vegetable Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots
- Turnips
- Lettuce
- Sorrel
- Chervil
- Lard or oil
- Broth
- Peas
- Asparagus heads
- Bread crusts

## Directions

1. Cut thin strips of carrots and turnips
2. Take lettuce, sorrel and chopped chervil, and sauté everything with lard or oil
3. Then add broth from the stew, throwing in a handful of peas and some asparagus heads
4. Once these vegetables are well cooked, skim and pour over bread crusts
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa a la Valenciana (Valencian Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one plate of rice
- four tablespoons oil
- saffron
- onion
- tomato
- pepper
- broth

## Directions

1. Wash the rice well and put in a serving dish with four tablespoons oil and a little saffron
2. Add onion in slices, tomato and pepper, all fried in oil
3. When the rice grain begins to open, fill the dish with broth and let boil until cooked
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa a la Valenciana (Valencian-Style Rice Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- One plate of rice
- Four tablespoons of oil
- A little saffron
- Onion in slices
- Tomato
- Pepper
- Broth
- Roasted peppers
- Green celery sprigs

## Directions

1. Wash the rice well and place it in a pan with four tablespoons of oil and a little saffron
2. Also add sliced onion, tomato and pepper, all fried in oil
3. When the rice grain begins to open, fill the pan with broth and let it boil until all the broth is consumed
4. The rice should remain so dry that it separates into grains
5. The soup can also be decorated with roasted peppers filled with rice from the soup and green celery sprigs
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa al Conde de París (Soup à la Comte de Paris)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces rice flour
- six cups broth
- four egg yolks
- one glass milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Add rice flour to a saucepan with six cups broth, adding gradually while taking care to dissolve the rice well in the broth
2. Place over fire stirring continuously until cooked; one hour of cooking is sufficient for serving
3. Add a mixture of four egg yolks dissolved in a glass of milk and a small piece of butter
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Arroz (Rice Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Broth

## Directions

1. Calculate one ounce per person if it is of good quality
2. After washing in warm water three or four times, rubbing it with your hands, put it to cook on low heat with cooked broth
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Arvejas (Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one hen
- salt
- celery
- leeks
- parsley
- lettuce leaves
- raw peas
- butter
- two tablespoons flour
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook the hen with salt, celery and leeks until tender
2. Clean parsley and lettuce leaves and grind together with raw peas on a stone
3. Put a piece of butter and two tablespoons flour in a saucepan
4. When melted and combined with the flour, add the ground vegetables and let heat for a while
5. Pour the hen broth over it and let boil
6. It should be somewhat thick
7. Strain into the soup tureen and can add small pieces of bread fried in butter and hen pieces
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Cabeza de Ternera, Llamada de Tortuga (Calf's Head Soup, Called Mock Turtle)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pieces of cooked calf's head with skin
- Thyme
- Shallot
- Laurel
- Mushroom
- Cured ham
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Butter
- Water

## Directions

1. Take pieces of cooked calf's head, keeping the skin and cutting it into small square pieces.
2. Add thyme, shallot, laurel, mushroom, cured ham, cloves, and pepper.
3. Mix everything with butter, making a roux with sufficient water for the soup, which should be fairly thick.
4. Serve.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Calabazas (Squash Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- squash
- flour
- onion
- tomato
- cheese
- water or broth
- salt
- three eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the squash into thin slices, put in the sun or oven for a while
2. Fry rolling them in flour together with onion slices and a little tomato
3. Arrange in a serving dish in layers of squash, layers of onion and grated cheese on top
4. Before putting on the fire add boiling water or broth with salt
5. Shortly before taking to the table, beat three eggs with broth and scatter over the entire dish
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Camarones (Shrimp Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimp shells (excluding head shells)
- butter
- flour
- broth
- bread crumbs
- egg yolks
- egg whites
- nutmeg
- salt
- shrimp tails

## Directions

1. Put shrimp shells (less those from the head) in the oven to toast well
2. Grind them to powder
3. Put a tablespoon of butter in a saucepan, when melted add the shell powder with a tablespoon of flour
4. Stir well over the fire and add about four tablespoons broth
5. Remove from fire and add plenty of broth
6. Add bread crumbs and bring to boil
7. Strain, and if too thick add more broth
8. At the time of serving add small balls made from bread crumbs, eggs, nutmeg and a small piece of butter cooked separately in broth
9. Beat a tablespoon of butter until foamy, add three yolks and two whites, a little nutmeg and salt, stir adding bread crumbs gradually until thick, wet hands in water and form balls, add to boiling broth, taking care not to cover the pot until serving
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Choros o Machas (Mussel or Razor Clam Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels or razor clams
- Water
- Sliced bread
- Ground garlic
- One onion head in segments
- Six ounces of rendered fat or butter
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Wash the mussels or razor clams well and place them to cook in a regular amount of water.
2. When it has boiled for a quarter hour, remove them from their shells.
3. In an earthenware dish prepared for this purpose, place sliced bread, ground garlic, onion head in segments, and six ounces of rendered fat or butter.
4. Add the mussels or razor clams and pour in the stock in which they were previously cooked.
5. Let it boil for another half hour if necessary.
6. Serve.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Fideos (Noodle Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Five cents worth of beef head
- Noodles
- Whole pepper
- Peppermint
- Onion

## Directions

1. Put five cents worth of beef head to cook and let it boil until the bones come loose
2. Strain this broth so it is clean and put it to boil again
3. Then add the noodle in the quantity desired and put it to cook again on low heat
4. Add whole pepper and peppermint, onion and let it rest
5. This soup can be made with any kind of noodle or macaroni
6. When you want to add bread to this soup, before serving it, toast some slices and add them, letting it rest a little so it takes on good flavor
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Gallina (Hen Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one hen
- onion slices
- two tablespoons flour
- white wine

## Directions

1. Cook the hen with onion slices
2. When the broth is good, remove the fat from the top
3. Dissolve two tablespoons of flour in this fat and add enough broth for the soup
4. Add the hen meat to the broth
5. At the time of sending to the table, add a glass of white wine
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Jigote (Jigote Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two onion heads
- Two loaves of bread
- Twenty cents worth of roasted and finely chopped meat
- Half a glass of wine
- Whole pepper
- Salt
- Broth

## Directions

1. Cut two onion heads into slices and fry them until they take on color
2. Cut two loaves of bread into slices and toast them
3. Arrange in a pan in layers of onion and bread, roasted and finely chopped meat, half a glass of wine, whole pepper, salt and a little broth
4. Place on the fire to boil well
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Lechugas (Lettuce Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine lettuce leaves
- Butter
- Broth
- Bread

## Directions

1. Cut fine lettuce leaves and fry them in butter
2. Pour broth over them and let it boil
3. In the tureen place small pieces of toasted bread fried in butter
4. Pour the broth over the bread
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Lentejas (Lentil Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lentils
- Broth
- Carrots
- Onions
- Two cloves
- Bread
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cook lentils in broth with carrots, onions and two cloves
2. When well cooked, strain them and pour into the tureen over small pieces of bread fried in butter
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Macarrones con Queso (Macaroni Soup with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- Broth
- Cheese

## Directions

1. Drop the macaroni into boiling broth, stir and skim continuously, letting them cook for a quarter of an hour, then remove them, letting them boil on low heat until well cooked
2. Before serving, add cheese shavings over them
3. The soup should be thick; cheese can also be served grated on a plate and this is preferable
4. Macaroni remains very tasty when cooked in stew broth
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Machas (Razor Clam Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- machas (razor clams)
- onion
- bread
- broth from the clams
- pepper
- parsley
- celery
- chili pepper

## Directions

1. Wash well and cook with the shells
2. When well cooked, remove from shells and wash again to remove sand
3. Fry a little onion in slices until colored, then fry a little bread (calculating that the soup not be too thick)
4. Add the broth in which the clams were cooked, well strained
5. Season with pepper, parsley, celery and a little chili pepper
6. Add the clams and let boil over low heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Ostras (Oyster Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons flour
- butter
- milk
- broth
- oyster broth
- oysters
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Fry about two tablespoons flour in butter
2. Add a little milk to make a paste
3. Dilute with broth and oyster broth, should be regular thickness
4. At the time of sending to the table, add the oysters (which should boil only an instant) and some toasted fried bread
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Primavera (Spring Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Small onions
- Small radishes
- Broth
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare as the macaroni soup, adding asparagus and small onions scalded beforehand, and small radishes
2. To remove the bitterness of these vegetables, add a little sugar to the broth
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Ravioles (Ravioli Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour
- Fresh eggs
- A little salt
- Mixed meats such as fish, poultry, veal
- Cheese
- Butter
- Broth

## Directions

1. Make a dough with flour, fresh eggs and a little salt, thin it until it is the thickness of paper
2. Have ready a filling made of whatever meats are at hand: fish, poultry, veal, etc.
3. Form small mounds at equal distances on half of the dough
4. Moisten and cover the filling with the other half, pressing with your fingers the space between each mound to isolate them and seal the dough
5. Cut this into regular-sized quarters and place them on the lid of the pot, then drop into broth to cook for five minutes
6. Serve these ravioli accompanied by a plate of grated cheese
7. Alternatively, place a layer of ravioli in the tureen, over these another layer of grated cheese and melted butter and continue alternating until the tureen is full, pouring over it the broth in which they were cooked
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Sémola (Semolina Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Broth or substance from the stew
- Semolina

## Directions

1. Cook the broth from the stew or substance and add the semolina
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Sémola (Semolina Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Stock from puchero or meat extract
- Semolina

## Directions

1. Cook the stock from puchero or meat extract and add the semolina.
2. Note that this pasta is more prone to clumping, so it must be stirred more continuously than other pastas.
3. Cook for half an hour until done.
4. For semolina in milk, prepare the same way but use milk instead of stock.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Tallerines (Noodle Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- tallerines (noodles)
- water
- salt
- stewed onion
- grated cheese
- broth from the stew
- wine

## Directions

1. Cook the noodles in water and salt
2. Arrange in a serving dish alternating layers of noodles and stewed onion, grated cheese and broth from the stew
3. For this soup it is necessary to make a stew with very good wine and substantial ingredients
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Tortuga (Turtle Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds turtle meat, well washed
- Three ounces of butter
- Two bay leaves
- Salt to taste
- Onions, chopped
- A small bundle of parsley, chopped
- Two cloves
- Ground pepper
- Toasted and crushed hazelnuts
- Four plates of stock
- One glass of white wine

## Directions

1. Fry the cleaned turtle meat in a pot with three ounces of butter.
2. Add two bay leaves, appropriate salt, and chopped onions.
3. When everything is fried, add a small bundle of chopped parsley, two cloves, ground pepper, toasted and crushed hazelnuts, four plates of stock and one glass of white wine.
4. Season and let simmer until the meat is cooked.
5. Serve.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Verdura (Vegetable Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Celery
- Parsley
- Small beans
- Onions
- Potatoes
- Poultry livers
- Good broth
- A little flour
- Cold water

## Directions

1. Chop very finely a little cabbage, celery, parsley, small beans, onions and potatoes
2. Also add chopped poultry livers
3. Put all of this to cook in good broth
4. Once well cooked, thicken it with a little flour dissolved in cold water
5. Stir and leave it a little while on the fire
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa de Zanahorias (Carrot Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots
- Broth
- A little flour
- Butter
- Fresh peas

## Directions

1. Cook carrots in broth and pass them through a strainer and add the broth from the soup
2. Fry a little flour in butter and add it to the broth to thicken it
3. Add fresh peas
4. When these are cooked, pour the soup into the tureen which should be prepared with small pieces of bread fried in butter
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa Imitación de Cola de Vaca (Imitation Oxtail Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- soup broth
- peppercorns
- potatoes
- carrots
- turnips
- butter or fat
- bread
- cooked meat

## Directions

1. In the soup broth add a handful of peppercorns and very thin slices of potatoes, carrots and turnips fried in butter or fat
2. Add plenty of bread in pieces and chopped cooked meat also fried in butter
3. Let everything boil until mealtime
4. Pass through a strainer, taking care it is not too thick
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa Juliana (Julienne Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two carrots
- one onion
- butter
- two cabbage leaves
- four to six chard leaves
- water
- a dozen lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Cut the carrots into thin slices
2. Cut the onion into very thin and small pieces and fry in a little butter
3. Chop two cabbage leaves and four to six chard leaves and cook them in boiling water
4. When cooked, wash and dry them in a napkin and add to the broth with the carrot and onion
5. When everything has boiled for three hours, add a dozen chopped lettuce leaves, let it boil a bit more and serve
6. If using chard, cook it separately and add at the time of serving
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopa Juliana (Julienne Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two carrots
- one onion
- butter
- two leaves of cabbage
- four to six leaves of chard

## Directions

1. Cut the carrots into thin slices
2. Cut the onion into very thin and small pieces and fry in a little butter
3. Chop two leaves of cabbage, four to six leaves of chard and put to cook in boiling water
4. When cooked, wash and dry in a napkin and put in the broth with the carrot and onion
5. When everything has boiled for three hours, add a dozen...
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sopladores (Puffers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds flour
- 4 ounces fat
- 4 eggs
- 2 ounces sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a mound with 2 pounds flour and leave a hollow in the middle
2. Place the 4 eggs without beating in the hollow
3. Add a little salt dissolved in boiling water and the hot fat
4. Stir with a knife so everything combines with the flour
5. The dough should be firm and is softened by kneading a lot
6. Cut the puffers
7. Place in moderate oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sorbete de Damascos (Apricot Sorbet)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots, peeled
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Pass the peeled apricots through a sieve.
2. Make a syrup that is thick until the thread stage.
3. Measure one glass of syrup and another of apricot juice.
4. Stir lightly, and when it has cooled, bottle it without putting it over the fire.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sorbete de Guindas (Sour Cherry Sorbet)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sour cherries
- 2 pounds sugar per 1 pound cherry juice

## Directions

1. Wash these in two waters and place them to drip in a basket, then remove the stems and crush them with the hand in a pot.
2. Let them rest four hours.
3. Then strain them through cloth; when strained, place in a cloth of three layers letting it drip until the next day.
4. For every pound of juice, add 2 pounds of sugar.
5. When the syrup reaches point, add the juice.
6. Let it boil until it is thick.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Sorbete de Limón (Lemon Sorbet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound lemon juice
- 1 pound 12 ounces ground sugar

## Directions

1. Place in a bain-marie until the sugar dissolves and the syrup becomes crystal clear.
2. Remove, let it cool and bottle it.
3. Be careful that it does not boil.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Stuffed Leg of Lamb (Pierna Rellena)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb leg
- fat or lard
- broth
- onion pieces
- herbs
- whole pepper
- cooked potatoes
- hard-boiled eggs
- onion, finely chopped
- hot water
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Fry the leg in a little fat
2. When golden, add broth, onion pieces, herbs and whole pepper
3. When cooked, cut a square in the middle
4. Fill with mashed cooked potatoes
5. Add chopped hard-boiled eggs, finely chopped onion that has been passed through hot water, oil and vinegar
6. Add the leftover filling to the broth the leg was cooked in to make sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Suspiros de Monja (Nun's Sighs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 of a cuartillo water
- sugar the size of 2 walnuts
- butter the size of 2 walnuts
- grated lemon zest
- flour
- eggs
- hot fat
- sugar or honey for serving
- orange blossom water (optional)

## Directions

1. Place in a pot 1/4 of a cuartillo water, sugar the size of 2 walnuts, butter the size of 2 walnuts, and grated lemon zest
2. Bring to a boil for a moment
3. Add handfuls of flour until the paste thickens well
4. Stir constantly until cooked, which is known when touching it with your finger it does not stick
5. Remove from heat and let cool
6. Break an egg and add it, stirring gently
7. When well incorporated, add another egg, and then another, until the dough is softer and the spoon releases easily when removed
8. Take with a spoon a piece of dough the size of a walnut and place it in hot fat
9. Strike a little with a stick so it rises
10. Serve the fritters with sugar or glazed with honey
11. If desired, add orange blossom water when adding the first egg
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tallos en Fuente (Stalks in a Platter)
body:
## Ingredients

- stalks
- butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook them without peeling
2. When they are soft, peel and cut them into small pieces
3. Place them in a platter with a piece of butter and bread crumbs on top
4. Put it in the oven to brown
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tallos en Fuente (Stalks in a Serving Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- stalks
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook the stalks without peeling them. When they are tender, peel them and cut into small pieces.
2. Place them in a serving dish with a piece of butter and breadcrumbs on top.
3. Put in the oven to brown them.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tejas (Tiles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound almonds
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 10 egg whites
- 1 heaping spoonful flour
- butter

## Directions

1. When the almond is peeled, dry it on a cloth until very dry
2. Reduce the almond to very fine paste
3. Add the 10 egg whites without beating and incorporate them with the paste
4. Add the flour last
5. Spread this paste very thin on buttered baking sheets
6. Place in the oven until golden
7. At this point remove the sheets to the mouth of the oven and there cut with a knife the squares of the size desired
8. Shape over a round stick
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tomates en Conserva (Canned Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a brine as strong as possible
2. Place raw tomatoes in it, taking care that they are not too ripe and that they are very healthy
3. Clean them well with a cloth and place them in the brine which should cover them well
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tomates Rellenos (Stuffed Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- parsley
- cucumbers
- hard-cooked eggs
- capers
- gherkins

## Directions

1. Remove the top point of the tomato to extract the seeds with a small spoon
2. Squeeze out the water with light pressure of the fingers
3. Fill with a paste made of chopped parsley, cucumbers, hard-cooked eggs, capers and gherkins, all chopped very finely
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tomates Rellenos (Stuffed Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- parsley
- cucumbers
- hard-boiled eggs
- capers
- pickled gherkins
- anchovies
- salt
- oil
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Remove the top point of the tomato to extract the seeds with a teaspoon and squeeze out the water with a light pressure of the fingers.
2. Fill with a paste made of finely chopped parsley, cucumbers, hard-boiled eggs, capers, and pickled gherkins, along with anchovies in small pieces, salt, and oil.
3. Once the tomato is filled, wrap it in breadcrumbs and put in the oven.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torcazas (Wood Pigeons)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wood pigeons
- Parsley
- Color
- Celery
- Bread
- Onion
- Seasonings
- Eggs
- Broth

## Directions

1. Cook the pigeons well
2. Remove the backbone from the top
3. Place in a serving dish with parsley, color, and celery
4. Put in the oven to brown
5. Make a sauce from soaked bread, fry some onion with seasonings, beat two eggs, mix with the soaked bread adding a little broth to make it clear
6. Pour this sauce over the pigeons and let cook briefly
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta Alemana (German Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound butter
- one pound flour
- one pound sugar
- half pound raisins
- thirteen eggs, whites well beaten and yolks half beaten
- half pound almonds in thin slices
- zest of one lemon
- a little cinnamon

## Directions

1. Butter a mold
2. Pour all ingredients, well mixed, into the mold
3. Bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Almendra (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs with shells
- flour equal in weight to the eggs
- butter, the freshest possible, equal in weight to the eggs
- powdered sugar equal in weight to the eggs
- three ounces almonds, peeled with boiling water
- lemon zest or orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Weigh three eggs with their shells and use equal weights of flour, butter, and powdered sugar
2. Grind the sugar with three ounces of blanched almonds
3. Unite this mixture with the flour, butter, lemon zest or orange blossom water, and the eggs
4. When all is well incorporated, butter a mold and pour in the mixture
5. Bake slowly between two fires
6. Serve cold or hot with ground sugar on top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Arroz (Rice Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound rice
- milk
- lemon zest
- a little vanilla
- sugar to taste
- a little salt
- four to six egg yolks
- egg whites
- orange blossom water (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the rice with hot water
2. Cook with milk, adding more milk as it thickens
3. Add lemon zest, vanilla, sugar to taste, and salt
4. When open and thick, remove from heat
5. Remove the lemon zest and vanilla
6. Add four to six egg yolks
7. Beat egg whites well and add them to the mixture, with orange blossom water if lemon zest or vanilla was not used
8. Mix everything very well
9. Butter a mold and dust with ground sugar
10. Pour in the rice
11. Bake three quarters of an hour in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Bizcocho (Almond Sponge Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 3/4 ounce bitter almonds
- 3/4 pound ground sugar
- 18 egg yolks
- 14 egg whites
- lemon rind
- nutmeg rind
- butter

## Directions

1. Grind very fine 1 pound sweet almonds and 3/4 ounce bitter almonds, which should first be washed and well dried
2. Mix with 3/4 pound ground sugar and 6 egg yolks, which are beaten with 12 more yolks for half an hour
3. Beat separately the whites of 14 eggs and very lightly fold in the mixture with lemon rind and a little nutmeg rind
4. Grease the mold with melted butter and bake in a gentle oven for one hour
5. Strips of fine paper greased with butter can be placed in the mold to remove the cake more easily
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Bizcochuelo (Sponge Cake Torte)
body:
## Ingredients

- sponge cake (bizcochuelo)
- lukewarm light syrup
- sweet preserve of choice
- meringue
- orange blossom water
- glazed candies, pasta figures, or caramelized pieces (for garnish)

## Directions

1. Arrange the sponge cake on a serving dish
2. Pour lukewarm light syrup over it
3. When soaked, arrange in layers, alternating one layer of sponge cake and one of preserve of your choice
4. Build the figure somewhat high and coat with meringue
5. Garnish with the same meringue or with glazed candies, pasta figures, or caramelized pieces
6. Can also be made with caramel or egg yolk
7. Orange blossom water suits the meringue very well
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Canela (Cinnamon Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- eighteen eggs
- half pound ground almonds
- one pound sugar
- one and a half ounces cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat eighteen eggs well
2. Add half pound ground almonds, one pound sugar, and one and a half ounces cinnamon
3. Mix everything well and place in an earthen pot between two fires
4. Dust with sugar and cinnamon
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Chocolate (Chocolate Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of sugar
- Half an ounce of cinnamon
- One quarter ounce of cloves
- Six ounces of chocolate
- Four eggs
- Fourteen egg yolks
- Fourteen egg whites
- Seven ounces of dried dark bread, ground
- Butter

## Directions

1. Grind the sugar, cinnamon, cloves, and chocolate together
2. Beat for one hour with four eggs and fourteen egg yolks
3. Add the fourteen egg whites, beaten separately
4. Add seven ounces of dried dark bread that has been ground
5. Grease the mold with butter
6. Add the batter when ready to place in the oven
7. Bake in a not-too-hot oven for one hour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Harina (Flour Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fresh butter
- 5 eggs
- 3/4 pound sugar
- lemon rind
- 1 pound flour

## Directions

1. Beat 1 pound fresh butter with 5 eggs and 3/4 pound sugar and the rind of a lemon
2. This should be beaten much
3. Add 1 pound flour
4. Grease the mold with butter and bake in a gentle oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Macarrones (Macaroni Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four ounces of butter
- Four ounces of sugar
- Six ounces of flour
- Three egg yolks
- Lemon zest
- Three egg whites
- Eight ounces of powdered sugar for topping
- Eight ounces of sweet almonds
- Two ounces of bitter almonds

## Directions

1. Form a dough with butter, sugar, flour, egg yolks, and lemon zest after mixing and kneading well
2. Roll out and form into a round cake the size of a plate
3. Place on a buttered baking sheet
4. Prepare topping by beating three egg whites and mixing with powdered sugar and almonds reduced to paste
5. Cover the cake with this topping
6. Bake in a gentle oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Papas (Potato Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen eggs (seven whole and seven yolks)
- half pound sugar
- twelve ounces grated potatoes
- lemon zest
- lemon juice
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes the day before without skin and drain thoroughly
2. The following day, grate the potatoes and weigh them
3. Beat the seven whole eggs and seven yolks with sugar for one hour
4. Add lemon zest and lemon juice to the beaten mixture
5. Butter a mold and dust with toasted and ground bread
6. Pour the mixture into the mold
7. Bake in a gentle oven, taking care the heat is not too strong
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Papas (Potato Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 eggs (7 whole and 7 yolks)
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 12 ounces grated potatoes
- lemon rind
- lemon juice
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat 14 eggs (7 whole and 7 yolks) with 1/2 pound sugar and 12 ounces grated potatoes for one hour
2. Potatoes should be cooked the day before without skin and left without water
3. The next day pass through a grater and weigh
4. Add lemon rind and lemon juice
5. When about to pour into the mold to bake it, taking care that it is not too strong, grease the mold with butter and sprinkle with flour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Papas al Vapor (Steamed Potato Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound potatoes
- nine ounces almonds
- nine eggs
- lemon zest
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat sugar with egg yolks
2. Add almonds and potatoes
3. Finally add beaten egg whites
4. Bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Papas Costarricense (Costa Rican Potato Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty egg yolks
- one pound sugar
- one pound cooked and grated potatoes
- half pound sweet almonds and a few bitter almonds
- zest and juice of one lemon
- twenty egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat twenty egg yolks with one pound sugar
2. Add one pound of cooked and grated potatoes, half pound sweet almonds and a few bitter almonds, and lemon zest and juice
3. Finally add twenty beaten egg whites
4. Butter the mold and dust with grated bread
5. Bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta de Sémola (Semolina Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- four glasses milk
- semolina
- orange blossom water
- lemon zest or vanilla
- sugar to taste
- four egg yolks
- four egg whites, well beaten
- butter
- finely grated bread

## Directions

1. Bring four glasses of milk to boil and gradually add semolina until thick
2. Let cook a little and then season with orange blossom water, lemon zest or vanilla, and sugar to taste
3. Add four egg yolks and four well-beaten egg whites
4. Butter a mold or pot and dust with finely grated bread
5. Pour everything in and finish cooking by surrounding the mold with hot ashes and moderate heat on top of the lid
6. Serve hot and well browned
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta Magdalena (Magdalena Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces fresh butter
- four ounces flour
- five ounces sugar
- zest of half a lemon
- one tablespoon orange blossom water
- three egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Place two ounces of fresh butter on the heat in a plate
2. When melted, add four ounces flour, five ounces sugar, and lemon zest
3. Add one tablespoon of orange blossom water and three egg yolks
4. Beat the egg whites perfectly and mix everything with a spoon
5. Bake in a mold in a camp oven with moderate heat for one hour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta Moris (Moris Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces well-washed butter (to remove salt)
- half pound powdered sugar
- six ounces flour
- ten egg whites, well beaten
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Mix the well-washed butter with powdered sugar
2. Add flour, beaten egg whites, and lemon zest
3. Pour everything together into a buttered mold
4. Bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta para el Té (Tea Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- powdered sugar equal in weight to four eggs
- flour equal in weight to four eggs
- butter equal in weight to four eggs
- peeled and sliced almonds
- Corinth raisins
- cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Place on the scale plate of a balance four eggs and equal weights of powdered sugar, flour, and butter
2. Beat the butter and add four egg yolks
3. Beat again for a time and finally add the sugar, flour, and four beaten egg whites separated as for meringues
4. Place this dough in a buttered mold and cover on top with peeled and sliced almonds, Corinth raisins, and cinnamon powder
5. Bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta Remojada (Soaked Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve eggs
- Half pound of refined powdered sugar
- Half pound of flour
- Three pounds of sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Beat twelve eggs with half pound of powdered sugar until very white, using a wooden spoon and copper spatula
2. When well combined, add half pound of flour and mix until fully incorporated
3. Place in a greased earthenware dish with a sheet of paper underneath
4. Bake in a moderate oven
5. Once cooked, remove from paper and scrape any stuck portions with a knife
6. Return to the same dish
7. Make a syrup with three pounds of sugar
8. Prick the cake with a thin stick and gradually pour hot syrup over it so it penetrates throughout
9. When cold, dust with sugar and cinnamon
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta Seca (Dry Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two and a half pounds flour
- one bottle milk
- half pound butter
- five eggs
- three tablespoons yeast
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Make a dough with two and a half pounds flour, one bottle milk, half pound butter, five eggs, three tablespoons yeast, sugar, cinnamon, and lemon zest
2. Pour into a mold
3. Let rise until it lifts
4. Then bake in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Torta Valdiviana (Valdivian Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty egg yolks
- one pound sugar
- ten egg whites
- twenty-two ounces chuño (freeze-dried potatoes)

## Directions

1. Stir twenty egg yolks in the same direction for one hour with one pound of sugar
2. Beat ten egg whites
3. Unite to the beaten mixture with twenty-two ounces of chuño, alternating spoonfuls of white with spoonfuls of chuño
4. When all is united, stir only as much as necessary and pour into a buttered mold
5. Bake in the oven and when removing, place sugar on top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla a la Francesa (French Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Milk
- Sugar
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Take as many eggs as tablespoons of milk and a little sugar and salt
2. Beat for a moment
3. Put this in a frying pan where there will be one tablespoon of butter
4. Open it with a knife so that the raw part on top passes to the bottom
5. Empty it folded onto a plate, sprinkle generously with sugar and brown it with a very hot iron
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla con Confituras (Omelette with Preserves)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Milk
- Sugar
- Salt
- Butter
- Lemon peel
- Preserves

## Directions

1. Make it like the sugar omelette, only add sufficient quantity of preserves before beating the eggs and finish the same way
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla con Ron (Rum Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Milk
- Sugar
- Salt
- Butter
- Lemon peel
- Rum

## Directions

1. Prepare the omelette as previously explained
2. Sprinkle it with rum, set it on fire and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla con Salsa (Omelette with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chard
- Leeks
- Carrots
- Parsnips
- Salt
- Eggs
- Butter
- Milk
- Flour
- Butter
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook chard, leeks, carrots and parsnips, season with salt
2. Make a beaten mixture of six to seven eggs, fry in butter
3. It should remain thick
4. When golden on one side, empty it onto a platter, then return it to the pan to fry on the other side so it becomes dry
5. Serve with a small milk sauce that is thickened with one tablespoon of flour fried in butter, season with pepper
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla de Azúcar (Sugar Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Lemon peel
- Sugar
- Rasped sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs as in the previous ones, mix with lemon peel, sugar and put in the pan
2. Serve immediately on a plate sprinkled with rasped sugar
3. Cover the omelette also with sugar and brown it with a very hot iron
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla de Erizos (Sea Urchin Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Finely chopped onion
- Parsley
- Ground pepper
- Salt
- Sea urchin tongues
- Fat

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs and add a little finely chopped onion and parsley, ground pepper and salt
2. Remove the tongues from the sea urchins and add to the beaten mixture
3. Beforehand have the fat very hot
4. Pour all this beaten mixture into the pan and be careful to flip it so it fries well
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla de Espárragos (Asparagus Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Fat
- Eggs
- Raw flour
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the tender part of the asparagus into small pieces and fry in plenty of fat, being careful to stir it constantly until the asparagus is half cooked
2. Then beat four eggs and add one tablespoon of raw flour, salt and pepper
3. Remove the asparagus from the fat and add to the beaten mixture
4. Keep the pan with very hot fat and fry the omelette
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla de Jamón Habanera (Havana Ham Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Oil
- Three tomatoes
- Sweet pepper
- Onion
- Eggs
- Oil
- Finely chopped ham
- Meat
- Spices

## Directions

1. Fry the ham with oil
2. Then fry three tomatoes, one sweet pepper and an onion in oil
3. Once fried, add the eggs beaten with the very finely chopped ham
4. Flip it as stated in the previous recipe and let it brown well
5. If made with meat, it must be previously cooked and with fine spices
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla de Manzana (Apple Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Three or four apples
- Eggs
- Milk
- Sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Fry three or four finely chopped apples in butter
2. When they are golden, pour over them a beaten egg mixture prepared like the previous one
3. Fry the same way, but do not brown it
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla de Verdura (Vegetable Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- Vegetables
- Eggs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cook the vegetables you desire and mix them with the beaten mixture of five or six eggs
2. Fry in butter like the others
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla Liguiense (Ligurian Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fourteen egg yolks and whites
- One pound of butter, beaten
- One pound of flour
- Twelve hard-boiled egg yolks, finely chopped
- Four ounces of sweet almonds
- Two ounces of bitter almonds, ground
- One pound of sugar
- Zest of two lemons
- Jam or cream for filling

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Spread on baking sheets in a round form
3. Make the dough the thickness of one finger
4. Bake in the oven
5. When cooked, layer the cakes in a dish, spreading jam or cream between each layer
6. Let cool in the air
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tortilla Soplada (Puffed Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Sugar
- Lemon peel
- Butter
- Beef fat

## Directions

1. Break eggs separating the whites and yolks
2. Dust the yolks with powdered sugar, adding finely chopped lemon peel
3. Mix everything, then beat the whites and unite them to the yolks and mix as well as possible
4. Put sufficient butter in a pan and place over low heat
5. Once the butter is melted, add the eggs, stir the omelette so the liquid stays on top
6. When you see the butter is absorbed, give it good form and pour it onto a platter spread with beef fat, which is placed over hot ashes
7. Sprinkle powdered sugar on top of the omelette and put in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Trucha (Trout)
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- water
- half a glass of vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- laurel
- onion
- carrot in slices
- butter
- mushrooms
- parsley
- small onions, chopped
- half a cooked bread in fish

## Directions

1. Cook the trout in water with half a glass of vinegar, salt, pepper, laurel, onion and carrot slices
2. Prepare the sauce: in a pan, put a piece of butter with mushrooms, parsley and small chopped onions
3. When it has cooked a little, add half a bread cooked in fish, stir everything well so it breaks down
4. Add a little of the trout cooking broth, pass through a strainer
5. Serve the sauce with the trout
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Trucha Rellena (Stuffed Trout)
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- salt
- melted butter
- onion, chopped
- bread crumbs
- vegetables of choice
- laurel leaves
- capers sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the trout well, cut it into pieces, add salt and after a while dry it with a cloth
2. Make a filling with melted butter, chopped onion, bread crumbs and the vegetables you wish
3. In a platter well-coated with butter, place the stuffed trout pieces with the filling, onion slices and laurel leaves
4. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and put in the oven
5. Serve with capers sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Truchas a la Casera (Homestyle Trout)
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- salt
- white wine
- butter
- flour
- onion
- parsley
- mint
- cloves
- basil
- thyme

## Directions

1. Cover the trout with salt one hour before cooking
2. Cook at high heat with white wine, butter and flour, onion, parsley, mint, cloves, basil and thyme
3. Once cooked, set aside and serve in the same sauce, removing the onion and thyme
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Truchas Cocidas (Cooked Trout)
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- water
- salt
- parsley sprigs
- lemon juice
- pepper
- oil
- spices

## Directions

1. Cook the trout, after scaling it, in salted water with some parsley sprigs
2. When cooked, drizzle a little lemon juice over it and dust with pepper
3. Alternatively, cook the trout in water and salt, oil and all spices; when half-cooked, remove that broth and add new broth to finish cooking
4. Make a sauce of egg yolks and lemon juice and serve
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Truchas con Huevos (Trout with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- pepper
- lemon sauce
- eggs
- onions
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the trout as described in previous recipes
2. Dust with a little pepper
3. Serve with lemon sauce in a sauce boat and garnish the plate with eggs sliced into rounds, all over a bed of finely chopped onions and parsley
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Truchas con Limón (Trout with Lemon)
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- lemon juice
- pepper
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Cook the cleaned trout
2. Sprinkle with lemon juice and a little pepper
3. Serve with lemon slices
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Turrajas de Ave (Poultry Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken meat
- Breadcrumbs
- Cream
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Remove all membranes and tendons from chicken meat
2. Chop it with breadcrumbs moistened in cream and egg yolks
3. When well incorporated, season
4. Form small meatballs and roll in breadcrumbs
5. Coat again with egg and fry
6. Serve with parsley prepared the same way
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tórtolas con Escarola (Doves with Endive)
body:
## Ingredients

- Doves
- Butter
- Endive
- Potatoes
- Carrots
- Bread
- Bacon

## Directions

1. Fry endive in butter with sliced potatoes, carrots, and bread
2. Arrange this in a serving dish
3. On top arrange the parboiled doves with very thin slices of bacon on the breast
4. Place in the oven to brown
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tórtolas con Manzanas (Doves with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- Doves
- Color
- Onion
- Apples

## Directions

1. Place a tablespoon of color and chopped onion in a pot
2. Add raw, well-washed doves
3. Chop a dozen apples on top
4. Cover the pot and place on low heat from early on, stirring occasionally so they do not stick
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tórtolas con Salsa (Doves with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Doves
- Bread
- Butter
- Broth from doves

## Directions

1. Parboil the doves
2. Fry breadcrumbs in butter and add broth from the doves to make a regular sauce
3. Cut the doves in half and cook in the sauce
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tórtolas con Salsa de Ají (Doves with Chili Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Doves
- Sweet chilies
- Tomatoes
- Walnuts
- Bread
- Wine
- Broth
- Seasonings

## Directions

1. Take some sweet chilies, toast half and soak the other half
2. Grind the soaked chilies with two roasted tomatoes, walnuts, and breadcrumbs soaked in wine
3. Place this mixture in a pot with the doves, broth, and seasonings
4. Let simmer well
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tórtolas en Papel (Doves in Paper)
body:
## Ingredients

- Doves
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Let the doves cool after being well cooked
2. Place a piece of butter and plenty of breadcrumbs on each
3. Wrap in paper
4. Place in the oven until they brown
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Tórtolas Estofadas (Stewed Doves)
body:
## Ingredients

- Doves
- Bacon

## Directions

1. Prepare as for bird en papillote, but keep them whole and add small pieces of bacon
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Vaca a la Moda (Beef in Fashion)
body:
## Ingredients

- good piece of beef
- strips of bacon
- parsley
- minced onion
- salt
- pepper
- fine spices
- white wine
- bacon pieces
- small whole onions
- carrot slices
- whole pepper

## Directions

1. Take a good piece of beef, remove the bones
2. Lard it with strips of bacon, parsley and minced onion, salt, pepper and fine spices
3. Put white wine in the saucepan, bacon pieces, small whole onions, carrot slices, whole pepper and a little salt
4. Place the meat on these ingredients
5. Cover the pot well and put on low heat
6. Leave it for five or six hours and serve with all its seasoning
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Vaca a la Papillote (Beef en Papillote)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef slices
- lard
- buttered paper

## Directions

1. Between two wide and thick slices of cooked beef, spread lard mixed with seasonings
2. Wrap the two surfaces of the meat with a sheet of buttered or oiled paper and place on the grill over low heat
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Vaca a la Sartén (Beef in the Pan)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- beef
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut an onion in half, separating the head and tail, place in the pan without letting it turn black
2. After cutting each half into thin strips, add the piece of meat, separating the bones, skin and sinews
3. Heat gently, tossing the slices
4. When serving hot, add a few drops of vinegar
5. For more economy, the fat of the meat can be used in place of lard
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Vaca al Gratín (Beef Gratin)
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon or broth or lard with water
- mushrooms
- parsley
- small onions or minced garlic
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- broth
- white wine or sherry or rum

## Directions

1. In a dish that can be placed on the fire, put pieces of bacon, or lacking that, broth or lard with a little water
2. Add mushrooms, parsley, small onions or minced garlic
3. Sprinkle with grated breadcrumbs, salt, pepper and spices
4. Place in that base the slices of cooked beef, covering them the same on top as below
5. Sprinkle with a little broth and heat over low heat
6. A little before serving, place coals above and below to gratin it
7. A little white wine or a small glass of sherry or rum can be added
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Vaca Cocida (Boiled Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef hindquarters
- bones
- bird and game bones
- bird and game necks
- salt
- roots

## Directions

1. Preferably choose the hindquarters or part of it, remove the bones, roll and tie with string
2. Place in a pot with the bones, pasta and necks of birds and game used for entrees
3. Skim well over high heat
4. Once it has cooked a little, add salt and all sorts of roots
5. After removing the beef, serve it surrounded with parsley sprigs, or chopped in sauce garnished with onions and vegetables, or with small pastries
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Vaca Frita Criolla (Creole Fried Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- oil
- salt
- white rice
- plantains

## Directions

1. Take sufficient oil for two pounds of beef and heat in the pan
2. Cut the beef into finger-thick strips and crush slightly with the mortar pestle
3. Add a little salt and fry
4. Serve with white rice and plantains cut into small rounds
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Verdura con Jamón (Vegetables with Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- Swiss chard
- carrots
- ham fat
- ham meat

## Directions

1. Cook and chop the Swiss chard and carrots well.
2. Fry them in the ham fat together with pieces of ham meat.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Verdura con Salsa de Leche (Vegetables with Milk Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- milk

## Directions

1. Fry a tablespoon of flour in butter and then add the milk and make a not-too-thick mixture.
2. This sauce goes well with stalks and the thick part of the Swiss chard, cauliflower, and green beans. All these vegetables must be cooked beforehand before using with this sauce; the green beans should be cut before cooking them.
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Verdura en Escabeche (Pickled Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- asparagus
- peppers
- vinegar
- salt
- whole pepper

## Directions

1. Peel medium-sized onions and prick them all over with a fork or brush
2. Take asparagus, cut off the stems and prick them together with peppers cut in half to remove the seeds
3. Wash everything in vinegar and arrange in jars in layers with salt and whole pepper
4. Add vinegar, and the next day refill and cover well with bladder
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Vol-au-Vent Alemán (German Vol-au-Vent)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter
- Raw meat
- Bread
- Nutmeg
- Eggs
- Salt
- Milk
- Sausages
- Brains
- Broth
- Capers

## Directions

1. Cook the chicken, cut into pieces, roll in breadcrumbs and fry in butter
2. Mince some raw meat, grate bread and a little nutmeg, add two eggs, salt, and a little milk
3. Make a dough from this mixture, form small balls, roll in breadcrumbs and fry in butter
4. Also fry some sausages and brains
5. Arrange everything in a serving dish
6. If desired, place fried meat slices underneath
7. Make a caper sauce with the chicken broth and pour over the top
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Vol-au-vent Alemán (German Vol-au-vent)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hen
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- raw meat
- bread
- nutmeg
- 2 eggs
- salt
- milk
- sausages
- brains
- fried meat slices
- capers
- hen broth
- puff pastry

## Directions

1. Cook the hen, cut it into pieces, roll in breadcrumbs and fry in butter
2. Chop some raw meat, grate bread and a little nutmeg and add 2 eggs, salt and a little milk
3. Make a paste from this mixture, form small balls, roll in breadcrumbs and fry in butter
4. Also fry some sausages and brains
5. Arrange everything on a platter; if desired, place some fried meat slices at the bottom
6. Make a caper sauce with the hen broth and pour it over
7. Cover the pastry with puff pastry
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Vol-au-Vent de Ave o Cordero Tierno (Vol-au-Vent of Poultry or Tender Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- Poultry or lamb
- Butter
- Onion
- White wine
- Salt
- Broth
- Flour
- Mushrooms
- Chestnuts
- Sausages
- Brains
- Meat
- Pepper
- Asparagus

## Directions

1. Cut up the bird or lamb and place in a pot with butter, very finely chopped onion, a glass of white wine, and a little salt
2. Fry for five minutes and add the necessary broth to cook
3. When well cooked, arrange the pieces in a serving dish
4. Strain the broth and thicken with a little flour fried in butter
5. In the same dish arrange raw washed mushrooms, cooked chestnuts, fried sausages, pieces of cooked brains, and small balls of meat and pepper
6. If in season, add asparagus tips, cover with puff pastry, and place in the oven
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: ¿Te Gusta? (Do You Like It?)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound peeled and ground almonds
- powdered sugar (some amount)
- 1/2 ounce ground cinnamon
- 1/2 ounce ground cloves
- zest of 1 lemon
- 2 candied lemons
- 3 ounces toasted bread, ground very fine
- 12 eggs
- butter
- ground biscuit

## Directions

1. Grind 1/2 pound peeled almonds with some powdered sugar
2. Add 1/2 ounce ground cinnamon, 1/2 ounce ground cloves, zest of 1 lemon, 2 candied lemons, and 3 ounces toasted bread ground very fine
3. Add 12 eggs
4. Beat everything together, always in one direction
5. Coat the mold with butter and ground biscuit
6. Bake in the oven for 3/4 hour
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno

title: Ó-ras (Lamb Rib Preparation)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb ribs
- flour
- milk
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the lamb ribs and arrange leaving the meat at the point
2. Make a batter of flour mixed with milk and 3 eggs
3. Roll the ribs in it and fry
source_title: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno
